docs/plans/eden-phase-7b-integrator.md:51:- `repo` is the experiment's git repository (bare or non-bare). The integrator writes `trial/*` refs here.
docs/plans/eden-phase-7b-integrator.md:127:- **Does not populate optional manifest fields** `evaluator` or `artifacts` (§4.3, §4.4). The v0 role contracts in `03-roles.md` §4.4 do not surface evaluator identity or per-file artifact inventory in the evaluator's submission shape; when role-bindings add that metadata (Phase 10's LLM evaluator is the likely source) the integrator grows two optional `Callable` hooks. Keeping the shape minimal now avoids inventing a submission field ahead of spec demand.
spec/v0/03-roles.md:5:A role contract does **not** specify how the role is hosted. A conforming planner MAY run as a local subprocess, a long-lived service reached over HTTP, an in-process adapter, or a human at a keyboard. The protocol constrains the *observable effects* a role has on the task store, the event log, and the artifact store; how a role is invoked is a deployment concern and is deferred to a later-phase *role-binding* specification. Within v0, implementations MAY document their own binding by defining additional top-level fields on the experiment config per [`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §2.4.
spec/v0/03-roles.md:41:- Read access to the set of proposals and trials already persisted for `E`. The mechanism (direct store read, orchestrator-supplied snapshot, event-log replay) is a role-binding concern.
spec/v0/03-roles.md:43:A planner MAY additionally use any context the experiment config surfaces to it via role-binding-specific fields. The protocol does not require the planner to read beyond what is listed above.
spec/v0/03-roles.md:82:- Read and write access to the git repository associated with `P.experiment_id`, at a revision reachable from `P.parent_commits`. How the repository is located and how access is granted is a role-binding concern (§6, [`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §2.4); the protocol constrains only that the implementer's writes land under `work/*` and that no commit is introduced whose history does not descend from `P.parent_commits` (§3.3).
spec/v0/03-roles.md:89:1. Persist a trial with `status == "starting"`, `experiment_id == P.experiment_id`, `proposal_id == P`, and `parent_commits == P.parent_commits`, before making any repository write observable to other roles.
spec/v0/03-roles.md:123:- Read access to the git repository at `T.commit_sha` on the worker branch (`T.branch`). Repository location and access are a role-binding concern, as for the implementer (§3.1).
spec/v0/03-roles.md:137:The evaluator reads the repository at the trial's worker-branch tip but MUST NOT push, rewrite, or delete that branch, and MUST NOT write to the canonical trial lineage. Any side effects of evaluation (test caches, build outputs, captured logs) MUST NOT be observable through any protocol-owned store, ref, or artifact location except via the evaluator's declared outputs (§4.2).
spec/v0/03-roles.md:184:## 6. Role binding (deferred)
spec/v0/03-roles.md:193:Role-binding information that an implementation needs to invoke its roles MAY be carried as additional top-level fields on the experiment config ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §2.4). A future v0 addition or a v1 chapter will specify a standardized role-binding representation; until then, the protocol constrains only what each role observes and produces, not how it is reached.

docs/archive/microservices-refactor-plan.md:39:- **Postgres** (one instance for MVP) — pluggable behind a repository interface. Hosts: control plane tables, tasks queue, events log, planner proposals, experiment results, worker context stores.
docs/archive/microservices-refactor-plan.md:85:- `implementer.workers[]` — same shape. LLM/script workers run `implement_command` in a sandbox container; human workers use the Web UI.
docs/archive/microservices-refactor-plan.md:86:- `evaluator.workers[]` — same shape. LLM/script workers run `evaluate_command`; human workers use the Web UI.
docs/archive/microservices-refactor-plan.md:106:- `proposals` (shared across experiments): `id`, `experiment_id`, `slug`, `parent_commits` (array, length 1 in MVP), `plan_md_uri`, `artifacts_uri`, `priority`, `routing_labels`, `author_worker_id`, `author_kind`, `created_at`, `validated_at`, `trial_id` (nullable, set when dispatched), `status`, `failure_reason` (nullable). **Proposal status lifecycle (supersedes today's `drafting → ready → dispatched → completed`):**
docs/archive/microservices-refactor-plan.md:115:- `worker_context` (optional per-worker persistent notes): `worker_id`, `experiment_id`, `kind`, `content_blob_uri`, `updated_at`. Workers may ignore this entirely; it's there for implementations that want durable context across restarts.
docs/archive/microservices-refactor-plan.md:117:All access goes through a **repository interface** so SQLite can be dropped in for local/dev later.
docs/archive/microservices-refactor-plan.md:141:**Planner worker.** Output: a row in `proposals` (with `experiment_id`, `author_worker_id`, `author_kind`, parent commit ref, artifact URIs) plus a `plan.md`-style markdown document + any supporting artifacts uploaded to blob storage. Proposal *metadata* (parent commit, slug, priority, routing labels) is structured data with many auto-generatable defaults — **UI form is the primary authoring surface** for humans. Proposal *content* (the markdown plan) can be authored in a text area in the UI or, optionally, uploaded as a file (upload post-MVP). No git interaction.
docs/archive/microservices-refactor-plan.md:152:- **Implementer LLM hosts** (context-per-task): the host spawns a **per-task sandbox** to execute the actual work — in Compose, a `docker run` with `implement_command` as entrypoint; in k8s (Milestone 3), a Job on a GPU-labeled node. The sandbox clones the bare repo, checks out the `work/*` branch at the parent commit, runs `implement_command`, commits, pushes, exits. The host waits for sandbox completion, reads exit status, and submits the final SHA. Sandbox-per-task matches today's fresh-subprocess model, keeps blast radius contained, and maps cleanly to k8s Jobs.

docs/plans/eden-phase-8b-service-extraction.md:301:6. **Integration — real-subprocess E2E** (`reference/services/orchestrator/tests/test_e2e.py`). Uses `subprocess.Popen` with `python -m eden_task_store_server`, `python -m eden_planner_host`, etc. The harness creates a tempdir and `git init --bare` inside, then calls `seed_bare_repo(path)` (the `_common` helper) to write an empty initial commit on `refs/heads/main` and capture its `base_sha` for threading into the planner's `--base-commit-sha`. It creates a separate tempdir for the sqlite DB (`--db-path` pointing at a file, not `:memory:` — this exercises the SQLite path end-to-end). It boots task-store-server on `--port 0`, reads `EDEN_TASK_STORE_LISTENING` from stdout to learn the port, then boots 3 workers + orchestrator against `http://127.0.0.1:<port>`; the implementer and orchestrator both point `--repo-path` at the bare repo, the planner and evaluator don't touch it, and all 5 subprocesses share `--shared-token test-token` to exercise auth. It waits up to 60 seconds for the orchestrator to exit 0. On timeout or non-zero exit, it dumps every subprocess's stderr into the pytest failure output (this is non-negotiable — the one thing that makes subprocess tests diagnosable is capturing every process's stderr on failure). It SIGTERMs the workers and task-store-server, awaits each for up to 10 seconds, then SIGKILLs. Finally it opens the DB with a fresh `SqliteStore` and asserts: 3 accepted plan tasks, 3 accepted implement tasks, 3 accepted evaluate tasks; 3 trials with `status="success"` and `trial_commit_sha` set; each `trial.parent_commits == [base_sha]` (lineage threaded correctly from `--base-commit-sha`); and each `trial_commit_sha` is a valid git commit in the bare repo with `repo.commit_parents(trial_commit_sha) == list(trial.parent_commits)` (matches the in-process real-integrator test's parent check at `reference/packages/eden-dispatch/tests/test_end_to_end.py`). It tears down tempdirs on every exit path. The test is decorated `@pytest.mark.e2e` so it can be selected / skipped independently; it runs by default in the test suite, since without it Phase 8b has no integration coverage of the whole stack. Marked platform-skipped on Windows (signal handling is different; not worth supporting in v0).
spec/v0/design-notes/integrator-atomicity.md:33:On success, all three artifacts exist when the promotion returns. On failure, the ref exists briefly and is then deleted; by the time the promotion returns, none of the three exist. A reader observing the repository after the promotion returns sees a consistent state. A reader that happens to walk refs during the ~microseconds or milliseconds the promotion is running MAY observe a ref that is about to be compensated away.
spec/v0/design-notes/integrator-atomicity.md:100:An operator running ad-hoc git commands against the repository during active experiments MAY transiently observe a `trial/*` ref that disappears a moment later. This can only happen on rollback and is rare in practice — rollbacks mean a store-side failure (typically a disk-full or IO-error condition). The final state is always consistent with the three-artifact invariant.

spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md:52:`POST /v0/experiments/{E}/tasks/{T}/claim` accepts a JSON body with at minimum the field `worker_id` and an OPTIONAL `expires_at` timestamp. On success the server returns 200 with a `claim` object matching the shape from [`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §3.4 (see also [`schemas/task.schema.json`](schemas/task.schema.json)) — notably including the fresh `token`. On a non-`pending` task the server returns 409 `eden://error/illegal-transition`. The claim-token secrecy rule ([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §3.2) implies that intermediary proxies and logs MUST NOT persist claim tokens in plaintext; that is a deployment concern and the binding does not enforce it.

docs/plans/review/eden-tests-dir-convention/20260424T210747/0.jsonl:6:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_1","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' AGENTS.md\"","aggregated_output":"# AGENTS.md\n\nThis file provides guidance to AI agents working with this repository.\n\n## What this repo is\n\nEDEN is a **protocol** for directed-code-evolution orchestration, not a single product. The repo contains three distinct kinds of artifact, each with different change discipline:\n\n| Layer | Path | Authoritative? | Change discipline |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| Protocol specification | [`spec/`](spec/) | Yes — the source of truth | RFC-style: versioned, normative (MUST/SHOULD/MAY), carefully reviewed |\n| Reference implementation | [`reference/`](reference/) | No — *one* valid impl | Normal code-review discipline |\n| Conformance suite | [`conformance/`](conformance/) | Normative for tests | Black-box; must be implementation-agnostic |\n\nNon-normative human docs live in [`docs/`](docs/).\n\n## Current phase\n\n**Phase 9 chunk 1 complete.** A reference Web UI service ([`reference/services/web-ui/`](reference/services/web-ui/)) ships the UI shell + planner module. It is a backend-for-frontend over `eden_wire.StoreClient`: holds the shared bearer, talks to the task-store-server only via the wire binding, and renders server-side Jinja templates. HTMX 1.9.12 is vendored under `static/` (SHA-256 pinned in the service README) as a progressive-enhancement layer — every mutating route works without JS via plain form-POST + 303 redirect / re-render, and HTMX-aware routes also serve a fragment when the browser sends `HX-Request: true` (the chunk-1 example is \"add another proposal row\" inline). The session cookie is `itsdangerous`-signed (HttpOnly + SameSite=Lax + Path=/, plus opt-in `--secure-cookies` for TLS deployments) and carries a per-session CSRF token validated on every mutating route. The planner page surfaces the experiment's `objective` and `metrics_schema` (read-only) plus a recent-proposals/recent-trials context panel, then walks a 3-phase write — `create_proposal(state=\"drafting\")` for every row, then `mark_proposal_ready` for each, then `submit` with retry-before-orphan (3 attempts, exponential backoff) leveraging chapter 07 §2.4 / §8.1 idempotent resubmit. The narrowest unsafe window (Phase 2 → Phase 3 ready-but-unreferenced proposals) is documented as a known limitation that applies equally to the existing scripted planner host. Cross-cutting addition: `eden_dispatch.sweep_expired_claims` runs once per `run_orchestrator_loop` iteration so UI claims with `expires_at` are reclaimed automatically when an abandoned tab strands a task. Tests cover unit (route-by-route + CSRF), cross-request flow (claim → submit + validation recovery + stranded-claim recovery via the sweeper), security invariants (cookie attributes + bearer non-leak), partial-write recovery (Phase-2 mid-loop failure leaves drafting-only; Phase-3 retry-then-orphan), and a two-process `pytest.mark.e2e` test that drives the full planner flow over real HTTP against a real task-store-server subprocess.\n\n**Phase 8c complete.** The in-process dispatch driver is removed: `eden-dispatch.run_experiment` was deleted and its public-API surface narrows to `run_orchestrator_iteration` plus the scripted workers (`ScriptedPlanner` / `ScriptedImplementer` / `ScriptedEvaluator`). The Phase 5 / 7b in-process end-to-end tests in `reference/packages/eden-dispatch/tests/test_end_to_end.py` were deleted; the lifecycle-reconstruction invariant they covered moved into `reference/services/orchestrator/tests/test_e2e.py` as a `_reconstruct_lifecycle` fold over the post-teardown `SqliteStore.read_range()`. The malformed-success → `validation_error` routing test in `eden-storage/tests/test_store_hardening.py` was rewritten to drive `run_orchestrator_iteration` directly through the legitimate `Store` API. The `pytest.mark.e2e` real-subprocess test from Phase 8b is now the only end-to-end coverage path; components communicate exclusively through the Phase 8a wire binding.\n\n**Phase 8b complete.** Five reference services under [`reference/services/`](reference/services/) extract the dispatch loop into independent OS processes that communicate only over the Phase 8a wire binding: [`task-store-server/`](reference/services/task-store-server/) hosts the `Store` behind uvicorn; [`orchestrator/`](reference/services/orchestrator/) runs the finalize/dispatch/integrate half of the loop against a `StoreClient`; [`planner/`](reference/services/planner/), [`implementer/`](reference/services/implementer/), and [`evaluator/`](reference/services/evaluator/) are standalone worker hosts. A new shared package [`reference/services/_common/`](reference/services/_common/) holds the JSON-line logger, SIGTERM/SIGINT stop flag, readiness probe, common argparse flags, scripted `plan_fn`/`implement_fn`/`evaluate_fn` profiles, and the `seed_bare_repo` helper. `eden-dispatch` exposes `run_orchestrator_iteration` as its orchestrator-side entry point so the standalone orchestrator drives only that half (the in-process `run_experiment` driver remained alongside it through 8b and was deleted in 8c). `eden-wire.make_app(store, *, shared_token=…)` and `eden-wire.StoreClient(…, token=…)` add reference-only bearer-token auth: requests without the right `Authorization: Bearer <token>` are rejected as `eden://reference-error/unauthorized` (HTTP 401), under a new namespace deliberately kept out of the normative `eden://error/…` vocabulary in [`spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md`](spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md) §7. A new informative §12 in chapter 07 documents the scheme. The implementer host writes real git commits (single-parent or merge, honoring `proposal.parent_commits`) on `work/*` branches in the bare repo the orchestrator integrates from. A `pytest.mark.e2e` test in [`reference/services/orchestrator/tests/test_e2e.py`](reference/services/orchestrator/tests/test_e2e.py) forks all five processes on an ephemeral port, drives a 3-trial experiment to quiescence over real HTTP + real SQLite + real git, and tears down with SIGTERM → SIGKILL fallback.\n\n**Phase 8a complete.** `eden-wire` ships an HTTP binding for the chapter 4 / 5 / 6 §3.4 / 8 §§1.1–2.1 operations specified in the new [`spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md`](spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md) chapter. The package exposes a FastAPI ``make_app(store)`` that routes every wire endpoint to a ``Store`` instance, and a ``StoreClient`` that satisfies the same ``Store`` Protocol against the HTTP surface — so existing callers (dispatch driver, integrator) work across the process boundary unchanged. Errors round-trip as RFC 7807 problem+json with a closed vocabulary of ``eden://error/<name>`` types (§7). ``Store.integrate_trial`` is now same-value idempotent (§5 of the chapter); the ``Integrator`` distinguishes different-SHA divergence (``AtomicityViolation``, no ref compensation) from other synchronous rejections (normal §3.4 compensating-delete flow). ``StoreClient.integrate_trial`` reconciles transport-indeterminate failures via read-back: the three outcomes are confirmed success, confirmed divergence (``InvalidPrecondition``), or ``IndeterminateIntegration`` when the server's outcome cannot be determined. Polling + long-poll-style subscribe are both bound (§6). Worker-process extraction, SSE/WebSocket push, and cut-over of in-process paths remain Phase 8b / 8c.\n\n**Phase 6 complete.** The `eden-storage` package ships the `Store` structural interface for the task store, event log, and proposal/trial persistence sides of chapter 8 — collapsed into a single Protocol per §7 implementation latitude. Two conforming backends satisfy it: `InMemoryStore` (fast, non-durable; moved from `eden-dispatch`) and `SqliteStore` (durable across restarts via a WAL-mode SQLite database with `synchronous=FULL`, matching §3.1's crash-survival requirement). The Protocol covers the spec-literal `create_task` / `replay` / `read_range` operations alongside the typed convenience helpers. Both backends share the transition logic in `_base.py`, and the same conformance scenarios are parametrized across both — drift from the Protocol surfaces in tests, not in production. Restart-safety tests close and reopen a SQLite store mid-experiment to confirm state, event log, and claim tokens survive, and a monkey-patched `_apply_commit` failure verifies rollback. The artifact store (§5), `subscribe` streaming (§2.1), and Postgres remain non-goals for Phase 6; they land in Phase 10 / Phase 8 / later respectively.\n\n**Phase 7b complete.** `eden-git` now also ships the `Integrator` that composes `GitRepo` with a `Store` to promote `success` trials per chapter 6. Given a trial with a recorded `commit_sha`, `Integrator.integrate` builds the §3.2 single-commit squash (worker-tip tree plus the eval manifest at `.eden/trials/<trial_id>/eval.json`), writes the `refs/heads/trial/<id>-<slug>` ref via zero-oid CAS, and routes the store's atomic `integrate_trial` write for `trial_commit_sha` and the `trial.integrated` event. On store failure the ref is compensatingly deleted per §3.4, matching the post-promotion reading recorded in [`spec/v0/design-notes/integrator-atomicity.md`](spec/v0/design-notes/integrator-atomicity.md). Re-invocation on an already-promoted trial is a verified no-op (§5.3): ref SHA, squash tree shape, and manifest bytes are re-derived and compared. §2 preconditions (`status == success`, `commit_sha` reachable from `branch` tip), §1.4 reachability, and §2 metrics validity are all enforced up front; the new public `Store.validate_metrics` closes the §2 MUST-NOT-promote clause even if upstream orchestrator validation were bypassed. The spec itself was tightened at §3.4 to make the post-promotion reading explicit. The dispatch driver accepted `integrate_trial: Callable[[str], object]` in place of the Phase 5 placeholder `integrator_commit_factory` parameter; in Phase 8c that hook moved to `run_orchestrator_iteration`. Eval-manifest bytes are deterministic (sorted keys, `indent=2`, trailing newline) to make §5.3 idempotency re-derivation stable.\n\n**Phase 7a complete.** `eden-git`'s subprocess wrapper ships `GitRepo` covering ref/object inspection (`rev_parse`, `resolve_ref`, `list_refs`, `is_ancestor`, `ls_tree`), plumbing (`write_blob`, `write_tree_from_entries`, `write_tree_with_file`, `commit_tree`, `create_ref`, `update_ref`), worktree management, and branch management. Author identity and `commit.gpgsign=false` are pinned per-invocation so the user's ambient git config never leaks into integrator commits. See [`docs/roadmap.md`](docs/roadmap.md) for the full 13-phase plan.\n\n## Commands\n\nAt Phase 9 chunk 1, markdown linting, JSON Schema validation, and the Python toolchain for the `eden-contracts`, `eden-dispatch`, `eden-git`, `eden-storage`, `eden-wire`, `eden-service-common`, `eden-task-store-server`, `eden-orchestrator`, `eden-planner-host`, `eden-implementer-host`, `eden-evaluator-host`, and `eden-web-ui` reference packages and services are wired up.\n\n| Command | Purpose |\n|---|---|\n| `npx --yes markdownlint-cli2@0.14.0 \"**/*.md\" \"#node_modules\" \"#.venv\" \"#docs/archive/**\" \"#docs/plans/review/**\"` | Lint all tracked markdown (pinned to CI's version; matches CI exactly) |\n| `pipx run 'check-jsonschema==0.29.4' --check-metaschema spec/v0/schemas/*.schema.json` | Validate each schema file against the Draft 2020-12 meta-schema (version pinned to CI) |\n| `pipx run 'check-jsonschema==0.29.4' --schemafile spec/v0/schemas/experiment-config.schema.json tests/fixtures/experiment/.eden/config.yaml` | Validate the fixture experiment config against its schema |\n| `uv sync` | Install/refresh the workspace virtualenv (all reference packages + services) |\n| `uv run ruff check .` | Lint Python (config in root `pyproject.toml`) |\n| `uv run pyright` | Type-check the reference Python packages |\n| `uv run pytest -q` | Run the full reference test suite (includes the `pytest.mark.e2e` real-subprocess test) |\n| `uv run pytest -m e2e` | Run only the real-subprocess Phase 8b end-to-end test |\n| `uv run pytest reference/packages/eden-contracts/tests/test_schema_parity.py` | Run only the schema ↔ Pydantic model parity check |\n| `python3 -m eden_task_store_server --db-path :memory: --experiment-id exp-1 --experiment-config tests/fixtures/experiment/.eden/config.yaml --port 0` | Run the reference task-store-server (announces `EDEN_TASK_STORE_LISTENING host=… port=…` on stdout). |\n| `python3 -m eden_orchestrator …` / `python3 -m eden_planner_host …` / `python3 -m eden_implementer_host …` / `python3 -m eden_evaluator_host …` / `python3 -m eden_web_ui …` | Run each reference service (see each service's `README.md` for full flag list). The web-ui announces `EDEN_WEB_UI_LISTENING host=… port=…` on stdout, mirroring the task-store-server convention. |\n| `python3 scripts/spec-xref-check.py` | Validate every `§N.M` reference in `spec/v0/*.md` resolves to a real section heading in its target chapter. Run before committing a normative spec change. |\n\n### Commands that will exist in later phases\n\nThese are listed for orientation; the tooling is not wired up yet.\n\n| Command | Lands in |\n|---|---|\n| `docker compose up` end-to-end | Phase 10 |\n\n## Contribution conventions\n\n### Spec edits\n\n+ Every cross-component contract (wire format, state machine, invariant) is defined in `spec/` first, then implemented in `reference/`. When they disagree, the spec wins and the impl gets a bug.\n+ Spec chapters use RFC 2119 language: MUST, SHOULD, MAY.\n+ Spec versions are frozen once stable (`spec/v0/` is a single lineage; breaking changes go to `spec/v1/`).\n+ Schema changes must update the spec prose, the JSON Schema file, and (from Phase 3 onward) the Pydantic bindings in lockstep. CI will enforce schema ↔ model parity once both exist.\n\n### Reference implementation\n\n+ Normal code-review discipline.\n+ Normative behavior is tested against the conformance suite (once it exists in Phase 11).\n+ The reference impl is explicitly not a monopoly on correctness. A third-party implementation that passes conformance is equally valid.\n\n### Adding a new service or package with its own `tests/` directory\n\nPytest's default `prepend` import mode names test modules by file basename, so two `tests/test_app.py` files in different packages collide on collection (`import file mismatch`). The repo's tests-dir layout is mixed — some packages (e.g. [`eden-contracts/tests/`](reference/packages/eden-contracts/tests/)) have `__init__.py` and use `from .conftest import …`; most service test dirs (e.g. [`web-ui/tests/`](reference/services/web-ui/tests/)) have no `__init__.py` and use `from conftest import …`.\n\nWhen adding a new tests directory:\n\n+ Use a basename that is unique across the whole `testpaths` set in [`pyproject.toml`](pyproject.toml). Grep `find reference -name 'test_*.py' -exec basename {} \\;` to verify before merging.\n+ Do **not** add `__init__.py` to a service tests dir while another tests dir already has one — both directories become the `tests` package and pytest's plugin manager rejects the second conftest with `Plugin already registered under a different name`.\n+ Switching the whole repo to `--import-mode=importlib` looks tempting but is **not** a clean fix today: the existing eden-contracts tests use relative `.conftest` imports that depend on the `__init__.py` package layout, and importlib mode keeps that collision while removing the sys.path hack the rest of the suite relies on.\n\nIf the constraint becomes load-bearing later (Phase 10 will add several more service test dirs), the cleanest fix is to give each tests dir a unique package name (e.g. `web_ui_tests/` instead of `tests/`) and switch to importlib mode in lockstep.\n\n### Adding or extending a JSON Schema + Pydantic binding\n\nThe `schema-parity` CI job is only as strong as what both sides of the test actually enforce. Several Pydantic and `jsonschema` defaults let drift through silently. When adding a new schema — or a new field type to an existing one — evaluate each of the following; reusable implementations live in [`reference/packages/eden-contracts/src/eden_contracts/_common.py`](reference/packages/eden-contracts/src/eden_contracts/_common.py).\n\n+ **Strict numeric parsing.** Top-level models set `ConfigDict(strict=True, extra=\"allow\")`. Non-strict mode coerces `True`/`\"2\"` into int, but the schemas treat `type: integer` / `type: number` as strict JSON types. New models must keep `strict=True`.\n+ **Format assertions on both sides.** `format` keywords (`uri`, `date-time`, …) are advisory by default in both the `jsonschema` library and Pydantic. The schema-side validator wires a custom `FormatChecker` in [`tests/conftest.py`](reference/packages/eden-contracts/tests/conftest.py), and the model side uses the reusable types in `_common.py`. A new `format` keyword in any schema requires handlers on *both* sides; the `test_format_coverage` test fails loudly if the schema-side handler is missing.\n+ **Real date-time / URI validation, not just regex or `urlparse`.** The regex on `DateTimeStr` accepts impossible values like `2026-99-99T…Z`; an `AfterValidator` runs `datetime.fromisoformat` to reject those. `UriStr` uses `rfc3986-validator`, not `urllib.parse.urlparse`, which accepts malformed schemeful URIs (e.g., spaces in the host).\n+ **Null vs absent.** JSON Schema's `type: X` rejects explicit `null`, but Pydantic's `X | None = None` accepts it. Wrap every optional typed field with `NotNone` from `_common.py` so absent is accepted and explicit null is rejected.\n+ **Round-trip emission.** `model.model_dump(mode=\"json\", exclude_none=True)` must re-validate against the schema. Add any new model to `tests/test_roundtrip.py` so this is checked.\n+ **Corpus coverage.** Parity is asserted over the fixture corpus in `tests/cases.py`. It discovers project-specific style guides and conventions automatically.\n\n## Instructions\n\n### Discovery\n\nLook for project-specific context:\n1. `CLAUDE.md` — agent instructions and rules\n2. `STYLE_GUIDE.md` or `style-guide.md` — formatting rules\n3. `static-analysis/markdown/.markdownlint.json` or `.markdownlint-cli2.cjs` — linting config\n4. `Tags.md` or `docs/Tags.md` — tag taxonomy\n5. Existing documents — infer conventions from what's already there\n\n### Identify the Document\n\nUse the file path from arguments, or detect from the current branch's diff against main.\n\n### Review Dimensions\n\nRead the document thoroughly. For each finding, classify as:\n- **CRITICAL** — must fix before merge\n- **SUGGESTION** — recommended improvement\n- **STYLE** — minor preference\n\n#### 1. Structure\n- Logical heading hierarchy (no skipped levels)\n- Sections flow coherently\n- No empty/stub sections\n- First heading level matches project convention\n\n#### 2. Cross-References\n- Internal links resolve to existing files\n- External links use proper syntax\n- Link text is descriptive\n- No bare URLs (if project convention prohibits them)\n\n#### 3. Content Completeness\n- Document achieves its apparent purpose\n- No unanswered inline questions without explanation\n- Referenced data/figures exist or are marked as pending\n\n#### 4. Frontmatter\n- Required fields present (infer from other docs if no explicit spec)\n- Values are valid (dates in ISO format, tags exist in taxonomy)\n\n#### 5. Tone and Clarity\n- Consistent tone throughout\n- Clear, concise sentences\n- Technical terms defined on first use\n- Acronyms expanded on first use\n\n#### 6. Terminology Consistency\n- Same concept uses same term throughout\n- Proper names correctly capitalized\n\n#### 7. Formatting\n- Matches project style guide\n- Code blocks have language identifiers\n- Tables properly formatted\n- Consistent list and emphasis style\n\n### Output Format\n\n```text\n## CRITICAL (must fix)\n- [finding] (line X)\n\n## SUGGESTIONS (recommended)\n- [finding] (line X)\n\n## STYLE (minor)\n- [finding] (line X)\n\n## Summary\n[2-3 sentence overall assessment]\n```\n\n## User input\n\n$ARGUMENTS\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

Mirrors the structural\ncontract pinned in chunk 9c (signed session cookies, per-session CSRF,\nHTMX progressive enhancement, retry-before-orphan + committed-state\nread-back keyed off `submissions_equivalent`) and the per-claim\nisolation pattern (`_CLAIMS` keyed on `(session.csrf, task_id)`),\nadapted for the evaluator's distinct shape.\n\nThe evaluator differs from the implementer in three load-bearing\nways:\n\n1. **No git work.** Per `spec/v0/03-roles.md` §4.3 the evaluator MUST\n   NOT mutate the worker branch or the canonical lineage; it reads\n   the trial at `trial.commit_sha` out-of-band (`git clone …` /\n   `git fetch && git checkout`) and types metrics back into the\n   form. The UI does not accept a `commit_sha` from the operator\n   and never calls `repo.create_ref` / any plumbing — the evaluator\n   module mounts unconditionally (no `--repo-path` requirement).\n2. **Trial already exists.** No `Phase 1 create_trial` step. The\n   evaluator's `EvaluateTask` carries `payload.trial_id` and the\n   trial is in `status=\"starting\"` with `commit_sha` set (per\n   §4.1 + chapter 04 §3.1). Submit becomes the single Phase, with\n   form validation up front and `store.submit` as the only mutation.\n3. **`status` has three values.** `success` / `error` / `eval_error`\n   per §4.4. Each maps to a different orchestrator-side trial\n   transition; `eval_error` keeps the trial in `starting` and may\n   be retried by a fresh evaluate task.\n\n## Non-goals\n\n- **Operator-side git operations.** Cloning the bare repo, checking\n  out the trial, running tests — all out-of-band. The UI surfaces\n  `trial.commit_sha` + `trial.branch` so the operator knows what to\n  check out, but the UI never proxies git.\n- **Metric autocomputation.** No \"run the evaluator script for me\"\n  button; chunk 9d is hand-typed metrics only. A scripted-evaluator\n  trigger button is a Phase 9e or later concern.\n- **Artifact upload by the UI.** The submission's `artifacts_uri` is\n  a free-text URI the operator types after uploading their\n  diagnostic logs / artifacts somewhere themselves. UI-driven\n  artifact upload is Phase 10 (compose stack ships an artifact\n  store) at the earliest.\n- **Per-metric optional/required policy at the UI level.** The spec\n  (§4.4) says metric keys are a subset of `metrics_schema`; we\n  honor the spec exactly (every metric is optional from the UI's\n  perspective, validated as a typed value when present), with one\n  pragmatic guardrail: **`status=success` requires at least one\n  metric value** so the trial isn't a \"successful no-signal\" black\n  hole. `error` and `eval_error` may carry zero metrics.\n- **Mutating `trial.metrics` directly from the UI.** That's the\n  orchestrator's exclusive write per §4.2 / §4.4 / chapter 04 §4.3.\n  The UI calls `store.submit` and the orchestrator's terminal\n  transition does the trial write atomically with the event.\n\n## Backwards-compatibility policy\n\nChunk 9d adds new routes (`/evaluator/*`), one new template family,\nand one new `parse_evaluate_form` helper. It changes nothing in the\nplanner or implementer modules, the wire binding, or the storage\nbackend. The CLI gains no new flag.\n\n## Tech-stack decision\n\nReuse everything chunk 9c established:\n\n- HTMX 1.9.12 vendored under `static/` for progressive enhancement\n  (chunk 9d does not introduce any HTMX-specific behavior — every\n  route works as a plain form-POST + 303 redirect — but the\n  evaluator templates extend `base.html` and inherit the\n  enhancement layer).\n- `itsdangerous`-signed session cookies (`HttpOnly`, `SameSite=Lax`,\n  `Path=/`; opt-in `Secure` via `--secure-cookies`).\n- Per-session CSRF token validated in constant time on every\n  mutating route.\n- Closed wire-error vocabulary: `eden://error/wrong-token`,\n  `eden://error/illegal-transition`,\n  `eden://error/conflicting-resubmission`,\n  `eden://error/invalid-precondition`.\n\nNo new dependencies. No new vendored assets.\n\n## §A New routes module\n\n`reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/evaluator.py`,\nprefix `/evaluator`. Routes:\n\n- `GET /evaluator/` — list pending `evaluate` tasks (kind=evaluate,\n  state=pending) plus a recent-trials context panel and the\n  experiment objective.\n- `POST /evaluator/{task_id}/claim` — claim with TTL (`expires_at =\n  now + claim_ttl_seconds`). Stash `(claim_token, trial_id)` keyed\n  on `(session.csrf, task_id)` in an in-process `_CLAIMS` dict.\n  `trial_id` is read from the *task payload*, not generated; see\n  §A.2.\n- `GET /evaluator/{task_id}/draft` — render the draft form (read-\n  only trial context + per-metric inputs + status radio + optional\n  artifacts_uri + optional description).\n- `POST /evaluator/{task_id}/submit` — validate, then submit with\n  retry-before-orphan + read-back. See §C.\n\nModule-level state:\n\n```python\n# (session.csrf, task_id) -> (claim token, trial_id)\n_CLAIMS: dict[tuple[str, str], tuple[str, str]] = {}\n```\n\nFixture-clearing: tests add an autouse fixture that clears `_CLAIMS`\nbetween tests, mirroring the implementer module.\n\nThe router is included **unconditionally** in `make_app` (no repo\ngate). Navigation in `base.html` gains a permanent \"evaluator\" link\n(no `evaluator_enabled` guard), reflecting that the evaluator\nmodule needs no service-level prerequisite beyond what the planner\nalready requires.\n\n### §A.1 Artifact trust boundary\n\nThe evaluator's draft view reuses\n`routes/_helpers.read_proposal_rationale(proposal, artifacts_dir)`\nto surface the proposal's rationale inline when (a) `artifacts_uri`\nis `file://`, (b) the resolved path is contained within the UI\nservice's `--artifacts-dir`, (c) the file is ≤ 1 MiB. Any other\nshape renders link-only. The helper was added in chunk 9c; chunk 9d\nadds **no new helper** — the same security envelope applies.\n\nThe trial's own `artifacts_uri` (set by a *previous* successful\nevaluation, then promoted onto the trial by the orchestrator —\nwhich never happens for a trial in `starting`, the only state the\nevaluator sees) is therefore always `None` while the evaluator is\nviewing it. We do not render trial-side artifact content at chunk\n9d. (If an evaluator wishes to attach diagnostic links to its own\nsubmission, those round-trip through the form's `artifacts_uri`\ntext input → `EvaluateSubmission.artifacts_uri` → orchestrator\nwrites onto the trial on terminal transition.)\n\nThe submission's `artifacts_uri` text input is **not** hyperlinked\non the confirmation page — it renders as a plain `<code>` block.\nHyperlinking a URL the operator just typed risks the same\nscheme-injection class lens 10 in\n`feedback_impl_review_lenses.md` flagged for the proposal's\n`artifacts_uri`. We avoid the question entirely by not rendering it\nas `<a href>`. (If the operator wants to follow it, they can copy\nit.)\n\n### §A.2 `trial_id` ownership\n\nThe evaluator's `trial_id` is **server-pinned via the task\npayload**, not generated by the UI:\n\n- `EvaluateTask.payload.trial_id` is the trial under evaluation;\n  the orchestrator put it there at task-creation time.\n- At claim, `routes/evaluator.claim` reads\n  `task.payload.trial_id` and stashes it in `_CLAIMS` alongside\n  the claim token.\n- `routes/evaluator.draft_form` and `routes/evaluator.submit` look\n  up `trial_id` from `_CLAIMS` (keyed on\n  `(session.csrf, task_id)`). The rendered form has no\n  `name=\"trial_id\"` input.\n- `routes/evaluator.submit` builds\n  `EvaluateSubmission(trial_id=trial_id, …)` from the `_CLAIMS`\n  entry. A forged `trial_id` form value is ignored.\n\nThis mirrors chunk 9c's lens — the request surface never carries\nthe canonical `trial_id`. The chunk-9c memory\n`feedback_impl_review_lenses.md` (lens-10 sibling: \"trial_id\nnon-leak\") applies unchanged.\n\n### §A.3 Metrics validation parity\n\nThe evaluator submission's `metrics` dict must satisfy:\n\n1. Keys are a subset of `experiment_config.metrics_schema.root`.\n   The form's per-metric inputs are *generated* from\n   `metrics_schema` so a well-behaved browser cannot submit a key\n   outside the schema — but the form parser still rejects unknown\n   names (a hand-crafted POST is the only way they appear).\n2. Each present value matches its declared `MetricType`:\n   - `integer` — Python `int` (rejects `bool`, rejects `1.5`).\n   - `real`    — Python `float`, finite (rejects `bool`, rejects\n                 `NaN` / `±inf`).\n   - `text`    — Python `str`.\n3. Empty form-input fields are treated as **omitted** (the metric\n   is left out of the dict, not stored as `None`).\n4. `status=\"success\"` requires `len(metrics) >= 1` (UI-side\n   guardrail; spec allows empty).\n\nThe Pydantic-bound `EvaluateSubmission.metrics: dict[str, Any]\n| None = None` accepts whatever Python types we put in. The\nauthoritative validation is `Store._validate_metrics`, which\n`store.submit` calls before transitioning the task. The form\nparser duplicates the spec's rules so the operator gets clean\nfield-level errors instead of a wire-error banner; on the\n*server* side, `store.submit` is the trust boundary and will\nraise `InvalidPrecondition` on any drift.\n\nThis is symmetric with how chunk 9c did §3.3 reachability twice\n(form-time + storage-time): cleaner UX, server still authoritative.\n\n## §B Forms helper\n\n`reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/forms.py` gains:\n\n```python\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass EvaluateDraft:\n    \"\"\"Validated evaluator-form input.\n\n    The route handler combines this with the server-owned\n    ``trial_id`` (from ``_CLAIMS``) to construct the\n    ``EvaluateSubmission`` object.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    status: Literal[\"success\", \"error\", \"eval_error\"]\n    metrics: dict[str, int | float | str]   # only present-and-valid keys\n    artifacts_uri: str | None\n    description: str | None\n\n\ndef parse_evaluate_form(\n    *,\n    metrics_schema: MetricsSchema,\n    status_raw: str,\n    metric_inputs: Mapping[str, str],   # key -> raw form value, one per schema entry\n    artifacts_uri_raw: str,\n    description_raw: str,\n) -> tuple[EvaluateDraft | None, FormErrors]:\n    ...\n```\n\nValidation rules:\n\n- `status` must be one of `{\"success\", \"error\", \"eval_error\"}`;\n  anything else → `errors.add(0, \"status\", …)` and `(None, errors)`.\n- Each `metric_inputs[name]`:\n  - empty/whitespace → omitted from the result dict (no error).\n  - present + non-empty:\n    - `metrics_schema.root[name] == \"integer\"`: parse with `int(s)`,\n      reject explicit `bool` (Python `bool` is `int`; the form-side\n      input is always a string so this isn't reachable, but the\n      parser checks `s.lower() not in (\"true\", \"false\")` to reject\n      a hand-crafted POST that puts `True` in an integer field).\n    - `\"real\"`: parse with `float(s)`, reject `nan`/`inf`/`-inf`\n      via `math.isfinite` (mirroring `Store._validate_metrics`).\n    - `\"text\"`: keep as `s.strip()` (non-empty after strip required;\n      an all-whitespace input is treated as omitted).\n  - any unknown key in `metric_inputs` outside `metrics_schema.root`\n    → `errors.add(0, name, \"metric not in schema\")`. (Note: the\n    template only emits inputs for schema keys, so this only\n    triggers on a hand-crafted POST.)\n- `status == \"success\"` requires `len(metrics) >= 1`; otherwise\n  `errors.add_overall(\"status=success requires at least one\n  metric value\")`.\n- `artifacts_uri_raw.strip()` → `None` if empty. No URI shape check\n  at form time (the spec doesn't constrain it); the operator is\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
docs/plans/review/eden-phase-9d-evaluator-module/20260424T232413/0.jsonl:9:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_1","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"if [ -f /Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/CLAUDE.md ]; then sed -n '1,260p' /Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/CLAUDE.md; else printf 'NO_CLAUDE_MD\\\\n'; fi\"","aggregated_output":"# AGENTS.md\n\nThis file provides guidance to AI agents working with this repository.\n\n## What this repo is\n\nEDEN is a **protocol** for directed-code-evolution orchestration, not a single product. The repo contains three distinct kinds of artifact, each with different change discipline:\n\n| Layer | Path | Authoritative? | Change discipline |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| Protocol specification | [`spec/`](spec/) | Yes — the source of truth | RFC-style: versioned, normative (MUST/SHOULD/MAY), carefully reviewed |\n| Reference implementation | [`reference/`](reference/) | No — *one* valid impl | Normal code-review discipline |\n| Conformance suite | [`conformance/`](conformance/) | Normative for tests | Black-box; must be implementation-agnostic |\n\nNon-normative human docs live in [`docs/`](docs/).\n\n## Current phase\n\n**Phase 9 chunk 9c complete.** The Web UI service gains the implementer module, gated on a new optional `--repo-path <path>` CLI flag. With the flag set, [`routes/implementer.py`](reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/implementer.py) walks claim → draft → submit for `implement` tasks: list pending tasks, claim with TTL, render a draft form that surfaces the proposal's slug/priority/parent_commits/`artifacts_uri` (plus the rationale markdown rendered inline only when its `file://` path resolves inside `--artifacts-dir` and is ≤ 1 MiB — the §A.1 trust-boundary helper `read_proposal_rationale` lives in [`routes/_helpers.py`](reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/_helpers.py)), then submit. The submit handler enforces [`spec/v0/03-roles.md`](spec/v0/03-roles.md) §3.3 reachability via `eden_git.GitRepo.commit_exists` + `is_ancestor(parent, commit_sha)` against every parent in `proposal.parent_commits`, runs a Pre-Phase-1 ref-collision guard (`ref_exists(\"refs/heads/work/<slug>-<trial_id>\")` short-circuits with a form error and no `create_trial`), then walks Phase 1 (`store.create_trial(status=\"starting\")` — no commit_sha; `_accept_implement` writes commit_sha onto the trial later), Phase 2 (`repo.create_ref` on the canonical work branch — runs *after* `create_trial` so the §3.2 step-1 ordering \"trial persisted before observable repo writes\" holds), and Phase 3 (`store.submit` with retry-before-orphan: 3 attempts, exponential backoff, `WrongToken`/`IllegalTransition`/`ConflictingResubmission` short-circuit, transport-shaped exceptions retried). On retry exhaustion the route runs a committed-state read-back keyed off `read_task` + `read_submission` + `submissions_equivalent` (not `worker_id`, which is shared by default) — distinguishing \"our submit committed; lost the response\" (success page) from \"never committed\" (orphan page) from \"different submission won the race\" (orphan page, `eden://error/conflicting-resubmission`). Orphaned `starting` trials auto-recover via the chunk-1 expired-claim sweeper (`_base.reclaim` composite-commits the trial to `error` atomically with `pending`). `trial_id` is server-only — generated at claim time and stored in the in-process `_CLAIMS` dict alongside the claim token, never round-trips through the request surface; the rendered form has no `name=\"trial_id\"` input and a forged form value is ignored. Tests cover per-route validation (`test_implementer_routes.py`), the cross-request flow including reachability rejections and the status=error path (`test_implementer_flow.py`), each branch of §C-recovery via monkeypatched failures (`test_implementer_partial_write.py`), security invariants including the artifact-rendering trust boundary and the `trial_id` non-leak (`test_implementer_security.py`), and a `pytest.mark.e2e` two-process test that drives the full implementer flow against a real task-store-server + web-ui subprocess (`test_implementer_e2e.py`).\n\n**Phase 9 chunk 1 complete.** A reference Web UI service ([`reference/services/web-ui/`](reference/services/web-ui/)) ships the UI shell + planner module. It is a backend-for-frontend over `eden_wire.StoreClient`: holds the shared bearer, talks to the task-store-server only via the wire binding, and renders server-side Jinja templates. HTMX 1.9.12 is vendored under `static/` (SHA-256 pinned in the service README) as a progressive-enhancement layer — every mutating route works without JS via plain form-POST + 303 redirect / re-render, and HTMX-aware routes also serve a fragment when the browser sends `HX-Request: true` (the chunk-1 example is \"add another proposal row\" inline). The session cookie is `itsdangerous`-signed (HttpOnly + SameSite=Lax + Path=/, plus opt-in `--secure-cookies` for TLS deployments) and carries a per-session CSRF token validated on every mutating route. The planner page surfaces the experiment's `objective` and `metrics_schema` (read-only) plus a recent-proposals/recent-trials context panel, then walks a 3-phase write — `create_proposal(state=\"drafting\")` for every row, then `mark_proposal_ready` for each, then `submit` with retry-before-orphan (3 attempts, exponential backoff) leveraging chapter 07 §2.4 / §8.1 idempotent resubmit. The narrowest unsafe window (Phase 2 → Phase 3 ready-but-unreferenced proposals) is documented as a known limitation that applies equally to the existing scripted planner host. Cross-cutting addition: `eden_dispatch.sweep_expired_claims` runs once per `run_orchestrator_loop` iteration so UI claims with `expires_at` are reclaimed automatically when an abandoned tab strands a task. Tests cover unit (route-by-route + CSRF), cross-request flow (claim → submit + validation recovery + stranded-claim recovery via the sweeper), security invariants (cookie attributes + bearer non-leak), partial-write recovery (Phase-2 mid-loop failure leaves drafting-only; Phase-3 retry-then-orphan), and a two-process `pytest.mark.e2e` test that drives the full planner flow over real HTTP against a real task-store-server subprocess.\n\n**Phase 8c complete.** The in-process dispatch driver is removed: `eden-dispatch.run_experiment` was deleted and its public-API surface narrows to `run_orchestrator_iteration` plus the scripted workers (`ScriptedPlanner` / `ScriptedImplementer` / `ScriptedEvaluator`). The Phase 5 / 7b in-process end-to-end tests in `reference/packages/eden-dispatch/tests/test_end_to_end.py` were deleted; the lifecycle-reconstruction invariant they covered moved into `reference/services/orchestrator/tests/test_e2e.py` as a `_reconstruct_lifecycle` fold over the post-teardown `SqliteStore.read_range()`. The malformed-success → `validation_error` routing test in `eden-storage/tests/test_store_hardening.py` was rewritten to drive `run_orchestrator_iteration` directly through the legitimate `Store` API. The `pytest.mark.e2e` real-subprocess test from Phase 8b is now the only end-to-end coverage path; components communicate exclusively through the Phase 8a wire binding.\n\n**Phase 8b complete.** Five reference services under [`reference/services/`](reference/services/) extract the dispatch loop into independent OS processes that communicate only over the Phase 8a wire binding: [`task-store-server/`](reference/services/task-store-server/) hosts the `Store` behind uvicorn; [`orchestrator/`](reference/services/orchestrator/) runs the finalize/dispatch/integrate half of the loop against a `StoreClient`; [`planner/`](reference/services/planner/), [`implementer/`](reference/services/implementer/), and [`evaluator/`](reference/services/evaluator/) are standalone worker hosts. A new shared package [`reference/services/_common/`](reference/services/_common/) holds the JSON-line logger, SIGTERM/SIGINT stop flag, readiness probe, common argparse flags, scripted `plan_fn`/`implement_fn`/`evaluate_fn` profiles, and the `seed_bare_repo` helper. `eden-dispatch` exposes `run_orchestrator_iteration` as its orchestrator-side entry point so the standalone orchestrator drives only that half (the in-process `run_experiment` driver remained alongside it through 8b and was deleted in 8c). `eden-wire.make_app(store, *, shared_token=…)` and `eden-wire.StoreClient(…, token=…)` add reference-only bearer-token auth: requests without the right `Authorization: Bearer <token>` are rejected as `eden://reference-error/unauthorized` (HTTP 401), under a new namespace deliberately kept out of the normative `eden://error/…` vocabulary in [`spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md`](spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md) §7. A new informative §12 in chapter 07 documents the scheme. The implementer host writes real git commits (single-parent or merge, honoring `proposal.parent_commits`) on `work/*` branches in the bare repo the orchestrator integrates from. A `pytest.mark.e2e` test in [`reference/services/orchestrator/tests/test_e2e.py`](reference/services/orchestrator/tests/test_e2e.py) forks all five processes on an ephemeral port, drives a 3-trial experiment to quiescence over real HTTP + real SQLite + real git, and tears down with SIGTERM → SIGKILL fallback.\n\n**Phase 8a complete.** `eden-wire` ships an HTTP binding for the chapter 4 / 5 / 6 §3.4 / 8 §§1.1–2.1 operations specified in the new [`spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md`](spec/v0/07-wire-protocol.md) chapter. The package exposes a FastAPI ``make_app(store)`` that routes every wire endpoint to a ``Store`` instance, and a ``StoreClient`` that satisfies the same ``Store`` Protocol against the HTTP surface — so existing callers (dispatch driver, integrator) work across the process boundary unchanged. Errors round-trip as RFC 7807 problem+json with a closed vocabulary of ``eden://error/<name>`` types (§7). ``Store.integrate_trial`` is now same-value idempotent (§5 of the chapter); the ``Integrator`` distinguishes different-SHA divergence (``AtomicityViolation``, no ref compensation) from other synchronous rejections (normal §3.4 compensating-delete flow). ``StoreClient.integrate_trial`` reconciles transport-indeterminate failures via read-back: the three outcomes are confirmed success, confirmed divergence (``InvalidPrecondition``), or ``IndeterminateIntegration`` when the server's outcome cannot be determined. Polling + long-poll-style subscribe are both bound (§6). Worker-process extraction, SSE/WebSocket push, and cut-over of in-process paths remain Phase 8b / 8c.\n\n**Phase 6 complete.** The `eden-storage` package ships the `Store` structural interface for the task store, event log, and proposal/trial persistence sides of chapter 8 — collapsed into a single Protocol per §7 implementation latitude. Two conforming backends satisfy it: `InMemoryStore` (fast, non-durable; moved from `eden-dispatch`) and `SqliteStore` (durable across restarts via a WAL-mode SQLite database with `synchronous=FULL`, matching §3.1's crash-survival requirement). The Protocol covers the spec-literal `create_task` / `replay` / `read_range` operations alongside the typed convenience helpers. Both backends share the transition logic in `_base.py`, and the same conformance scenarios are parametrized across both — drift from the Protocol surfaces in tests, not in production. Restart-safety tests close and reopen a SQLite store mid-experiment to confirm state, event log, and claim tokens survive, and a monkey-patched `_apply_commit` failure verifies rollback. The artifact store (§5), `subscribe` streaming (§2.1), and Postgres remain non-goals for Phase 6; they land in Phase 10 / Phase 8 / later respectively.\n\n**Phase 7b complete.** `eden-git` now also ships the `Integrator` that composes `GitRepo` with a `Store` to promote `success` trials per chapter 6. Given a trial with a recorded `commit_sha`, `Integrator.integrate` builds the §3.2 single-commit squash (worker-tip tree plus the eval manifest at `.eden/trials/<trial_id>/eval.json`), writes the `refs/heads/trial/<id>-<slug>` ref via zero-oid CAS, and routes the store's atomic `integrate_trial` write for `trial_commit_sha` and the `trial.integrated` event. On store failure the ref is compensatingly deleted per §3.4, matching the post-promotion reading recorded in [`spec/v0/design-notes/integrator-atomicity.md`](spec/v0/design-notes/integrator-atomicity.md). Re-invocation on an already-promoted trial is a verified no-op (§5.3): ref SHA, squash tree shape, and manifest bytes are re-derived and compared. §2 preconditions (`status == success`, `commit_sha` reachable from `branch` tip), §1.4 reachability, and §2 metrics validity are all enforced up front; the new public `Store.validate_metrics` closes the §2 MUST-NOT-promote clause even if upstream orchestrator validation were bypassed. The spec itself was tightened at §3.4 to make the post-promotion reading explicit. The dispatch driver accepted `integrate_trial: Callable[[str], object]` in place of the Phase 5 placeholder `integrator_commit_factory` parameter; in Phase 8c that hook moved to `run_orchestrator_iteration`. Eval-manifest bytes are deterministic (sorted keys, `indent=2`, trailing newline) to make §5.3 idempotency re-derivation stable.\n\n**Phase 7a complete.** `eden-git`'s subprocess wrapper ships `GitRepo` covering ref/object inspection (`rev_parse`, `resolve_ref`, `list_refs`, `is_ancestor`, `ls_tree`), plumbing (`write_blob`, `write_tree_from_entries`, `write_tree_with_file`, `commit_tree`, `create_ref`, `update_ref`), worktree management, and branch management. Author identity and `commit.gpgsign=false` are pinned per-invocation so the user's ambient git config never leaks into integrator commits. See [`docs/roadmap.md`](docs/roadmap.md) for the full 13-phase plan.\n\n## Commands\n\nAt Phase 9 chunk 9c, markdown linting, JSON Schema validation, and the Python toolchain for the `eden-contracts`, `eden-dispatch`, `eden-git`, `eden-storage`, `eden-wire`, `eden-service-common`, `eden-task-store-server`, `eden-orchestrator`, `eden-planner-host`, `eden-implementer-host`, `eden-evaluator-host`, and `eden-web-ui` reference packages and services are wired up.\n\n| Command | Purpose |\n|---|---|\n| `npx --yes markdownlint-cli2@0.14.0 \"**/*.md\" \"#node_modules\" \"#.venv\" \"#docs/archive/**\" \"#docs/plans/review/**\"` | Lint all tracked markdown (pinned to CI's version; matches CI exactly) |\n| `pipx run 'check-jsonschema==0.29.4' --check-metaschema spec/v0/schemas/*.schema.json` | Validate each schema file against the Draft 2020-12 meta-schema (version pinned to CI) |\n| `pipx run 'check-jsonschema==0.29.4' --schemafile spec/v0/schemas/experiment-config.schema.json tests/fixtures/experiment/.eden/config.yaml` | Validate the fixture experiment config against its schema |\n| `uv sync` | Install/refresh the workspace virtualenv (all reference packages + services) |\n| `uv run ruff check .` | Lint Python (config in root `pyproject.toml`) |\n| `uv run pyright` | Type-check the reference Python packages |\n| `uv run pytest -q` | Run the full reference test suite (includes the `pytest.mark.e2e` real-subprocess test) |\n| `uv run pytest -m e2e` | Run only the real-subprocess Phase 8b end-to-end test |\n| `uv run pytest reference/packages/eden-contracts/tests/test_schema_parity.py` | Run only the schema ↔ Pydantic model parity check |\n| `python3 -m eden_task_store_server --db-path :memory: --experiment-id exp-1 --experiment-config tests/fixtures/experiment/.eden/config.yaml --port 0` | Run the reference task-store-server (announces `EDEN_TASK_STORE_LISTENING host=… port=…` on stdout). |\n| `python3 -m eden_orchestrator …` / `python3 -m eden_planner_host …` / `python3 -m eden_implementer_host …` / `python3 -m eden_evaluator_host …` / `python3 -m eden_web_ui …` | Run each reference service (see each service's `README.md` for full flag list). The web-ui announces `EDEN_WEB_UI_LISTENING host=… port=…` on stdout, mirroring the task-store-server convention. Pass `--repo-path <bare-repo>` to the web-ui to enable the implementer module; omit it for a planner-only deployment. |\n| `python3 scripts/spec-xref-check.py` | Validate every `§N.M` reference in `spec/v0/*.md` resolves to a real section heading in its target chapter. Run before committing a normative spec change. |\n\n### Commands that will exist in later phases\n\nThese are listed for orientation; the tooling is not wired up yet.\n\n| Command | Lands in |\n|---|---|\n| `docker compose up` end-to-end | Phase 10 |\n\n## Contribution conventions\n\n### Spec edits\n\n+ Every cross-component contract (wire format, state machine, invariant) is defined in `spec/` first, then implemented in `reference/`. When they disagree, the spec wins and the impl gets a bug.\n+ Spec chapters use RFC 2119 language: MUST, SHOULD, MAY.\n+ Spec versions are frozen once stable (`spec/v0/` is a single lineage; breaking changes go to `spec/v1/`).\n+ Schema changes must update the spec prose, the JSON Schema file, and (from Phase 3 onward) the Pydantic bindings in lockstep. CI will enforce schema ↔ model parity once both exist.\n\n### Reference implementation\n\n+ Normal code-review discipline.\n+ Normative behavior is tested against the conformance suite (once it exists in Phase 11).\n+ The reference impl is explicitly not a monopoly on correctness. A third-party implementation that passes conformance is equally valid.\n\n### Adding a new service or package with its own `tests/` directory\n\nPytest's default `prepend` import mode names test modules by file basename, so two `tests/test_app.py` files in different packages collide on collection (`import file mismatch`). The repo's tests-dir layout is mixed — some packages (e.g. [`eden-contracts/tests/`](reference/packages/eden-contracts/tests/)) have `__init__.py` and use `from .conftest import …`; most service test dirs (e.g. [`web-ui/tests/`](reference/services/web-ui/tests/)) have no `__init__.py` and use `from conftest import …`.\n\nWhen adding a new tests directory:\n\n+ Use a basename that is unique across the whole `testpaths` set in [`pyproject.toml`](pyproject.toml). Grep `find reference -name 'test_*.py' -exec basename {} \\;` to verify before merging.\n+ Do **not** add `__init__.py` to a service tests dir while another tests dir already has one — both directories become the `tests` package and the second conftest collides on `tests.conftest` import/registration. Under default `prepend` mode this surfaces as `ImportPathMismatchError`; under `importlib` mode it surfaces as `Plugin already registered under a different name`.\n+ Switching the whole repo to `--import-mode=importlib` looks tempting but is **not** a clean fix today: the existing eden-contracts tests use relative `.conftest` imports that depend on the `__init__.py` package layout, and importlib mode keeps that collision while removing the sys.path hack the rest of the suite relies on.\n\nIf the constraint becomes load-bearing later (Phase 10 will add several more service test dirs), the cleanest fix is a three-step lockstep: rename each tests dir to a unique package name (e.g. `web_ui_tests/` instead of `tests/`), switch every bare `from conftest import …` to a package-relative `from .conftest import …`, and add `--import-mode=importlib` to pytest's `addopts`. A mechanical basename-uniqueness check in CI is also a sensible follow-up — AGENTS-only guidance is weaker than a guardrail.\n\n### Adding or extending a JSON Schema + Pydantic binding\n\nThe `schema-parity` CI job is only as strong as what both sides of the test actually enforce. Several Pydantic and `jsonschema` defaults let drift through silently. When adding a new schema — or a new field type to an existing one — evaluate each of the following; reusable implementations live in [`reference/packages/eden-contracts/src/eden_contracts/_common.py`](reference/packages/eden-contracts/src/eden_contracts/_common.py).\n\n+ **Strict numeric parsing.** Top-level models set `ConfigDict(strict=True, extra=\"allow\")`. Non-strict mode coerces `True`/`\"2\"` into int, but the schemas treat `type: integer` / `type: number` as strict JSON types. New models must keep `strict=True`.\n+ **Format assertions on both sides.** `format` keywords (`uri`, `date-time`, …) are advisory by default in both the `jsonschema` library and Pydantic. The schema-side validator wires a custom `FormatChecker` in [`tests/conftest.py`](reference/packages/eden-contracts/tests/conftest.py), and the model side uses the reusable types in `_common.py`. A new `format` keyword in any schema requires handlers on *both* sides; the `test_format_coverage` test fails loudly if the schema-side handler is missing.\n+ **Real date-time / URI validation, not just regex or `urlparse`.** The regex on `DateTimeStr` accepts impossible values like `2026-99-99T…Z`; an `AfterValidator` runs `datetime.fromisoformat` to reject those. `UriStr` uses `rfc3986-validator`, not `urllib.parse.urlparse`, which accepts malformed schemeful URIs (e.g., spaces in the host).\n+ **Null vs absent.** JSON Schema's `type: X` rejects explicit `null`, but Pydantic's `X | None = None` accepts it. Wrap every optional typed field with `NotNone` from `_common.py` so absent is accepted and explicit null is rejected.\n+ **Round-trip emission.** `model.model_dump(mode=\"json\", exclude_none=True)` must re-validate against the schema. Add any new model to `tests/test_roundtrip.py` so this is checked.\n+ **Corpus coverage.** Parity is asserted over the fixture corpus in `tests/cases.py`. A new field type deserves at least one accept fixture and one reject fixture per constraint the schema imposes (required, pattern, enum, min/max, format, cross-field).\n\n## Commit guidelines\n\n+ Short imperative subjects (e.g., \"Add event protocol chapter\", \"Pin task state machine\").\n+ For pull requests, include: what the change is, which phase it advances, and any spec ↔ impl implications.\n\n## Related docs\n\n+ [`docs/naming.md`](docs/naming.md) — what EDEN is, at the concept level.\n+ [`docs/roadmap.md`](docs/roadmap.md) — the 13-phase build-up plan with unit-level decomposition.\n+ [`docs/plans/`](docs/plans/) — active implementation plans.\n+ [`docs/archive/`](docs/archive/) — historical reference docs.\n+ [`STYLE_GUIDE.md`](STYLE_GUIDE.md) — formatting and naming conventions.\n+ [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](CONTRIBUTING.md) — setup and PR workflow.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

Spec prose (`spec/`) has its own conventions noted at the end.\n\n## General\n\n- Indentation: 4 spaces (Python), 2 spaces (JSON, YAML).\n- Maximum line length: 120 characters.\n- Use trailing commas in multiline structures.\n- End files with a single newline.\n\n## Python\n\n- Target version: Python 3.12+.\n- Formatter/linter: [Ruff](https://docs.astral.sh/ruff/).\n- Type checker: [Pyright](https://github.com/microsoft/pyright), standard mode.\n\n### Naming\n\n| Kind | Convention | Example |\n|---|---|---|\n| Modules | `snake_case.py` | `task_queue.py` |\n| Test files | `test_<area>.py` | `test_task_queue.py` |\n| Functions, variables | `snake_case` | `claim_task()` |\n| Classes | `PascalCase` | `TaskClaim` |\n| Constants | `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` | `MAX_CLAIM_LEASE_S` |\n| Booleans | prefix with `is`, `has`, `should`, `can` | `is_idempotent` |\n\nRelated config fields that serve parallel roles use consistent grammatical form — e.g., all imperative verbs: `plan_command`, `implement_command`, `evaluate_command` — not a mix of verb/noun.\n\n### Type annotations\n\n- Explicit types on all public API signatures and dataclass fields.\n- Use `from __future__ import annotations` when needed for forward refs.\n- Prefer built-in generics (`list[str]`, `dict[str, int]`) over `typing` imports.\n\n### Docstrings\n\nGoogle style:\n\n```python\ndef claim_task(store: TaskStore, worker_id: str) -> Task | None:\n    \"\"\"Atomically claim the next ready task for a worker.\n\n    Args:\n        store: The task store.\n        worker_id: Identity of the claiming worker.\n\n    Returns:\n        The claimed task, or None if no tasks are ready.\n    \"\"\"\n```\n\nModule-, magic-method-, and `__init__` docstrings are not required.\n\n### Patterns\n\n**Frozen dataclasses for value types**:\n\n```python\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass TaskClaim:\n    task_id: str\n    worker_id: str\n    claim_token: str\n```\n\n**Explicit error handling at boundaries** (subprocess, network, file I/O):\n\n```python\ntry:\n    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, check=True)\nexcept subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:\n    raise ExecutionError(f\"Command failed: {e.stderr}\") from e\n```\n\n**Avoid mutable default arguments**:\n\n```python\n# Avoid\ndef process(items: list[str] = []) -> None: ...\n\n# Prefer\ndef process(items: list[str] | None = None) -> None:\n    items = items or []\n```\n\n## Ruff configuration\n\nLives in `pyproject.toml` once the first Python package lands in Phase 3. Baseline rules to carry over:\n\n- line-length 120, target Python 3.12\n- enabled rule sets: `A`, `B`, `D`, `E`, `F`, `I`, `PT`, `SIM`, `UP`\n- ignored: `D100`, `D105`, `D107`, `SIM108`\n- docstring rules disabled for test files\n\n## Pyright configuration\n\n- `typeCheckingMode = \"standard\"`\n- `reportUnnecessaryTypeIgnoreComment = \"warning\"`\n- `reportMissingTypeStubs = \"none\"`\n\n## Spec prose (`spec/`)\n\n- Use RFC 2119 normative keywords (MUST, SHOULD, MAY) where behavior is prescriptive. Informative prose uses ordinary English and says so.\n- Numbered sections per chapter.\n- Every wire format has a corresponding JSON Schema under `spec/v*/schemas/`. The chapter cites the schema file by path.\n- No references to specific technologies (Postgres, Redis, FastAPI). The spec describes *semantics*; mechanisms are reference-impl detail.\n\n## Markdown (all `.md` in the repo)\n\n- Atx-style headings (`#`, `##`, `###`).\n- Reference-style links are fine when a URL repeats; inline otherwise.\n- Code fences specify a language tag when applicable.\n- `markdownlint-cli2` is the authoritative linter; [`.markdownlint.json`](.markdownlint.json) holds the exception list. `MD013` (line length) is disabled on purpose (see next bullet).\n- **No mid-paragraph hard wrapping.** Each paragraph is one line; each list item is one line; line breaks appear only at block boundaries (paragraph, list item, heading, code fence, table row). This makes diffs localized to actual content changes rather than rewrap churn. Use `~/Documents/toolchain/scripts/reflow_markdown.py FILE` to reflow a hard-wrapped file before committing; run `markdownlint-cli2` after to catch any list-structure artifacts the heuristic reflow introduces (MD029 ordered-list prefixes are the common one).\n\nFor commands to run linters, see [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#commands).\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

The first submission's\n       `artifacts_uri` wins.)\n     - non-equivalent → orphan with `recovery_kind=\"conflict\"`\n       (`eden://error/conflicting-resubmission`).\n   - `task.state == \"pending\"` → orphan with\n     `recovery_kind=\"auto\"` (\"task reclaimed before our submit\n     committed; retry safe by re-claiming the task\").\n   - `read_task` itself raises → orphan with\n     `recovery_kind=\"transport\"` (read-back probe failure).\n\nThe single-step structure means there's no Phase-1-failed /\nPhase-2-failed split. The submit either succeeds, definitively\nfails (rendered as a form error or orphan), or transport-fails\ninto the read-back arm.\n\n### §C-recovery (per-error)\n\n| Outcome | Definition | UI rendering | Operator action |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| ok | submit returned cleanly OR retry returned cleanly OR read-back found equivalent prior submission | `evaluator_submitted.html`, 200 | none |\n| InvalidPrecondition (form-time, e.g. metric type drift only the server caught) | server rejected the metrics shape | re-render draft with the wire-error banner attached | fix the metrics and resubmit |\n| WrongToken | the prior reclaim invalidated our claim (sweeper or operator) | orphan, `recovery_kind=\"auto\"` | none — prior reclaim already restored task to `pending` |\n| IllegalTransition | task was reclaimed/terminalized before our submit landed | orphan, `recovery_kind=\"auto\"` | none |\n| ConflictingResubmission | a different submission won the race for this task | orphan, `recovery_kind=\"conflict\"` | operator triage; the system's record stands |\n| transport, retry exhausted, claim still ours | network glitch persisted; claim TTL will eventually reclaim | orphan, `recovery_kind=\"auto\"` | wait for TTL, or operator-side reclaim |\n| transport, retry exhausted, server committed equivalent | \"submitted, response lost\" sub-case A | success page (treated as `ok`) | none |\n| transport, retry exhausted, server committed non-equivalent | \"different submission won\" sub-case A' | orphan, `recovery_kind=\"conflict\"` | operator triage |\n| transport, retry exhausted, store invariant violated | implementation-illegal: terminal task with no recorded submission | orphan, `recovery_kind=\"transport\"` | operator investigation |\n| transport, retry exhausted, read-back probe itself failed | indeterminate | orphan, `recovery_kind=\"transport\"` | operator investigation |\n\nKey contrast with chunk 9c: there is **no orphaned `starting`\ntrial** to worry about. So the\n\"sweeper composite-commits the trial to error on reclaim\" recovery\nbranch from 9c does not apply here.
