docs/plans/review/eden-phase-9d-evaluator-module/20260424T232413/0.jsonl:40:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_20","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' reference/packages/eden-dispatch/src/eden_dispatch/workers.py\"","aggregated_output":"\"\"\"Scripted reference workers.\n\nThese workers drive the in-memory store through a full experiment\nlifecycle using deterministic fake outputs. They exercise the real\nstate machine — claim, execute, submit — so the dispatch loop's\nbehavior can be asserted end-to-end without any LLM or git machinery.\n\nPhase 5 non-goals (roadmap):\n  • no git: implementer ``commit_sha`` values are fabricated.\n  • no evaluation logic: metrics come from a script hook.\n  • no dispatch policy: there is one worker per role.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom collections.abc import Callable\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom typing import Any, Literal\n\nfrom eden_contracts import (\n    EvaluateTask,\n    ImplementTask,\n    PlanTask,\n    Proposal,\n    Trial,\n)\nfrom eden_storage import (\n    EvaluateSubmission,\n    ImplementSubmission,\n    PlanSubmission,\n    Store,\n)\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass ProposalTemplate:\n    \"\"\"Stand-in for the planner's domain logic; planner persists these as proposals.\"\"\"\n\n    slug: str\n    priority: float\n    parent_commits: tuple[str, ...]\n    artifacts_uri: str\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass ImplementOutcome:\n    \"\"\"Stand-in for the implementer's output.\"\"\"\n\n    status: Literal[\"success\", \"error\"]\n    commit_sha: str | None = None\n    branch: str | None = None\n    description: str | None = None\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass EvaluateOutcome:\n    \"\"\"Stand-in for the evaluator's output.\"\"\"\n\n    status: Literal[\"success\", \"error\", \"eval_error\"]\n    metrics: dict[str, Any] | None = None\n    artifacts_uri: str | None = None\n\n\nPlanFn = Callable[[PlanTask], list[ProposalTemplate]]\nImplementFn = Callable[[ImplementTask, Proposal], ImplementOutcome]\nEvaluateFn = Callable[[EvaluateTask, Trial], EvaluateOutcome]\n\n\nclass ScriptedPlanner:\n    \"\"\"Poll-and-run planner worker.\n\n    Discovers pending ``plan`` tasks, claims each in turn, drafts its\n    scripted proposals (one by one, marking each ``ready`` before\n    submitting), and submits with ``status=success``. Multi-proposal\n    drafting per plan is supported; zero-proposal plans also submit\n    with success per ``03-roles.md`` §2.4.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        worker_id: str,\n        plan_fn: PlanFn,\n        *,\n        proposal_id_factory: Callable[[], str],\n        now: Callable[[], str],\n    ) -> None:\n        self._worker_id = worker_id\n        self._plan_fn = plan_fn\n        self._proposal_id_factory = proposal_id_factory\n        self._now = now\n\n    @property\n    def worker_id(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Opaque worker identifier this planner claims tasks under.\"\"\"\n        return self._worker_id\n\n    def run_pending(\n        self,\n        store: Store,\n        *,\n        stop: Callable[[], bool] | None = None,\n    ) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Claim and process every pending plan task. Returns count processed.\n\n        If ``stop`` is provided, it is consulted before each task; the loop\n        returns early when it returns ``True``. This lets a host running\n        in a SIGTERM-aware loop break mid-drain instead of finishing the\n        whole queue first.\n        \"\"\"\n        count = 0\n        while True:\n            if stop is not None and stop():\n                return count\n            pending = store.list_tasks(kind=\"plan\", state=\"pending\")\n            if not pending:\n                return count\n            task = pending[0]\n            assert isinstance(task, PlanTask)\n            self._handle(store, task)\n            count += 1\n\n    def _handle(self, store: Store, task: PlanTask) -> None:\n        claim = store.claim(task.task_id, self._worker_id)\n        templates = self._plan_fn(task)\n        proposal_ids: list[str] = []\n        for tpl in templates:\n            proposal_id = self._proposal_id_factory()\n            proposal = Proposal(\n                proposal_id=proposal_id,\n                experiment_id=store.experiment_id,\n                slug=tpl.slug,\n                priority=tpl.priority,\n                parent_commits=list(tpl.parent_commits),\n                artifacts_uri=tpl.artifacts_uri,\n                state=\"drafting\",\n                created_at=self._now(),\n            )\n            store.create_proposal(proposal)\n            store.mark_proposal_ready(proposal_id)\n            proposal_ids.append(proposal_id)\n        store.submit(\n            task.task_id,\n            claim.token,\n            PlanSubmission(status=\"success\", proposal_ids=tuple(proposal_ids)),\n        )\n\n\nclass ScriptedImplementer:\n    \"\"\"Poll-and-run implementer worker.\n\n    Discovers pending ``implement`` tasks, reads the referenced\n    proposal, creates a ``starting`` trial on a scripted ``work/*``\n    branch, and submits with the scripted outcome. A successful\n    outcome carries ``commit_sha``; an errored outcome submits with\n    ``status=error``.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        worker_id: str,\n        implement_fn: ImplementFn,\n        *,\n        trial_id_factory: Callable[[], str],\n        now: Callable[[], str],\n    ) -> None:\n        self._worker_id = worker_id\n        self._implement_fn = implement_fn\n        self._trial_id_factory = trial_id_factory\n        self._now = now\n\n    @property\n    def worker_id(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Opaque worker identifier this implementer claims tasks under.\"\"\"\n        return self._worker_id\n\n    def run_pending(\n        self,\n        store: Store,\n        *,\n        stop: Callable[[], bool] | None = None,\n    ) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Claim and process every pending implement task. Returns count processed.\n\n        ``stop`` lets a host break mid-drain on SIGTERM. See\n        :meth:`ScriptedPlanner.run_pending`.\n        \"\"\"\n        count = 0\n        while True:\n            if stop is not None and stop():\n                return count\n            pending = store.list_tasks(kind=\"implement\", state=\"pending\")\n            if not pending:\n                return count\n            task = pending[0]\n            assert isinstance(task, ImplementTask)\n            self._handle(store, task)\n            count += 1\n\n    def _handle(self, store: Store, task: ImplementTask) -> None:\n        proposal = store.read_proposal(task.payload.proposal_id)\n        claim = store.claim(task.task_id, self._worker_id)\n\n        trial_id = self._trial_id_factory()\n        outcome = self._implement_fn(task, proposal)\n        branch = outcome.branch or f\"work/{proposal.slug}-{trial_id}\"\n        trial_kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {\n            \"trial_id\": trial_id,\n            \"experiment_id\": store.experiment_id,\n            \"proposal_id\": proposal.proposal_id,\n            \"status\": \"starting\",\n            \"parent_commits\": list(proposal.parent_commits),\n            \"branch\": branch,\n            \"started_at\": self._now(),\n        }\n        if outcome.description is not None:\n            trial_kwargs[\"description\"] = outcome.description\n        trial = Trial(**trial_kwargs)\n        store.create_trial(trial)\n        store.submit(\n            task.task_id,\n            claim.token,\n            ImplementSubmission(\n                status=outcome.status,\n                trial_id=trial_id,\n                commit_sha=outcome.commit_sha,\n            ),\n        )\n\n\nclass ScriptedEvaluator:\n    \"\"\"Poll-and-run evaluator worker.\n\n    Discovers pending ``evaluate`` tasks, reads the referenced trial,\n    runs its scripted evaluation, and submits. ``success`` and\n    ``error`` write metrics on the trial via the orchestrator's\n    terminal transition; ``eval_error`` leaves the trial in\n    ``starting`` per ``03-roles.md`` §4.4.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        worker_id: str,\n        evaluate_fn: EvaluateFn,\n    ) -> None:\n        self._worker_id = worker_id\n        self._evaluate_fn = evaluate_fn\n\n    @property\n    def worker_id(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Opaque worker identifier this evaluator claims tasks under.\"\"\"\n        return self._worker_id\n\n    def run_pending(\n        self,\n        store: Store,\n        *,\n        stop: Callable[[], bool] | None = None,\n    ) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Claim and process every pending evaluate task. Returns count processed.\n\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
docs/plans/review/eden-phase-9d-evaluator-module/20260424T232413/0.jsonl:55:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_27","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"nl -ba docs/plans/eden-phase-9d-evaluator-module.md | sed -n '1,260p'\"","aggregated_output":"     1\t# Phase 9 chunk 9d — Web UI evaluator module\n     2\t\n     3\t## Goal\n     4\t\n     5\tAdd the evaluator role to the reference Web UI so a human can claim a\n     6\tpending `evaluate` task, inspect the trial-under-evaluation, fill in\n     7\tmetrics by hand, and submit the result. Mirrors the structural\n     8\tcontract pinned in chunk 9c (signed session cookies, per-session CSRF,\n     9\tHTMX progressive enhancement, retry-before-orphan + committed-state\n    10\tread-back keyed off `submissions_equivalent`) and the per-claim\n    11\tisolation pattern (`_CLAIMS` keyed on `(session.csrf, task_id)`),\n    12\tadapted for the evaluator's distinct shape.\n    13\t\n    14\tThe evaluator differs from the implementer in three load-bearing\n    15\tways:\n    16\t\n    17\t1. **No git work.** Per `spec/v0/03-roles.md` §4.3 the evaluator MUST\n    18\t   NOT mutate the worker branch or the canonical lineage; it reads\n    19\t   the trial at `trial.commit_sha` out-of-band (`git clone …` /\n    20\t   `git fetch && git checkout`) and types metrics back into the\n    21\t   form. The UI does not accept a `commit_sha` from the operator\n    22\t   and never calls `repo.create_ref` / any plumbing — the evaluator\n    23\t   module mounts unconditionally (no `--repo-path` requirement).\n    24\t2. **Trial already exists.** No `Phase 1 create_trial` step. The\n    25\t   evaluator's `EvaluateTask` carries `payload.trial_id` and the\n    26\t   trial is in `status=\"starting\"` with `commit_sha` set (per\n    27\t   §4.1 + chapter 04 §3.1). Submit becomes the single Phase, with\n    28\t   form validation up front and `store.submit` as the only mutation.\n    29\t3. **`status` has three values.** `success` / `error` / `eval_error`\n    30\t   per §4.4. Each maps to a different orchestrator-side trial\n    31\t   transition; `eval_error` keeps the trial in `starting` and may\n    32\t   be retried by a fresh evaluate task.\n    33\t\n    34\t## Non-goals\n    35\t\n    36\t- **Operator-side git operations.** Cloning the bare repo, checking\n    37\t  out the trial, running tests — all out-of-band. The UI surfaces\n    38\t  `trial.commit_sha` + `trial.branch` so the operator knows what to\n    39\t  check out, but the UI never proxies git.\n    40\t- **Metric autocomputation.** No \"run the evaluator script for me\"\n    41\t  button; chunk 9d is hand-typed metrics only. A scripted-evaluator\n    42\t  trigger button is a Phase 9e or later concern.\n    43\t- **Artifact upload by the UI.** The submission's `artifacts_uri` is\n    44\t  a free-text URI the operator types after uploading their\n    45\t  diagnostic logs / artifacts somewhere themselves. UI-driven\n    46\t  artifact upload is Phase 10 (compose stack ships an artifact\n    47\t  store) at the earliest.\n    48\t- **Per-metric optional/required policy at the UI level.** The spec\n    49\t  (§4.4) says metric keys are a subset of `metrics_schema`; we\n    50\t  honor the spec exactly (every metric is optional from the UI's\n    51\t  perspective, validated as a typed value when present), with one\n    52\t  pragmatic guardrail: **`status=success` requires at least one\n    53\t  metric value** so the trial isn't a \"successful no-signal\" black\n    54\t  hole. `error` and `eval_error` may carry zero metrics.\n    55\t- **Mutating `trial.metrics` directly from the UI.** That's the\n    56\t  orchestrator's exclusive write per §4.2 / §4.4 / chapter 04 §4.3.\n    57\t  The UI calls `store.submit` and the orchestrator's terminal\n    58\t  transition does the trial write atomically with the event.\n    59\t\n    60\t## Backwards-compatibility policy\n    61\t\n    62\tChunk 9d adds new routes (`/evaluator/*`), one new template family,\n    63\tand one new `parse_evaluate_form` helper. It changes nothing in the\n    64\tplanner or implementer modules, the wire binding, or the storage\n    65\tbackend. The CLI gains no new flag.\n    66\t\n    67\t## Tech-stack decision\n    68\t\n    69\tReuse everything chunk 9c established:\n    70\t\n    71\t- HTMX 1.9.12 vendored under `static/` for progressive enhancement\n    72\t  (chunk 9d does not introduce any HTMX-specific behavior — every\n    73\t  route works as a plain form-POST + 303 redirect — but the\n    74\t  evaluator templates extend `base.html` and inherit the\n    75\t  enhancement layer).\n    76\t- `itsdangerous`-signed session cookies (`HttpOnly`, `SameSite=Lax`,\n    77\t  `Path=/`; opt-in `Secure` via `--secure-cookies`).\n    78\t- Per-session CSRF token validated in constant time on every\n    79\t  mutating route.\n    80\t- Closed wire-error vocabulary: `eden://error/wrong-token`,\n    81\t  `eden://error/illegal-transition`,\n    82\t  `eden://error/conflicting-resubmission`,\n    83\t  `eden://error/invalid-precondition`.\n    84\t\n    85\tNo new dependencies. No new vendored assets.\n    86\t\n    87\t## §A New routes module\n    88\t\n    89\t`reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/evaluator.py`,\n    90\tprefix `/evaluator`. Routes:\n    91\t\n    92\t- `GET /evaluator/` — list pending `evaluate` tasks (kind=evaluate,\n    93\t  state=pending) plus a recent-trials context panel and the\n    94\t  experiment objective.\n    95\t- `POST /evaluator/{task_id}/claim` — claim with TTL (`expires_at =\n    96\t  now + claim_ttl_seconds`). Stash `(claim_token, trial_id)` keyed\n    97\t  on `(session.csrf, task_id)` in an in-process `_CLAIMS` dict.\n    98\t  `trial_id` is read from the *task payload*, not generated; see\n    99\t  §A.2.\n   100\t- `GET /evaluator/{task_id}/draft` — render the draft form (read-\n   101\t  only trial context + per-metric inputs + status radio + optional\n   102\t  artifacts_uri + optional description).\n   103\t- `POST /evaluator/{task_id}/submit` — validate, then submit with\n   104\t  retry-before-orphan + read-back. See §C.\n   105\t\n   106\tModule-level state:\n   107\t\n   108\t```python\n   109\t# (session.csrf, task_id) -> (claim token, trial_id)\n   110\t_CLAIMS: dict[tuple[str, str], tuple[str, str]] = {}\n   111\t```\n   112\t\n   113\tFixture-clearing: tests add an autouse fixture that clears `_CLAIMS`\n   114\tbetween tests, mirroring the implementer module.\n   115\t\n   116\tThe router is included **unconditionally** in `make_app` (no repo\n   117\tgate). Navigation in `base.html` gains a permanent \"evaluator\" link\n   118\t(no `evaluator_enabled` guard), reflecting that the evaluator\n   119\tmodule needs no service-level prerequisite beyond what the planner\n   120\talready requires.\n   121\t\n   122\t### §A.1 Artifact trust boundary\n   123\t\n   124\tThe evaluator's draft view reuses\n   125\t`routes/_helpers.read_proposal_rationale(proposal, artifacts_dir)`\n   126\tto surface the proposal's rationale inline when (a) `artifacts_uri`\n   127\tis `file://`, (b) the resolved path is contained within the UI\n   128\tservice's `--artifacts-dir`, (c) the file is ≤ 1 MiB. Any other\n   129\tshape renders link-only. The helper was added in chunk 9c; chunk 9d\n   130\tadds **no new helper** — the same security envelope applies.\n   131\t\n   132\tThe trial's own `artifacts_uri` (set by a *previous* successful\n   133\tevaluation, then promoted onto the trial by the orchestrator —\n   134\twhich never happens for a trial in `starting`, the only state the\n   135\tevaluator sees) is therefore always `None` while the evaluator is\n   136\tviewing it. We do not render trial-side artifact content at chunk\n   137\t9d. (If an evaluator wishes to attach diagnostic links to its own\n   138\tsubmission, those round-trip through the form's `artifacts_uri`\n   139\ttext input → `EvaluateSubmission.artifacts_uri` → orchestrator\n   140\twrites onto the trial on terminal transition.)\n   141\t\n   142\tThe submission's `artifacts_uri` text input is **not** hyperlinked\n   143\ton the confirmation page — it renders as a plain `<code>` block.\n   144\tHyperlinking a URL the operator just typed risks the same\n   145\tscheme-injection class lens 10 in\n   146\t`feedback_impl_review_lenses.md` flagged for the proposal's\n   147\t`artifacts_uri`. We avoid the question entirely by not rendering it\n   148\tas `<a href>`. (If the operator wants to follow it, they can copy\n   149\tit.)\n   150\t\n   151\t### §A.2 `trial_id` ownership\n   152\t\n   153\tThe evaluator's `trial_id` is **server-pinned via the task\n   154\tpayload**, not generated by the UI:\n   155\t\n   156\t- `EvaluateTask.payload.trial_id` is the trial under evaluation;\n   157\t  the orchestrator put it there at task-creation time.\n   158\t- At claim, `routes/evaluator.claim` reads\n   159\t  `task.payload.trial_id` and stashes it in `_CLAIMS` alongside\n   160\t  the claim token.\n   161\t- `routes/evaluator.draft_form` and `routes/evaluator.submit` look\n   162\t  up `trial_id` from `_CLAIMS` (keyed on\n   163\t  `(session.csrf, task_id)`). The rendered form has no\n   164\t  `name=\"trial_id\"` input.\n   165\t- `routes/evaluator.submit` builds\n   166\t  `EvaluateSubmission(trial_id=trial_id, …)` from the `_CLAIMS`\n   167\t  entry. A forged `trial_id` form value is ignored.\n   168\t\n   169\tThis mirrors chunk 9c's lens — the request surface never carries\n   170\tthe canonical `trial_id`. The chunk-9c memory\n   171\t`feedback_impl_review_lenses.md` (lens-10 sibling: \"trial_id\n   172\tnon-leak\") applies unchanged.\n   173\t\n   174\t### §A.3 Metrics validation parity\n   175\t\n   176\tThe evaluator submission's `metrics` dict must satisfy:\n   177\t\n   178\t1. Keys are a subset of `experiment_config.metrics_schema.root`.\n   179\t   The form's per-metric inputs are *generated* from\n   180\t   `metrics_schema` so a well-behaved browser cannot submit a key\n   181\t   outside the schema — but the form parser still rejects unknown\n   182\t   names (a hand-crafted POST is the only way they appear).\n   183\t2. Each present value matches its declared `MetricType`:\n   184\t   - `integer` — Python `int` (rejects `bool`, rejects `1.5`).\n   185\t   - `real`    — Python `float`, finite (rejects `bool`, rejects\n   186\t                 `NaN` / `±inf`).\n   187\t   - `text`    — Python `str`.\n   188\t3. Empty form-input fields are treated as **omitted** (the metric\n   189\t   is left out of the dict, not stored as `None`).\n   190\t4. `status=\"success\"` requires `len(metrics) >= 1` (UI-side\n   191\t   guardrail; spec allows empty).\n   192\t\n   193\tThe Pydantic-bound `EvaluateSubmission.metrics: dict[str, Any]\n   194\t| None = None` accepts whatever Python types we put in. The\n   195\tauthoritative validation is `Store._validate_metrics`, which\n   196\t`store.submit` calls before transitioning the task. The form\n   197\tparser duplicates the spec's rules so the operator gets clean\n   198\tfield-level errors instead of a wire-error banner; on the\n   199\t*server* side, `store.submit` is the trust boundary and will\n   200\traise `InvalidPrecondition` on any drift.\n   201\t\n   202\tThis is symmetric with how chunk 9c did §3.3 reachability twice\n   203\t(form-time + storage-time): cleaner UX, server still authoritative.\n   204\t\n   205\t## §B Forms helper\n   206\t\n   207\t`reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/forms.py` gains:\n   208\t\n   209\t```python\n   210\t@dataclass(frozen=True)\n   211\tclass EvaluateDraft:\n   212\t    \"\"\"Validated evaluator-form input.\n   213\t\n   214\t    The route handler combines this with the server-owned\n   215\t    ``trial_id`` (from ``_CLAIMS``) to construct the\n   216\t    ``EvaluateSubmission`` object.\n   217\t    \"\"\"\n   218\t\n   219\t    status: Literal[\"success\", \"error\", \"eval_error\"]\n   220\t    metrics: dict[str, int | float | str]   # only present-and-valid keys\n   221\t    artifacts_uri: str | None\n   222\t    description: str | None\n   223\t\n   224\t\n   225\tdef parse_evaluate_form(\n   226\t    *,\n   227\t    metrics_schema: MetricsSchema,\n   228\t    status_raw: str,\n   229\t    metric_inputs: Mapping[str, str],   # key -> raw form value, one per schema entry\n   230\t    artifacts_uri_raw: str,\n   231\t    description_raw: str,\n   232\t) -> tuple[EvaluateDraft | None, FormErrors]:\n   233\t    ...\n   234\t```\n   235\t\n   236\tValidation rules:\n   237\t\n   238\t- `status` must be one of `{\"success\", \"error\", \"eval_error\"}`;\n   239\t  anything else → `errors.add(0, \"status\", …)` and `(None, errors)`.\n   240\t- Each `metric_inputs[name]`:\n   241\t  - empty/whitespace → omitted from the result dict (no error).\n   242\t  - present + non-empty:\n   243\t    - `metrics_schema.root[name] == \"integer\"`: parse with `int(s)`,\n   244\t      reject explicit `bool` (Python `bool` is `int`; the form-side\n   245\t      input is always a string so this isn't reachable, but the\n   246\t      parser checks `s.lower() not in (\"true\", \"false\")` to reject\n   247\t      a hand-crafted POST that puts `True` in an integer field).\n   248\t    - `\"real\"`: parse with `float(s)`, reject `nan`/`inf`/`-inf`\n   249\t      via `math.isfinite` (mirroring `Store._validate_metrics`).\n   250\t    - `\"text\"`: keep as `s.strip()` (non-empty after strip required;\n   251\t      an all-whitespace input is treated as omitted).\n   252\t  - any unknown key in `metric_inputs` outside `metrics_schema.root`\n   253\t    → `errors.add(0, name, \"metric not in schema\")`. (Note: the\n   254\t    template only emits inputs for schema keys, so this only\n   255\t    triggers on a hand-crafted POST.)\n   256\t- `status == \"success\"` requires `len(metrics) >= 1`; otherwise\n   257\t  `errors.add_overall(\"status=success requires at least one\n   258\t  metric value\")`.\n   259\t- `artifacts_uri_raw.strip()` → `None` if empty. No URI shape check\n   260\t  at form time (the spec doesn't constrain it); the operator is\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

The retry policy is identical to\n   278\t   chunks 1 / 9c:\n   279\t   - 3 attempts total, backoff `(0.05, 0.2, 0.5)`.\n   280\t   - `WrongToken` / `IllegalTransition` /\n   281\t     `ConflictingResubmission` / `InvalidPrecondition` short-\n   282\t     circuit (definitive store-domain errors; not retried).\n   283\t   - Transport-shaped exceptions (`Exception` not in the four\n   284\t     above) are retried.\n   285\t3. **Read-back on retry exhaustion.** `store.read_task(task_id)` +\n   286\t   `store.read_submission(task_id)` + `submissions_equivalent`.\n   287\t   Decisions:\n   288\t   - `task.state == \"claimed\"` and `task.claim.token == our token`\n   289\t     → orphan with `recovery_kind=\"auto\"` (\"transport failure;\n   290\t     retry safe — claim still ours, sweeper will reclaim eventually\").\n   291\t   - `task.state == \"claimed\"` but our token is gone → orphan with\n   292\t     `recovery_kind=\"auto\"` (`eden://error/wrong-token`) — same\n   293\t     prose; the prior reclaim already invalidated us.\n   294\t   - `task.state in {\"submitted\", \"completed\", \"failed\"}`:\n   295\t     - `read_submission` returns `None` → render orphan with\n   296\t       `recovery_kind=\"transport\"` (\"store invariant violation\").\n   297\t       Implementation-illegal but defensively handled.\n   298\t     - `submissions_equivalent(prior, our)` → success page.\n   299\t       (Note: `EvaluateSubmission` equivalence keys off\n   300\t       `status + trial_id + metrics`; `artifacts_uri` is **not**\n   301\t       part of equivalence per chapter 04 §4.2 + the\n   302\t       `submissions_equivalent` impl. The first submission's\n   303\t       `artifacts_uri` wins.)\n   304\t     - non-equivalent → orphan with `recovery_kind=\"conflict\"`\n   305\t       (`eden://error/conflicting-resubmission`).\n   306\t   - `task.state == \"pending\"` → orphan with\n   307\t     `recovery_kind=\"auto\"` (\"task reclaimed before our submit\n   308\t     committed; retry safe by re-claiming the task\").\n   309\t   - `read_task` itself raises → orphan with\n   310\t     `recovery_kind=\"transport\"` (read-back probe failure).\n   311\t\n   312\tThe single-step structure means there's no Phase-1-failed /\n   313\tPhase-2-failed split. The submit either succeeds, definitively\n   314\tfails (rendered as a form error or orphan), or transport-fails\n   315\tinto the read-back arm.\n   316\t\n   317\t### §C-recovery (per-error)\n   318\t\n   319\t| Outcome | Definition | UI rendering | Operator action |\n   320\t|---|---|---|---|\n   321\t| ok | submit returned cleanly OR retry returned cleanly OR read-back found equivalent prior submission | `evaluator_submitted.html`, 200 | none |\n   322\t| InvalidPrecondition (form-time, e.g. metric type drift only the server caught) | server rejected the metrics shape | re-render draft with the wire-error banner attached | fix the metrics and resubmit |\n   323\t| WrongToken | the prior reclaim invalidated our claim (sweeper or operator) | orphan, `recovery_kind=\"auto\"` | none — prior reclaim already restored task to `pending` |\n   324\t| IllegalTransition | task was reclaimed/terminalized before our submit landed | orphan, `recovery_kind=\"auto\"` | none |\n   325\t| ConflictingResubmission | a different submission won the race for this task | orphan, `recovery_kind=\"conflict\"` | operator triage; the system's record stands |\n   326\t| transport, retry exhausted, claim still ours | network glitch persisted; claim TTL will eventually reclaim | orphan, `recovery_kind=\"auto\"` | wait for TTL, or operator-side reclaim |\n   327\t| transport, retry exhausted, server committed equivalent | \"submitted, response lost\" sub-case A | success page (treated as `ok`) | none |\n   328\t| transport, retry exhausted, server committed non-equivalent | \"different submission won\" sub-case A' | orphan, `recovery_kind=\"conflict\"` | operator triage |\n   329\t| transport, retry exhausted, store invariant violated | implementation-illegal: terminal task with no recorded submission | orphan, `recovery_kind=\"transport\"` | operator investigation |\n   330\t| transport, retry exhausted, read-back probe itself failed | indeterminate | orphan, `recovery_kind=\"transport\"` | operator investigation |\n   331\t\n   332\tKey contrast with chunk 9c: there is **no orphaned `starting`\n   333\ttrial** to worry about. The evaluator never creates a trial; the\n   334\ttrial already existed when the evaluate task was created. So the\n   335\t\"sweeper composite-commits the trial to error on reclaim\" recovery\n   336\tbranch from 9c does not apply here. On reclaim of an evaluate task\n   337\twith an orphaned submit, `_base.reclaim` reverts the task from\n   338\t`claimed → pending`; the trial stays `starting` and a fresh\n   339\tevaluate task can be created (per §4.4 retry semantics). docs/plans/review/eden-phase-7a-git-wrapper/impl/20260423T172233/0.jsonl:5:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_1","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' /Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/AGENTS.md\"","aggregated_output":"# AGENTS.md\n\nThis file provides guidance to AI agents working with this repository.\n\n## What this repo is\n\nEDEN is a **protocol** for directed-code-evolution orchestration, not a\nsingle product. The repo contains three distinct kinds of artifact, each\nwith different change discipline:\n\n| Layer | Path | Authoritative? | Change discipline |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| Protocol specification | [`spec/`](spec/) | Yes — the source of truth | RFC-style: versioned, normative (MUST/SHOULD/MAY), carefully reviewed |\n| Reference implementation | [`reference/`](reference/) | No — *one* valid impl | Normal code-review discipline |\n| Conformance suite | [`conformance/`](conformance/) | Normative for tests | Black-box; must be implementation-agnostic |\n\nNon-normative human docs live in [`docs/`](docs/).\n\n## Current phase\n\n**Phase 6 complete.** The `eden-storage` package ships the `Store`\nstructural interface for the task store, event log, and\nproposal/trial persistence sides of chapter 8 — collapsed into a\nsingle Protocol per §7 implementation latitude. Two conforming\nbackends satisfy it: `InMemoryStore` (fast, non-durable; moved from\n`eden-dispatch`) and `SqliteStore` (durable across restarts via a\nWAL-mode SQLite database with `synchronous=FULL`, matching §3.1's\ncrash-survival requirement). The Protocol covers the spec-literal\n`create_task` / `replay` / `read_range` operations alongside the\ntyped convenience helpers. Both backends share the transition logic\nin `_base.py`, and the same conformance scenarios are parametrized\nacross both — drift from the Protocol surfaces in tests, not in\nproduction. Restart-safety tests close and reopen a SQLite store\nmid-experiment to confirm state, event log, and claim tokens\nsurvive, and a monkey-patched `_apply_commit` failure verifies\nrollback. The artifact store (§5), `subscribe` streaming (§2.1),\nand Postgres remain non-goals for Phase 6; they land in Phase 10 /\nPhase 8 / later respectively.\n\n**Phase 7a complete.** The `eden-git` package ships the subprocess\nwrapper the Phase 7b integrator composes into §3.2 squash commits.\n`GitRepo` covers ref/object inspection (`rev_parse`, `resolve_ref`,\n`list_refs`, `is_ancestor`, `ls_tree`), plumbing (`write_blob`,\n`write_tree_from_entries`, `write_tree_with_file`, `commit_tree`,\n`create_ref`, `update_ref`), worktree management, and branch management.\nAuthor identity and `commit.gpgsign=false` are pinned per-invocation so\nthe user's ambient git config never leaks into integrator commits.\nTests (36, all green against a real `git` subprocess on `tmp_path`)\ncover every primitive. Phase 7b — the integrator flow — lands next:\nobserving `trial.succeeded`, assembling the trial/* commit per §3.2,\nand writing ref + `trial_commit_sha` + `trial.integrated` atomically\nper §3.4. See [`docs/roadmap.md`](docs/roadmap.md) for the full\n13-phase plan.\n\n## Commands\n\nAt Phase 7a, markdown linting, JSON Schema validation, and the Python\ntoolchain for the `eden-contracts`, `eden-dispatch`, `eden-git`, and\n`eden-storage` reference packages are wired up.\n\n| Command | Purpose |\n|---|---|\n| `npx --yes markdownlint-cli2@0.14.0 \"**/*.md\" \"#node_modules\" \"#.venv\" \"#docs/archive/**\" \"#docs/plans/review/**\"` | Lint all tracked markdown (pinned to CI's version; matches CI exactly) |\n| `pipx run 'check-jsonschema==0.29.4' --check-metaschema spec/v0/schemas/*.schema.json` | Validate each schema file against the Draft 2020-12 meta-schema (version pinned to CI) |\n| `pipx run 'check-jsonschema==0.29.4' --schemafile spec/v0/schemas/experiment-config.schema.json tests/fixtures/experiment/.eden/config.yaml` | Validate the fixture experiment config against its schema |\n| `uv sync` | Install/refresh the workspace virtualenv (root + `reference/packages/eden-contracts` + `reference/packages/eden-dispatch` + `reference/packages/eden-git` + `reference/packages/eden-storage`) |\n| `uv run ruff check .` | Lint Python (config in root `pyproject.toml`) |\n| `uv run pyright` | Type-check the reference Python packages |\n| `uv run pytest -q` | Run the reference-package test suite (includes schema ↔ model parity) |\n| `uv run pytest reference/packages/eden-contracts/tests/test_schema_parity.py` | Run only the schema ↔ Pydantic model parity check |\n| `python3 scripts/spec-xref-check.py` | Validate every `§N.M` reference in `spec/v0/*.md` resolves to a real section heading in its target chapter. Run before committing a normative spec change. |\n\n### Commands that will exist in later phases\n\nThese are listed for orientation; the tooling is not wired up yet.\n\n| Command | Lands in |\n|---|---|\n| `docker compose up` end-to-end | Phase 10 |\n\n## Contribution conventions\n\n### Spec edits\n\n+ Every cross-component contract (wire format, state machine, invariant)\n  is defined in `spec/` first, then implemented in `reference/`. When\n  they disagree, the spec wins and the impl gets a bug.\n+ Spec chapters use RFC 2119 language: MUST, SHOULD, MAY.\n+ Spec versions are frozen once stable (`spec/v0/` is a single lineage;\n  breaking changes go to `spec/v1/`).\n+ Schema changes must update the spec prose, the JSON Schema file, and\n  (from Phase 3 onward) the Pydantic bindings in lockstep. CI will\n  enforce schema ↔ model parity once both exist.\n\n### Reference implementation\n\n+ Normal code-review discipline.\n+ Normative behavior is tested against the conformance suite (once it\n  exists in Phase 11).\n+ The reference impl is explicitly not a monopoly on correctness. A\n  third-party implementation that passes conformance is equally valid.\n\n### Adding or extending a JSON Schema + Pydantic binding\n\nThe `schema-parity` CI job is only as strong as what both sides of the\ntest actually enforce. Several Pydantic and `jsonschema` defaults let\ndrift through silently. When adding a new schema — or a new field type\nto an existing one — evaluate each of the following; reusable\nimplementations live in\n[`reference/packages/eden-contracts/src/eden_contracts/_common.py`](reference/packages/eden-contracts/src/eden_contracts/_common.py).\n\n+ **Strict numeric parsing.** Top-level models set\n  `ConfigDict(strict=True, extra=\"allow\")`. Non-strict mode coerces\n  `True`/`\"2\"` into int, but the schemas treat `type: integer` /\n  `type: number` as strict JSON types. New models must keep\n  `strict=True`.\n+ **Format assertions on both sides.** `format` keywords (`uri`,\n  `date-time`, …) are advisory by default in both the `jsonschema`\n  library and Pydantic. The schema-side validator wires a custom\n  `FormatChecker` in\n  [`tests/conftest.py`](reference/packages/eden-contracts/tests/conftest.py),\n  and the model side uses the reusable types in `_common.py`. A new\n  `format` keyword in any schema requires handlers on *both* sides;\n  the `test_format_coverage` test fails loudly if the schema-side\n  handler is missing.\n+ **Real date-time / URI validation, not just regex or `urlparse`.**\n  The regex on `DateTimeStr` accepts impossible values like\n  `2026-99-99T…Z`; an `AfterValidator` runs `datetime.fromisoformat`\n  to reject those. `UriStr` uses `rfc3986-validator`, not\n  `urllib.parse.urlparse`, which accepts malformed schemeful URIs\n  (e.g., spaces in the host).\n+ **Null vs absent.** JSON Schema's `type: X` rejects explicit `null`,\n  but Pydantic's `X | None = None` accepts it. Wrap every optional\n  typed field with `NotNone` from `_common.py` so absent is accepted\n  and explicit null is rejected.\n+ **Round-trip emission.** `model.model_dump(mode=\"json\",\n  exclude_none=True)` must re-validate against the schema. Add any\n  new model to `tests/test_roundtrip.py` so this is checked.\n+ **Corpus coverage.** Parity is asserted over the fixture corpus in\n  `tests/cases.py`. A new field type deserves at least one accept\n  fixture and one reject fixture per constraint the schema imposes\n  (required, pattern, enum, min/max, format, cross-field).\n\n## Commit guidelines\n\n+ Short imperative subjects (e.g., \"Add event protocol chapter\",\n  \"Pin task state machine\").\n+ For pull requests, include: what the change is, which phase it\n  advances, and any spec ↔ impl implications.\n\n## Related docs\n\n+ [`docs/naming.md`](docs/naming.md) — what EDEN is, at the concept level.\n+ [`docs/roadmap.md`](docs/roadmap.md) — the 13-phase build-up plan with\n  unit-level decomposition.\n+ [`docs/plans/`](docs/plans/) — active implementation plans.\n+ [`docs/archive/`](docs/archive/) — historical reference docs.\n+ [`STYLE_GUIDE.md`](STYLE_GUIDE.md) — formatting and naming conventions.\n+ [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](CONTRIBUTING.md) — setup and PR workflow.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
docs/plans/review/eden-phase-7a-git-wrapper/impl/20260423T172233/0.jsonl:9:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_3","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' /Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/spec/v0/06-integrator.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Integrator\n\nThis chapter specifies the integrator role in full: the git topology\nit operates on, the promotion trigger, the squash rule, the\neval-manifest shape, and the invariants it preserves across the\ncanonical trial lineage.\n\nThe integrator is introduced in [`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §5,\nwhich pins its boundary rules — exclusive authority over `trial/*`,\nexclusive authority over `trial_commit_sha`. This chapter specifies\nwhat the integrator *produces* inside those boundaries.\n\nThe three-namespace topology (`main`, `trial/*`, `work/*`) is defined\nin [`01-concepts.md`](01-concepts.md) §9. This chapter pins the\ninvariants on each namespace and the transformation from worker\nbranch to canonical trial commit.\n\n## 1. Topology invariants\n\nA conforming EDEN deployment operates on a single git repository per\nexperiment. That repository carries three ref namespaces:\n\n### 1.1 `main`\n\n- Names the experiment's starting commit.\n- MUST be present at experiment registration time and MUST name a\n  commit reachable by every proposal's `parent_commits`\n  ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §5.2).\n- Is **immutable** for the duration of the experiment. A conforming\n  integrator MUST NOT move, rewrite, or delete `main`; neither MAY\n  any worker. External tooling (e.g. repo maintainers merging work\n  into `main` outside the experiment) is the operator's concern and\n  lies outside the protocol. An experiment MAY tolerate such\n  external moves only if it does not depend on `main` as a\n  persistent parent; conforming implementations SHOULD record the\n  experiment's starting commit SHA explicitly and use that SHA, not\n  the current `main`, when validating parent reachability.\n\n### 1.2 `trial/*`\n\n- Holds the canonical trial lineage, one commit per integrated trial.\n- The integrator is the **sole writer** ([`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md)\n  §5.1). No other role — planner, implementer, evaluator,\n  orchestrator, operator tooling invoked through protocol-owned\n  channels — MAY write to `trial/*`.\n- Each `trial/*` branch MUST name exactly one trial. Branch naming\n  conforms to §3.1.\n- A `trial/*` branch MUST NOT be deleted or rewritten once written.\n  If an integrator discovers a bug in an earlier promotion, it MAY\n  record corrective state in subsequent events or operator-level\n  channels, but MUST NOT rewrite history on `trial/*`.\n\n### 1.3 `work/*`\n\n- Holds per-trial worker branches written by implementers\n  ([`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §3.3).\n- Each `work/*` branch MUST be unique to a single trial. A trial's\n  `branch` field ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1)\n  records the exact branch name.\n- Worker branches are **inputs** to the integrator, not normative\n  outputs. A conforming deployment MAY retain them for audit and\n  debugging after promotion, MAY garbage-collect them after a\n  retention window, and MAY delete them eagerly once a trial is\n  integrated — the retention policy is a deployment concern. No\n  protocol-owned reader MAY depend on a worker branch after the\n  trial's promotion.\n- The evaluator reads a worker branch at the trial's `commit_sha`\n  during evaluation ([`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §4.1); the\n  implementer's read-your-writes guarantee applies only until\n  promotion, after which the worker branch's reachability is no\n  longer a protocol-owned concern.\n\n### 1.4 Reachability rule\n\nEvery commit on a `work/*` branch MUST descend from the proposal's\ndeclared `parent_commits` ([`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §3.3). The\nintegrator MUST reject a trial whose `commit_sha` does not satisfy\nthis rule; rejection produces no `trial/*` commit and no\n`trial.integrated` event. The orchestrator MAY transition the trial\nto `error` via the normal channels if a reachability violation is\ndiscovered, but the integrator MUST NOT fabricate a `trial.errored`\nevent itself — events are paired with state changes, and the\nintegrator is not the writer for trial `status`.\n\n## 2. Promotion trigger\n\nThe integrator promotes a trial iff:\n\n- The trial's `status` is `success`\n  ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1).\n- The trial's `commit_sha` is set and resolves to a commit on the\n  trial's `branch` in the experiment repository.\n- The trial has not yet been promoted (`trial_commit_sha` is absent).\n\nThe mechanism by which the integrator observes the trigger —\nsubscribing to `trial.succeeded` events, polling the trial store, or\nreceiving a dispatch call — is a binding concern and is not pinned.\n[`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §5.2.\n\nA conforming integrator MUST NOT promote trials in any other status.\nIn particular, `error`, `eval_error`, and `starting` trials MUST NOT\nreceive a `trial/*` commit.\n\nThe integrator MUST NOT promote a trial whose `metrics` do not\nvalidate against the experiment's `metrics_schema`\n([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.2,\n[`08-storage.md`](08-storage.md) §4). The orchestrator's acceptance\nof a `success` submission is the primary guard for this; the\nintegrator MAY additionally re-validate as defense in depth but MUST\nNOT silently drop or coerce invalid metrics.\n\n## 3. Promotion output\n\n### 3.1 `trial/*` branch name\n\nThe integrator creates a branch under `trial/` named:\n\n```text\ntrial/<trial_id>-<slug>\n```\n\nwhere `<trial_id>` is the trial's `trial_id`\n([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1) and `<slug>` is the\n`slug` of the trial's parent proposal\n([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §5.1). The slug is already\nconstrained to `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$`, and `trial_id` is opaque; the\nresulting branch name MUST be valid under git's ref-format rules\n([git check-ref-format]). If an implementation's `trial_id` choice\ncould violate ref-format rules, the implementation MUST choose a\ndifferent `trial_id` representation — it MUST NOT mutate the stored\n`trial_id` in the trial object to accommodate its branch-naming\nscheme.\n\n[git check-ref-format]: https://git-scm.com/docs/git-check-ref-format\n\n### 3.2 Squash rule\n\nThe trial's worker branch MAY contain multiple commits (implementer\nstep-by-step work, partial progress, iterated attempts). The\ncanonical trial commit MUST be a **single commit** whose tree is the\nworker branch's tip tree plus exactly the eval-manifest file.\n\nConcretely: the integrator MUST produce a commit `T` with:\n\n- `tree(T)` equal to `tree(commit_sha)` with exactly one path added:\n  the eval-manifest file at the path given in §4.1. The added file's\n  bytes MUST be the manifest defined by §4.2. `commit_sha` is the\n  trial's recorded worker-branch tip. No other path — not a file,\n  not a directory entry — MAY be added, removed, or modified in the\n  squash.\n- `parents(T) == <parent_commits>` of the trial, in the order\n  recorded on the trial\n  ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1 inherits from the\n  proposal, §5.1).\n- A commit message whose first line (subject) records the trial\n  identity in a machine-parseable form. §3.3 pins the format.\n\nThe implementer is not required to carry the eval manifest in its\nworker branch; the manifest is exclusively the integrator's write.\nIf a worker branch incidentally contains a file at the eval-manifest\npath (e.g. an implementer mistake), the integrator MUST reject the\ntrial rather than silently overwrite the file. Rejection produces\nno `trial/*` commit and no `trial.integrated` event; the orchestrator\nMAY transition the trial via normal channels.\n\nThe squash is a single merge-commit-shaped object (parents may be\none or many, per the proposal's `parent_commits`); intermediate\nwork-branch commits MUST NOT appear in `T`'s ancestor chain along a\nfirst-parent path when read from `trial/*`. Readers of the\ncanonical lineage see one commit per trial, not the worker's\nintermediate history.\n\nA conforming implementation MAY additionally preserve the worker\nbranch's history elsewhere (retained `work/*` ref, archive) for\naudit, but that preservation MUST NOT make intermediate commits\nreachable from `trial/*`.\n\n### 3.3 Commit message\n\nThe `trial/*` commit message's first line (subject) MUST match:\n\n```text\ntrial: <trial_id> <slug>\n```\n\nThe subject MUST carry both the `trial_id` and the parent proposal's\n`slug`, separated by a single space. The body MAY include additional\nhuman-readable context. The machine-parseable header allows a\nsubscriber reading git alone — without the event log — to recover the\n`trial_id` of any `trial/*` commit.\n\n### 3.4 Atomic write\n\nThe promotion MUST be atomic with two other state changes:\n\n1. Writing the `trial_commit_sha` field on the trial to the SHA of\n   the new `trial/*` commit\n   ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1).\n2. Appending a `trial.integrated` event to the event log\n   ([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §3.3).\n\nIf any of the three steps fails — git ref write, trial-object\nfield write, event append — the integrator MUST roll back any\nalready-performed step. In particular, a dangling `trial/*` ref with\nno `trial_commit_sha` field and no `trial.integrated` event is a\nprotocol violation. Implementations MAY use store-level\ntransactions, outbox patterns, compensating deletes, or any other\nmechanism; the observable invariant is that a reader of any one of\nthe three artifacts (ref, field, event) MUST observe the other two.\n\n## 4. Eval manifest\n\nEach `trial/*` commit MUST carry an **eval manifest** at a fixed path\ninside its tree. The manifest is the canonical, git-embedded record\nof the trial's evaluator outputs: what was measured, which worker\ncommit was measured, where the supporting artifacts live.\n\n### 4.1 Path\n\nThe eval manifest is a JSON file at:\n\n```text\n.eden/trials/<trial_id>/eval.json\n```\n\nrelative to the root of the committed tree. The `<trial_id>`\ncomponent matches the trial's `trial_id`. An integrator MUST NOT add\nany file under `.eden/trials/<other_trial_id>/` to a trial's commit;\neach `trial/*` commit carries exactly one eval-manifest path,\ncorresponding to its own trial.\n\n### 4.2 Shape\n\nThe manifest is a JSON object with the following required fields.\nEach required field is taken from state the protocol already writes\nvia the role contracts in [`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) — no role\nsubmission is extended by this chapter.\n\n| Field | Type | Source |\n|---|---|---|\n| `trial_id` | string | The trial's `trial_id` ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1). |\n| `proposal_id` | string | The trial's `proposal_id` ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1). |\n| `commit_sha` | string | The worker-branch tip the evaluator measured ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1). |\n| `parent_commits` | array of string | The trial's `parent_commits`, in order ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1). |\n| `metrics` | object | The evaluator's metrics payload ([`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §4.4), conforming to the experiment's `metrics_schema`. |\n| `completed_at` | timestamp | The trial's `completed_at` ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1). UTC, RFC 3339 profile as elsewhere in the data model. |\n\nOptional fields:\n\n| Field | Type | Source |\n|---|---|---|\n| `artifacts_uri` | string (URI) | The trial's `artifacts_uri` if the evaluator supplied one ([`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §4.4). |\n| `description` | string | The trial's `description`, copied from the trial object if present. |\n| `evaluator` | object | Implementation-defined identity of the evaluator (§4.3). Present when the evaluator's role-binding surfaces this metadata. |\n| `artifacts` | object | Implementation-defined inventory of per-file artifact metadata (§4.4). Present when the role-binding surfaces it. |\n\nEvery required field's value MUST equal the corresponding field on\nthe trial object at the moment of promotion. A conforming integrator\nMUST NOT synthesize or transform required-field values; they are a\ncanonical, in-tree snapshot of the trial state, not a separate\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
docs/plans/review/eden-phase-7a-git-wrapper/impl/20260423T172233/0.jsonl:11:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_4","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' /Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/review/eden-phase-7a-git-wrapper/impl/20260423T172233/0.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Implementation Review: eden-phase-7a-git-wrapper\n\n## Plan\n\n- Path: `docs/plans/eden-phase-7a-git-wrapper.md`\n- Summary: Port direvo's git subprocess wrapper into a new `eden-git`\n  workspace package. Provides the primitives (ref/object inspection,\n  plumbing, worktree/branch management) that the Phase 7b integrator\n  will compose into §3.2 squash commits. No integrator policy lives\n  here — 7a is plumbing only.\n\n## Implementation files\n\n| File | Action | Plan section |\n|---|---|---|\n| `reference/packages/eden-git/pyproject.toml` | Created | \"Files to create\" |\n| `reference/packages/eden-git/README.md` | Created | \"Files to create\" |\n| `reference/packages/eden-git/src/eden_git/__init__.py` | Created | \"`GitRepo` surface\" (re-exports) |\n| `reference/packages/eden-git/src/eden_git/errors.py` | Created | \"Key design decisions\" #7 (typed errors) |\n| `reference/packages/eden-git/src/eden_git/repo.py` | Created | \"`GitRepo` surface\" + \"Key design decisions\" |\n| `reference/packages/eden-git/tests/conftest.py` | Created | \"Tests\" (fixtures) |\n| `reference/packages/eden-git/tests/test_repo_plumbing.py` | Created | \"Tests\" |\n| `reference/packages/eden-git/tests/test_repo_branches_worktrees.py` | Created | \"Tests\" |\n| `pyproject.toml` | Modified | \"Files to modify\" (workspace wiring) |\n| `AGENTS.md` (symlinked as `CLAUDE.md`) | Modified | \"Files to modify\" (phase note + `uv sync`) |\n| `docs/roadmap.md` | Modified | \"Files to modify\" (mark 7a complete) |\n\n## Change summary\n\n- New `eden-git` workspace package scaffolded under\n  `reference/packages/eden-git/` and wired into the uv workspace,\n  pyright include, pytest testpaths, and dev dependency group.\n- `GitRepo` class wraps a repository path with roughly 25 methods\n  split across factories, introspection, plumbing, and branch /\n  worktree management. All operations shell out to the local `git`\n  binary via `subprocess.run`; failures raise `GitError` carrying\n  the full argv plus captured streams.\n- Per-invocation hardening: `-c safe.directory=<repo>` (no wildcard),\n  `-c commit.gpgsign=false` (prevents ambient signing config from\n  breaking integrator commits), and `commit_tree` sets\n  `GIT_AUTHOR_*` / `GIT_COMMITTER_*` env vars explicitly so\n  `user.name` / `user.email` from the user's global config is never\n  consulted.\n- `write_tree_with_file` — the main non-trivial primitive — builds a\n  new tree equal to `base_tree_sha` with exactly one file added,\n  using an isolated `GIT_INDEX_FILE` in a tempdir so no index state\n  escapes. Rejects if the target path already exists in the base\n  tree (the §3.2 \"worker branch MUST NOT contain the eval-manifest\n  path\" guard that 7b will invoke).\n- `create_ref` calls `update-ref name sha <zero>` to atomically\n  create a ref that must not currently exist, supporting the §1.2\n  `trial/*` immutability invariant.\n- 36 tests run against a real `git` subprocess on a `tmp_path` repo,\n  covering every public method and the two explicit reject paths\n  (pre-existing path in `write_tree_with_file`, pre-existing ref in\n  `create_ref`).\n\n## Focus areas for this review\n\n1. **Spec-literal audit.** The contract-bearing methods\n   (`write_tree_with_file`, `create_ref`, `update_ref`,\n   `commit_tree`) will be invoked by 7b to satisfy §3.2 / §3.4. Are\n   they shaped such that a 7b implementation could build the squash\n   and publish it atomically without reaching for private state?\n   Specifically: is there anything §3.2 demands that 7a can't express\n   today?\n2. **Environment isolation.** `commit.gpgsign=false`, explicit\n   identity env vars, `safe.directory=<path>`. Is there any other\n   ambient git config or env var that could leak into integrator\n   behavior (e.g., `core.autocrlf`, `core.hooksPath`,\n   `init.templateDir`, `GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL`, `GIT_SSH_COMMAND`)?\n3. **`GIT_INDEX_FILE` scoping.** `write_tree_with_file` isolates the\n   index via a tempdir. Is there any case where a `git read-tree` /\n   `update-index` / `write-tree` sequence would still mutate repo\n   state or leak state to other concurrent invocations on the same\n   repo?\n4. **Zero-OID hard-coding.** `_ZERO_SHA = \"0\" * 40` assumes SHA-1.\n   The package README notes this as a known limitation. Is there a\n   cheap way to make this hash-algo-aware now (e.g., query\n   `extensions.objectFormat`) or is deferring to Phase 13 fine?\n5. **Test coverage gaps.** 36 tests run green; are there obvious\n   edge cases that aren't covered yet and would bite 7b hard?\n   Examples that come to mind: a worker branch with a directory\n   entry (tree, not blob) at the eval-manifest path; a symlink\n   (`120000`) in the base tree; `update-ref` CAS against a ref that\n   simultaneously moved; orphan / unborn-HEAD repos.\n\n## Review status\n\nRound 0 — awaiting initial review.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
docs/plans/review/eden-phase-7a-git-wrapper/impl/20260423T172233/0.jsonl:20:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_9","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' /Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/packages/eden-git/README.md\"","aggregated_output":"# eden-git\n\nReference git primitives for the EDEN integrator role (spec/v0 chapter\n6). Wraps the `git` CLI as subprocesses and exposes the porcelain and\nplumbing operations the reference integrator needs to shape `trial/*`\ncommits.\n\nThis package is a thin subprocess wrapper around the local `git` binary\n— the conformance contract is defined in\n[`spec/v0/06-integrator.md`](../../../spec/v0/06-integrator.md), not\nhere. A conforming integrator that talks to Gitea, GitHub, or an\nin-memory fake is equally valid; this package is simply the local-repo\nreference.\n\nCurrent surface (Phase 7a):\n\n- `GitRepo` — opens an existing repository path.\n- Ref and object inspection: `rev_parse`, `commit_exists`,\n  `resolve_ref`, `ref_exists`, `list_refs`, `is_ancestor`, `ls_tree`.\n- Worktree management: `add_worktree`, `remove_worktree`, `prune_worktrees`,\n  `list_worktrees`.\n- Branch management: `create_branch`, `delete_branch`, `current_branch`.\n- Plumbing for integrator squashing (Phase 7b): `write_blob`,\n  `write_tree`, `commit_tree`, `create_ref`, `update_ref`.\n\nThe integrator flow itself — observing `trial.succeeded`, shaping the\nsquashed commit per §3.2, atomically writing ref + `trial_commit_sha` +\n`trial.integrated` per §3.4 — lands in Phase 7b.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
docs/plans/review/eden-phase-7a-git-wrapper/impl/20260423T172233/0.jsonl:29:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_13","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' /Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/packages/eden-git/tests/conftest.py\"","aggregated_output":"\"\"\"Shared fixtures for eden-git tests.\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nimport pytest\nfrom eden_git import GitRepo, Identity, TreeEntry\n\nTEST_AUTHOR = Identity(name=\"EDEN Test\", email=\"test@eden.example\")\nFIXED_DATE = \"2026-04-23T00:00:00+00:00\"\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef test_author() -> Identity:\n    \"\"\"Default identity stamped on test commits.\"\"\"\n    return TEST_AUTHOR\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef fixed_date() -> str:\n    \"\"\"Fixed RFC 3339 timestamp for deterministic commit SHAs.\"\"\"\n    return FIXED_DATE\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef bare_repo(tmp_path: Path) -> GitRepo:\n    \"\"\"A freshly initialized bare repository.\"\"\"\n    return GitRepo.init_bare(tmp_path / \"repo.git\")\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef repo_with_main(tmp_path: Path) -> tuple[GitRepo, str]:\n    \"\"\"A bare repository with a ``main`` branch pointing at a seed commit.\n\n    Returns the repo and the seed commit's SHA. The seed tree contains\n    a single file, ``README``, with content ``b\"seed\\\\n\"``.\n    \"\"\"\n    repo = GitRepo.init_bare(tmp_path / \"repo.git\")\n    blob = repo.write_blob(b\"seed\\n\")\n    seed_tree = repo.write_tree_from_entries(\n        [TreeEntry(mode=\"100644\", type=\"blob\", sha=blob, path=\"README\")]\n    )\n    seed = repo.commit_tree(\n        seed_tree,\n        parents=[],\n        message=\"seed\",\n        author=TEST_AUTHOR,\n        author_date=FIXED_DATE,\n        committer_date=FIXED_DATE,\n    )\n    repo.create_ref(\"refs/heads/main\", seed)\n    return repo, seed\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef non_bare_repo_with_main(tmp_path: Path) -> tuple[GitRepo, str]:\n    \"\"\"A non-bare repo with a seed commit on main (hosts worktrees).\"\"\"\n    repo = GitRepo.init(tmp_path / \"repo\")\n    blob = repo.write_blob(b\"seed\\n\")\n    tree = repo.write_tree_from_entries(\n        [TreeEntry(mode=\"100644\", type=\"blob\", sha=blob, path=\"README\")]\n    )\n    seed = repo.commit_tree(\n        tree,\n        parents=[],\n        message=\"seed\",\n        author=TEST_AUTHOR,\n        author_date=FIXED_DATE,\n        committer_date=FIXED_DATE,\n    )\n    repo.create_ref(\"refs/heads/main\", seed)\n    return repo, seed\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
docs/plans/review/eden-phase-7a-git-wrapper/impl/20260423T172233/0.jsonl:39:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_19","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' /Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/packages/eden-git/src/eden_git/repo.py\"","aggregated_output":"\"\"\"Subprocess wrapper around a local git repository.\n\n``GitRepo`` wraps a single repository directory (bare or non-bare) and\nexposes the porcelain and plumbing operations the reference integrator\nneeds (spec/v0 chapter 6). Every failed ``git`` invocation raises\n:class:`eden_git.errors.GitError` carrying the executed argv, exit\ncode, and captured streams.\n\nDesign points:\n\n* Each instance is path-scoped: ``GitRepo(repo_path)`` runs commands\n  under that directory. Worktrees attached to the same underlying\n  repository are addressed by their own ``GitRepo`` instance pointed at\n  the worktree path.\n* Identity stamping is explicit. ``commit_tree`` and ``create_commit``\n  take ``author`` / ``committer`` so the caller can pin them; the\n  wrapper does not read ``user.name`` / ``user.email`` from the\n  ambient git config.\n* Plumbing operations (``write_blob``, ``write_tree``, ``commit_tree``,\n  ``create_ref``, ``update_ref``) are the primitives the Phase 7b\n  integrator composes into the §3.2 squash. Worktree and branch ops\n  are for implementer and operator paths in later phases.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport os\nimport subprocess\nimport tempfile\nfrom collections.abc import Iterable, Mapping\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom .errors import GitError\n\n_ZERO_SHA = \"0\" * 40\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass TreeEntry:\n    \"\"\"One entry in a git tree object.\n\n    ``mode`` is the six-character git mode string (``100644``,\n    ``100755``, ``120000``, ``040000``, ``160000``). ``type`` is\n    ``\"blob\"``, ``\"tree\"``, or ``\"commit\"``. ``sha`` is the referenced\n    object's hex SHA. ``path`` is the entry name relative to the tree\n    (or the full path when ``ls_tree`` is called recursively).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    mode: str\n    type: str\n    sha: str\n    path: str\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass WorktreeInfo:\n    \"\"\"One entry from ``git worktree list --porcelain``.\"\"\"\n\n    path: Path\n    head: str | None\n    branch: str | None\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass Identity:\n    \"\"\"An author/committer identity stamped on a git commit.\"\"\"\n\n    name: str\n    email: str\n\n\nclass GitRepo:\n    \"\"\"A git repository on the local filesystem.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, path: Path | str) -> None:\n        self.path = Path(path).resolve()\n\n    # --- factories --------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @classmethod\n    def init_bare(cls, path: Path | str) -> GitRepo:\n        \"\"\"Initialize a new bare repository at ``path`` and return a repo.\"\"\"\n        target = Path(path)\n        target.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        _run_git([\"init\", \"--bare\", \"--initial-branch=main\", str(target)], cwd=target.parent)\n        return cls(target)\n\n    @classmethod\n    def init(cls, path: Path | str) -> GitRepo:\n        \"\"\"Initialize a new non-bare repository at ``path`` and return a repo.\"\"\"\n        target = Path(path)\n        target.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        _run_git([\"init\", \"--initial-branch=main\", str(target)], cwd=target.parent)\n        return cls(target)\n\n    # --- introspection ----------------------------------------------------\n\n    def is_bare(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return whether the repository is bare.\"\"\"\n        result = self._run([\"rev-parse\", \"--is-bare-repository\"])\n        return result.stdout.strip() == \"true\"\n\n    def rev_parse(self, ref_or_sha: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Resolve a ref or SHA prefix to a full 40-character SHA.\"\"\"\n        result = self._run([\"rev-parse\", \"--verify\", f\"{ref_or_sha}^{{commit}}\"])\n        return result.stdout.strip()\n\n    def commit_exists(self, sha: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return whether ``sha`` names a commit object in the repository.\"\"\"\n        result = self._run([\"cat-file\", \"-t\", sha], check=False)\n        return result.returncode == 0 and result.stdout.strip() == \"commit\"\n\n    def ref_exists(self, refname: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return whether a ref exists (e.g. ``refs/heads/trial/t1-slug``).\"\"\"\n        result = self._run([\"show-ref\", \"--verify\", \"--quiet\", refname], check=False)\n        return result.returncode == 0\n\n    def resolve_ref(self, refname: str) -> str | None:\n        \"\"\"Return the SHA a ref points to, or ``None`` if the ref is absent.\"\"\"\n        result = self._run([\"show-ref\", \"--verify\", refname], check=False)\n        if result.returncode != 0:\n            return None\n        line = result.stdout.strip().split()\n        return line[0] if line else None\n\n    def list_refs(self, pattern: str) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n        \"\"\"Return ``(refname, sha)`` pairs matching a ``for-each-ref`` pattern.\"\"\"\n        result = self._run(\n            [\"for-each-ref\", \"--format=%(refname) %(objectname)\", pattern],\n        )\n        pairs: list[tuple[str, str]] = []\n        for line in result.stdout.splitlines():\n            line = line.strip()\n            if not line:\n                continue\n            name, _, sha = line.partition(\" \")\n            pairs.append((name, sha))\n        return pairs\n\n    def is_ancestor(self, maybe_ancestor: str, descendant: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return whether ``maybe_ancestor`` is an ancestor of ``descendant``.\n\n        Wraps ``git merge-base --is-ancestor``. A commit is its own\n        ancestor (git's convention).\n        \"\"\"\n        result = self._run(\n            [\"merge-base\", \"--is-ancestor\", maybe_ancestor, descendant],\n            check=False,\n        )\n        if result.returncode == 0:\n            return True\n        if result.returncode == 1:\n            return False\n        raise GitError(result.args, result.returncode, result.stdout, result.stderr)\n\n    def commit_tree_sha(self, commit_sha: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Return the tree SHA recorded on a commit.\"\"\"\n        return self._run([\"rev-parse\", \"--verify\", f\"{commit_sha}^{{tree}}\"]).stdout.strip()\n\n    def commit_parents(self, commit_sha: str) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Return the list of parent SHAs of a commit, in order.\"\"\"\n        result = self._run([\"rev-list\", \"--parents\", \"-n\", \"1\", commit_sha])\n        tokens = result.stdout.strip().split()\n        return tokens[1:]  # first is the commit itself\n\n    def commit_message(self, commit_sha: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Return the full commit message of a commit.\"\"\"\n        return self._run([\"log\", \"-1\", \"--format=%B\", commit_sha]).stdout\n\n    def commit_message_subject(self, commit_sha: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Return the subject line of a commit.\"\"\"\n        return self._run([\"log\", \"-1\", \"--format=%s\", commit_sha]).stdout.strip()\n\n    def ls_tree(\n        self, tree_ish: str, path: str = \"\", *, recursive: bool = False\n    ) -> list[TreeEntry]:\n        \"\"\"List entries of a tree (or commit's tree).\n\n        With ``recursive=True`` the listing flattens sub-trees to blob\n        entries and returns full paths. Tree entries are omitted from\n        the recursive listing (matches ``git ls-tree -r`` behavior).\n        \"\"\"\n        args = [\"ls-tree\"]\n        if recursive:\n            args.append(\"-r\")\n        args.append(tree_ish)\n        if path:\n            args.append(path)\n        result = self._run(args)\n        entries: list[TreeEntry] = []\n        for line in result.stdout.splitlines():\n            # Format: \"<mode> <type> <sha>\\t<path>\"\n            if \"\\t\" not in line:\n                continue\n            head, _, entry_path = line.partition(\"\\t\")\n            mode, kind, sha = head.split()\n            entries.append(TreeEntry(mode=mode, type=kind, sha=sha, path=entry_path))\n        return entries\n\n    def tree_entry_exists(self, tree_ish: str, path: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return whether ``path`` exists anywhere in the tree.\"\"\"\n        result = self._run([\"ls-tree\", \"-r\", tree_ish, \"--\", path])\n        return bool(result.stdout.strip())\n\n    def read_blob(self, blob_sha: str) -> bytes:\n        \"\"\"Return the raw byte content of a blob.\"\"\"\n        result = subprocess.run(\n            self._git_argv([\"cat-file\", \"blob\", blob_sha]),\n            cwd=self.path,\n            capture_output=True,\n            check=False,\n            env=self._env(),\n        )\n        if result.returncode != 0:\n            raise GitError(\n                list(result.args),\n                result.returncode,\n                result.stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\"),\n                result.stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\"),\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

0:\n            raise GitError(list(result.args), result.returncode, result.stdout, result.stderr)\n        return result.stdout.strip()\n\n    def write_tree_from_entries(self, entries: Iterable[TreeEntry]) -> str:\n        r\"\"\"Create a tree containing exactly the given entries; return its SHA.\n\n        Uses ``git mktree`` which expects ``<mode> <type> <sha>\\t<path>``\n        lines on stdin. ``entries`` is iterated once.\n        \"\"\"\n        lines = \"\".join(f\"{e.mode} {e.type} {e.sha}\\t{e.path}\\n\" for e in entries)\n        result = subprocess.run(\n            self._git_argv([\"mktree\"]),\n            cwd=self.path,\n            input=lines,\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True,\n            check=False,\n            env=self._env(),\n        )\n        if result.returncode != 0:\n            raise GitError(list(result.args), result.returncode, result.stdout, result.stderr)\n        return result.stdout.strip()\n\n    def write_tree_with_file(\n        self,\n        base_tree_sha: str,\n        file_path: str,\n        blob_sha: str,\n        *,\n        mode: str = \"100644\",\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Return the SHA of a new tree = ``base_tree_sha`` plus one file added.\n\n        Uses an isolated index file (``GIT_INDEX_FILE``) so the\n        operation never touches the repo's main index or any worktree.\n        Raises :class:`GitError` if ``file_path`` already exists in\n        the base tree — the §3.2 squash rule requires the eval-manifest\n        path to be the only addition, and a pre-existing file at that\n        path would mean a silent overwrite.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.tree_entry_exists(base_tree_sha, file_path):\n            raise GitError(\n                [\"write_tree_with_file\", base_tree_sha, file_path, blob_sha],\n                1,\n                \"\",\n                f\"path {file_path!r} already exists in tree {base_tree_sha}\",\n            )\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix=\"eden-git-index-\") as tmp:\n            index_path = str(Path(tmp) / \"index\")\n            env = self._env()\n            env[\"GIT_INDEX_FILE\"] = index_path\n            # Seed the index from the base tree.\n            self._run_with_env([\"read-tree\", base_tree_sha], env=env)\n            # Add the one new entry.\n            self._run_with_env(\n                [\n                    \"update-index\",\n                    \"--add\",\n                    \"--cacheinfo\",\n                    f\"{mode},{blob_sha},{file_path}\",\n                ],\n                env=env,\n            )\n            result = self._run_with_env([\"write-tree\"], env=env)\n        return result.stdout.strip()\n\n    def commit_tree(\n        self,\n        tree_sha: str,\n        *,\n        parents: Iterable[str],\n        message: str,\n        author: Identity,\n        committer: Identity | None = None,\n        author_date: str | None = None,\n        committer_date: str | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a commit object pointing at ``tree_sha``.\n\n        Returns the new commit's SHA. The commit is not attached to any\n        ref — callers use :meth:`create_ref` / :meth:`update_ref` to\n        publish it. ``parents`` may be empty (root commit), one (single-\n        parent), or many (merge). ``committer`` defaults to ``author``.\n        \"\"\"\n        args = [\"commit-tree\", tree_sha]\n        for parent in parents:\n            args.extend([\"-p\", parent])\n        args.extend([\"-m\", message])\n        committer = committer or author\n        env = self._env()\n        env[\"GIT_AUTHOR_NAME\"] = author.name\n        env[\"GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL\"] = author.email\n        env[\"GIT_COMMITTER_NAME\"] = committer.name\n        env[\"GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL\"] = committer.email\n        if author_date is not None:\n            env[\"GIT_AUTHOR_DATE\"] = author_date\n        if committer_date is not None:\n            env[\"GIT_COMMITTER_DATE\"] = committer_date\n        result = self._run_with_env(args, env=env)\n        return result.stdout.strip()\n\n    def create_ref(self, refname: str, new_sha: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Create a ref atomically; raise if it already exists.\n\n        Uses ``update-ref --create-reflog <ref> <new> <zero>`` which\n        requires the ref to currently point at the zero OID (i.e. be\n        absent). This is the primitive the integrator uses when\n        publishing a `trial/*` branch — the §1.2 invariant forbids\n        overwriting an existing `trial/*` ref.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._run([\"update-ref\", refname, new_sha, _ZERO_SHA])\n\n    def update_ref(\n        self, refname: str, new_sha: str, *, expected_old_sha: str | None = None\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Compare-and-swap a ref to a new SHA.\n\n        If ``expected_old_sha`` is None, overwrites unconditionally. If\n        provided, ``git update-ref`` verifies the ref currently points\n        at ``expected_old_sha`` and raises :class:`GitError` otherwise.\n        \"\"\"\n        args = [\"update-ref\", refname, new_sha]\n        if expected_old_sha is not None:\n            args.append(expected_old_sha)\n        self._run(args)\n\n    def delete_ref(self, refname: str, *, expected_old_sha: str | None = None) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Delete a ref, optionally guarded by ``expected_old_sha``.\"\"\"\n        args = [\"update-ref\", \"-d\", refname]\n        if expected_old_sha is not None:\n            args.append(expected_old_sha)\n        self._run(args)\n\n    # --- branches & worktrees --------------------------------------------\n\n    def create_branch(self, branch_name: str, start_point: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Create a new branch at ``start_point``. Raises if it exists.\"\"\"\n        self._run([\"branch\", branch_name, start_point])\n\n    def delete_branch(self, branch_name: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Delete a local branch (force).\"\"\"\n        self._run([\"branch\", \"-D\", branch_name])\n\n    def current_branch(self) -> str | None:\n        \"\"\"Return the current branch name, or ``None`` if detached.\"\"\"\n        result = self._run([\"symbolic-ref\", \"--short\", \"-q\", \"HEAD\"], check=False)\n        if result.returncode != 0:\n            return None\n        return result.stdout.strip() or None\n\n    def add_worktree(\n        self,\n        worktree_path: Path | str,\n        *,\n        start_point: str,\n        branch: str | None = None,\n        detach: bool = False,\n    ) -> Path:\n        \"\"\"Add a worktree at ``worktree_path`` starting from ``start_point``.\n\n        Exactly one of ``branch`` or ``detach`` must be supplied. When\n        ``branch`` is given, a new branch is created at ``start_point``\n        and the worktree checks it out. When ``detach`` is True, the\n        worktree is a detached checkout of ``start_point``.\n        \"\"\"\n        if (branch is None) == (not detach):\n            raise ValueError(\"specify exactly one of `branch` or `detach=True`\")\n        target = Path(worktree_path).resolve()\n        args = [\"worktree\", \"add\"]\n        if detach:\n            args.extend([\"--detach\", str(target), start_point])\n        else:\n            assert branch is not None\n            args.extend([\"-b\", branch, str(target), start_point])\n        self._run(args)\n        return target\n\n    def remove_worktree(self, worktree_path: Path | str, *, force: bool = False) -> None:\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
