docs/plans/review/eden-phase-7a-git-wrapper/impl/20260423T172233/0.jsonl:59:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_30","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' /Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/roadmap.md\"","aggregated_output":"# EDEN Roadmap\n\nIncremental build-up from empty repo to a complete EDEN protocol\nspecification, reference implementation, and conformance suite.\n\n## Two orthogonal concepts\n\n- **Units** — the fine-grained decomposition. Each unit is a distinct,\n  named piece of work with its own exit criterion. Units are the\n  progress-tracking granularity: a unit can be crossed off even when it\n  ships in a shared commit with siblings.\n- **Chunks** — execution grouping. Units that must be designed and\n  implemented together (cross-referencing spec chapters, protocol\n  alongside its first consumer, shared commit-sized work) travel as one\n  chunk. Chunks are the review/commit granularity.\n\n**Rule:** a chunk can span multiple units; no unit spans chunks. If\nimplementation runs over context mid-chunk, stop at the next unit\nboundary — never mid-unit. That keeps handoffs, resumes, and partial\nprogress legible across sessions.\n\nEach phase also has explicit **non-goals** to prevent creep.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 0 — Bootstrap\n\nRepo shape, documentation shell, CI, GitHub hygiene. No runnable code\nand no spec content.\n\n**Units:**\n\n- **0a** — Repo scaffold (directory tree + section READMEs + root docs +\n  CI workflow + `.gitignore` + `.markdownlint.json` + `CLAUDE.md`\n  symlink). First `main` commit on GitHub; branch protection requiring\n  `docs-lint`.\n\n**Chunk:** 0a is one chunk — this bootstrap.\n\n**Non-goals:** any spec chapter content; any JSON Schema files; any\nPython; any reference code; any conformance scenarios.\n\n**Exit:** `docs-lint` CI job green on `main`; protected branch in place;\nall root and section READMEs accurately describe what's in the repo and\nwhat's next.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 1 — Spec v0 core concepts + schemas + fixture migration\n\nWrites the first normative chapters and the JSON Schemas they describe.\n\n**Units:**\n\n- **1a** — `spec/v0/00-overview.md`, `01-concepts.md`,\n  `02-data-model.md` prose. Defines experiment, trial, proposal, role,\n  artifact, metric, worker.\n- **1b** — JSON Schemas for config, task, event, proposal, trial,\n  metrics-meta under `spec/v0/schemas/`. Each cited from the data-model\n  chapter.\n- **1c** — Migrate `tests/fixtures/experiment/.eden/config.yaml` from\n  direvo; assert `experiment-config.schema.json` validates it. Add a\n  `schema-validity` CI job.\n\n**Chunk:** 1a + 1b + 1c — one chunk. Prose, schemas, and fixture\ncross-constrain each other; splitting would produce inconsistency.\n\n**Non-goals:** role contracts (Phase 2); event/integrator/storage\nchapters (Phase 4); any Pydantic code (Phase 3).\n\n**Exit:** schemas validate the fixture; `schema-validity` CI green;\n`02-data-model.md` cross-references all six schemas.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 2 — Spec v0 role contracts + task protocol\n\nTwo more chapters. Defines what each role does and the task lifecycle\nthey operate against.\n\n**Units:**\n\n- **2a** — `03-roles.md`. Planner, implementer, evaluator contracts:\n  discovery, claiming, context reading, execution, submission, release.\n  Per-role outputs and their expected schema shapes.\n- **2b** — `04-task-protocol.md`. Full task state machine (legal and\n  illegal transitions enumerated), claim-token semantics, submit\n  idempotency rule, wire format for task objects.\n\n**Chunk:** 2a + 2b — one chunk. Role contracts reference task\nlifecycle and vice versa.\n\n**Non-goals:** event protocol (Phase 4); any implementation (Phase 5+).\n\n**Exit:** state machine is pinned; `04-task-protocol.md` enumerates\nevery legal transition with its pre- and post-conditions.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 3 — Reference contracts package (`eden-contracts`)\n\nFirst real Python code: Pydantic bindings for the v0 JSON Schemas.\nIntroduces the uv workspace + Python toolchain.\n\n**Units:**\n\n- **3a** — Pydantic models for the six v0 schemas, along with the\n  root `pyproject.toml` (uv workspace root), a per-package\n  `reference/packages/eden-contracts/pyproject.toml`, and a\n  `.python-version` file. Ruff/pyright config ported from direvo.\n  CI jobs: `python-lint` (ruff), `python-typecheck` (pyright),\n  `python-test` (pytest).\n- **3b** — Schema ↔ model parity check. CI job `schema-parity` that\n  loads each JSON Schema and asserts the matching Pydantic model can\n  round-trip any instance; fails CI on drift.\n\n**Chunk:** 3a + 3b — one chunk.\n\n**Non-goals:** any domain logic, just data types; storage, git,\ndispatch (later phases).\n\n**Exit:** all six Pydantic models exist and parity CI is green against\nthe Phase 1 schemas.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 4 — Spec v0 events + integrator + storage\n\nThree more chapters. Heavy cross-referencing: the integrator emits\nevents; storage persists them.\n\n**Units:**\n\n- **4a** — `05-event-protocol.md` + `event.schema.json` refinement.\n  Transactional invariant (state change + event insert must commit\n  atomically); delivery guarantees subscribers can rely on.\n- **4b** — `06-integrator.md`. Git topology invariants: `work/*`,\n  `trial/*`, `main` namespaces; sole-integrator rule; squash rule;\n  eval-manifest shape (metrics + blob URIs + hashes).\n- **4c** — `08-storage.md`. Repository interface as protocol-level\n  contract; durability requirements; per-experiment metrics schemas.\n\n**Chunk:** 4a + 4b + 4c — one chunk. Cross-references are dense and\nsplitting risks inconsistency.\n\n**Non-goals:** control-plane (Phase 12); conformance chapter (Phase 11);\nany code.\n\n**Exit:** three chapters land with consistent cross-references; schema\nupdates (if any) pass parity.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 5 — In-memory reference dispatch loop\n\nFirst executable implementation. Proves the spec's state machines are\nimplementable. Single process; no git yet; no persistence.\n\n**Units:**\n\n- **5a — complete.** `eden_dispatch.InMemoryStore` collapses the task\n  store, event log, and proposal/trial persistence into one in-process\n  object. Every mutating operation stages writes in a `_Tx` and\n  applies them in a single `_commit`; precondition failures raise\n  before commit, so readers never observe a partial state change.\n  All six composite-commit patterns from `05-event-protocol.md` §2.2\n  land atomically.\n- **5b — complete.** `ScriptedPlanner`, `ScriptedImplementer`, and\n  `ScriptedEvaluator` participate in the real task protocol (claim →\n  execute → submit) with deterministic fake outputs. `run_experiment`\n  orchestrates a full plan → implement → evaluate → integrate cycle.\n- **5c — complete.** 36 pytest scenarios cover every legal\n  transition in §1.2, every negative rule, claim-token authorization\n  (§3.3), idempotent resubmit (§4.2), terminal immutability (§4.4),\n  the reclamation policy (§5), every composite commit (§2.2), and a\n  3-trial end-to-end experiment whose event log alone reconstructs\n  every entity's lifecycle.\n\n**Chunks:** 5a one chunk; 5b + 5c one chunk (scenarios need a worker\nharness to drive).\n\n**Non-goals:** git integration; SQLite persistence; cross-process.\n\n**Exit achieved:** the in-memory loop runs a 3-trial experiment end\nto end; 36 conformance scenarios pass; all CI gates green.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 6 — Reference storage backend (`eden-storage`) — complete\n\nMakes Phase 5 persist across restarts.\n\n**Units:**\n\n- **6a — complete.** `Store` Protocol in `reference/packages/eden-storage/\n  src/eden_storage/protocol.py` covers the task store, event log reads\n  (`replay`, `read_range`; `events()` retained as alias), and\n  proposal/trial persistence sides of chapter 8; the spec-literal\n  `create_task(task)` sits alongside typed convenience helpers\n  (`create_plan_task`, etc.). The artifact store (§5), `subscribe`\n  streaming (§2.1), and cross-process wire transport are out of scope\n  for Phase 6 and are tracked for Phase 10 / Phase 8 respectively.\n  Shared transition logic lives in `_base.py`; `InMemoryStore` (moved\n  from `eden-dispatch`) and the new `SqliteStore` both satisfy the\n  Protocol and inherit the same validation and composite-commit paths.\n- **6b — complete.** SQLite-backed backend in `eden_storage/sqlite.py`\n  with a linear-migration schema in `_schema.py`. Every public op opens\n  a `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` transaction; commit on success, rollback on\n  exception. `WAL` + `synchronous = FULL` provides fsync-on-commit\n  durability matching the strict reading of chapter 8 §3.1\n  (\"survives a subsequent crash of the store's host\"). A reopened\n  store resumes the event-id counter from the persisted event count\n  (via the AUTOINCREMENT `seq` column — format-independent), inherits\n  the metrics schema, and rejects a reopen whose `experiment_id`\n  mismatches or whose `metrics_schema` differs semantically (compared\n  via `sort_keys=True`, so key-order noise doesn't false-trigger §4.2).\n  Migrations run inside an explicit `BEGIN`/`COMMIT` so a partial DDL\n  failure rolls back cleanly. Conformance scenarios (65 per backend)\n  run via a parametrized `make_store` fixture, and 9 restart-safety\n  tests close/reopen a SQLite store mid-experiment and verify\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

A conforming integrator that talks to Gitea, GitHub, or an\n    12\tin-memory fake is equally valid; this package is simply the local-repo\n    13\treference.\n    14\t\n    15\tCurrent surface (Phase 7a):\n    16\t\n    17\t- `GitRepo` — opens an existing repository path.\n    18\t- Ref and object inspection: `rev_parse`, `commit_exists`,\n    19\t  `resolve_ref`, `ref_exists`, `list_refs`, `is_ancestor`, `ls_tree`.\n    20\t- Worktree management: `add_worktree`, `remove_worktree`, `prune_worktrees`,\n    21\t  `list_worktrees`.\n    22\t- Branch management: `create_branch`, `delete_branch`, `current_branch`.\n    23\t- Plumbing for integrator squashing (Phase 7b): `write_blob`,\n    24\t  `write_tree`, `commit_tree`, `create_ref`, `update_ref`.\n    25\t\n    26\tThe integrator flow itself — observing `trial.succeeded`, shaping the\n    27\tsquashed commit per §3.2, atomically writing ref + `trial_commit_sha` +\n    28\t`trial.integrated` per §3.4 — lands in Phase 7b.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

docs/plans/review/eden-phase-9c-implementer-module/20260424T215740/1-review.md:3:Assessment: the major context gaps from the prior round are addressed. The new [artifact trust-boundary section](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-9c-implementer-module.md:221) and the optional `--repo-path` gating in the [compatibility policy](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-9c-implementer-module.md:51) close the two biggest missing assumptions.
docs/plans/review/eden-phase-9c-implementer-module/20260424T215740/1-review.md:17:- The document is still internally inconsistent in several places about `trial_id` ownership and `--repo-path` semantics. The new [A.2 section](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-9c-implementer-module.md:251) says `trial_id` is server-only, but the route overview still says it is generated at draft time and embedded in a hidden field ([plan](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-9c-implementer-module.md:188)), the templates section still says `implementer_claim.html` includes a hidden `trial_id` ([plan](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-9c-implementer-module.md:509)), and the risks section still discusses hidden-field leakage ([plan](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-9c-implementer-module.md:828)). Likewise, `--repo-path` is now optional in the CLI section ([plan](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-9c-implementer-module.md:546)), but the docs-update section still calls it a required flag ([plan](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-9c-implementer-module.md:568)). These are not just editorial nits; they change what gets implemented and tested.
docs/plans/review/eden-phase-9c-implementer-module/20260424T215740/1-review.md:21:- The committed-state read-back logic is still too weak to prove “our submit committed.” The plan treats `read_task(task_id)` returning `submitted` or `completed` plus matching `worker_id` as enough to declare success ([plan](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-9c-implementer-module.md:395)). But the same plan explicitly keeps the default `worker_id` shared as `web-ui-1` ([plan](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-9c-implementer-module.md:562)), so `worker_id` is not a claim-unique identifier. The store already exposes `read_submission(task_id)` ([protocol](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/protocol.py:109)). The stronger read-back is: for `submitted`, compare the live claim token exactly; for `completed`, compare the committed submission payload to the submission we attempted. Without that, the UI can falsely report success for another claimant’s later submit.
docs/plans/review/eden-phase-9c-implementer-module/20260424T215740/1-review.md:29:This revision is materially better. The main round-0 blockers are largely fixed, and the overall design now looks sound for Phase 9c. I would not call it implementation-ready yet, though: clean up the stale contradictions, move or redefine the ref-collision guard, and strengthen the read-back confirmation so it keys off exact claim/submission identity rather than `worker_id`. After that, an edge-case/risk pass should be worthwhile rather than premature.

docs/plans/review/eden-phase-9-web-ui-impl/20260424T172430/0-review.md:6:- High: claim tokens are not stored “in the session keyed by task_id” as the plan says; they live in a process-global `_CLAIMS` map keyed only by `(worker_id, task_id)` in [planner.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/planner.py:41). Because sign-in always uses the configured `worker_id` in [auth.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/services/web-ui/src/eden_web_ui/routes/auth.py:29), a second browser session with the same worker id can open `/planner/{task}/draft` and reuse the first session’s claim. I reproduced this locally: session 2 received 200 on the draft page after only session 1 claimed the task. That weakens the UI auth boundary and breaks the intended claim ownership model.
docs/plans/review/phase-6-storage/impl/20260423T163433/0.jsonl:8:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_2","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' CLAUDE.md\"","aggregated_output":"# AGENTS.md\n\nThis file provides guidance to AI agents working with this repository.\n\n## What this repo is\n\nEDEN is a **protocol** for directed-code-evolution orchestration, not a\nsingle product. The repo contains three distinct kinds of artifact, each\nwith different change discipline:\n\n| Layer | Path | Authoritative? | Change discipline |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| Protocol specification | [`spec/`](spec/) | Yes — the source of truth | RFC-style: versioned, normative (MUST/SHOULD/MAY), carefully reviewed |\n| Reference implementation | [`reference/`](reference/) | No — *one* valid impl | Normal code-review discipline |\n| Conformance suite | [`conformance/`](conformance/) | Normative for tests | Black-box; must be implementation-agnostic |\n\nNon-normative human docs live in [`docs/`](docs/).\n\n## Current phase\n\n**Phase 6 complete.** The `eden-storage` package ships the `Store`\nstructural interface for chapter 8 — task store + event log +\nproposal/trial persistence collapsed into a single Protocol — and\ntwo conforming backends: `InMemoryStore` (fast, non-durable; moved\nfrom `eden-dispatch`) and `SqliteStore` (durable across restarts via\na WAL-mode SQLite database). Both share the transition logic in\n`_base.py`, and the same conformance scenarios are parametrized\nacross both backends — drift from the Protocol surfaces in tests,\nnot in production. Restart-safety tests close and reopen a SQLite\nstore mid-experiment to confirm state, event log, and claim tokens\nsurvive, and a monkey-patched commit failure verifies rollback.\nPhase 7 is next: the reference git integrator (`eden-git`). See\n[`docs/roadmap.md`](docs/roadmap.md) for the full 13-phase plan.\n\n## Commands\n\nAt Phase 6, markdown linting, JSON Schema validation, and the Python\ntoolchain for the `eden-contracts`, `eden-dispatch`, and `eden-storage`\nreference packages are wired up.\n\n| Command | Purpose |\n|---|---|\n| `npx --yes markdownlint-cli2@0.14.0 \"**/*.md\" \"#node_modules\" \"#.venv\" \"#docs/archive/**\" \"#docs/plans/review/**\"` | Lint all tracked markdown (pinned to CI's version; matches CI exactly) |\n| `pipx run 'check-jsonschema==0.29.4' --check-metaschema spec/v0/schemas/*.schema.json` | Validate each schema file against the Draft 2020-12 meta-schema (version pinned to CI) |\n| `pipx run 'check-jsonschema==0.29.4' --schemafile spec/v0/schemas/experiment-config.schema.json tests/fixtures/experiment/.eden/config.yaml` | Validate the fixture experiment config against its schema |\n| `uv sync` | Install/refresh the workspace virtualenv (root + `reference/packages/eden-contracts` + `reference/packages/eden-dispatch` + `reference/packages/eden-storage`) |\n| `uv run ruff check .` | Lint Python (config in root `pyproject.toml`) |\n| `uv run pyright` | Type-check the reference Python packages |\n| `uv run pytest -q` | Run the reference-package test suite (includes schema ↔ model parity) |\n| `uv run pytest reference/packages/eden-contracts/tests/test_schema_parity.py` | Run only the schema ↔ Pydantic model parity check |\n| `python3 scripts/spec-xref-check.py` | Validate every `§N.M` reference in `spec/v0/*.md` resolves to a real section heading in its target chapter. Run before committing a normative spec change. |\n\n### Commands that will exist in later phases\n\nThese are listed for orientation; the tooling is not wired up yet.\n\n| Command | Lands in |\n|---|---|\n| `docker compose up` end-to-end | Phase 10 |\n\n## Contribution conventions\n\n### Spec edits\n\n+ Every cross-component contract (wire format, state machine, invariant)\n  is defined in `spec/` first, then implemented in `reference/`. When\n  they disagree, the spec wins and the impl gets a bug.\n+ Spec chapters use RFC 2119 language: MUST, SHOULD, MAY.\n+ Spec versions are frozen once stable (`spec/v0/` is a single lineage;\n  breaking changes go to `spec/v1/`).\n+ Schema changes must update the spec prose, the JSON Schema file, and\n  (from Phase 3 onward) the Pydantic bindings in lockstep. CI will\n  enforce schema ↔ model parity once both exist.\n\n### Reference implementation\n\n+ Normal code-review discipline.\n+ Normative behavior is tested against the conformance suite (once it\n  exists in Phase 11).\n+ The reference impl is explicitly not a monopoly on correctness. A\n  third-party implementation that passes conformance is equally valid.\n\n### Adding or extending a JSON Schema + Pydantic binding\n\nThe `schema-parity` CI job is only as strong as what both sides of the\ntest actually enforce. Several Pydantic and `jsonschema` defaults let\ndrift through silently. When adding a new schema — or a new field type\nto an existing one — evaluate each of the following; reusable\nimplementations live in\n[`reference/packages/eden-contracts/src/eden_contracts/_common.py`](reference/packages/eden-contracts/src/eden_contracts/_common.py).\n\n+ **Strict numeric parsing.** Top-level models set\n  `ConfigDict(strict=True, extra=\"allow\")`. Non-strict mode coerces\n  `True`/`\"2\"` into int, but the schemas treat `type: integer` /\n  `type: number` as strict JSON types. New models must keep\n  `strict=True`.\n+ **Format assertions on both sides.** `format` keywords (`uri`,\n  `date-time`, …) are advisory by default in both the `jsonschema`\n  library and Pydantic. The schema-side validator wires a custom\n  `FormatChecker` in\n  [`tests/conftest.py`](reference/packages/eden-contracts/tests/conftest.py),\n  and the model side uses the reusable types in `_common.py`. A new\n  `format` keyword in any schema requires handlers on *both* sides;\n  the `test_format_coverage` test fails loudly if the schema-side\n  handler is missing.\n+ **Real date-time / URI validation, not just regex or `urlparse`.**\n  The regex on `DateTimeStr` accepts impossible values like\n  `2026-99-99T…Z`; an `AfterValidator` runs `datetime.fromisoformat`\n  to reject those. `UriStr` uses `rfc3986-validator`, not\n  `urllib.parse.urlparse`, which accepts malformed schemeful URIs\n  (e.g., spaces in the host).\n+ **Null vs absent.** JSON Schema's `type: X` rejects explicit `null`,\n  but Pydantic's `X | None = None` accepts it. Wrap every optional\n  typed field with `NotNone` from `_common.py` so absent is accepted\n  and explicit null is rejected.\n+ **Round-trip emission.** `model.model_dump(mode=\"json\",\n  exclude_none=True)` must re-validate against the schema. Add any\n  new model to `tests/test_roundtrip.py` so this is checked.\n+ **Corpus coverage.** Parity is asserted over the fixture corpus in\n  `tests/cases.py`. A new field type deserves at least one accept\n  fixture and one reject fixture per constraint the schema imposes\n  (required, pattern, enum, min/max, format, cross-field).\n\n## Commit guidelines\n\n+ Short imperative subjects (e.g., \"Add event protocol chapter\",\n  \"Pin task state machine\").\n+ For pull requests, include: what the change is, which phase it\n  advances, and any spec ↔ impl implications.\n\n## Related docs\n\n+ [`docs/naming.md`](docs/naming.md) — what EDEN is, at the concept level.\n+ [`docs/roadmap.md`](docs/roadmap.md) — the 13-phase build-up plan with\n  unit-level decomposition.\n+ [`docs/plans/`](docs/plans/) — active implementation plans.\n+ [`docs/archive/`](docs/archive/) — historical reference docs.\n+ [`STYLE_GUIDE.md`](STYLE_GUIDE.md) — formatting and naming conventions.\n+ [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](CONTRIBUTING.md) — setup and PR workflow.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
docs/plans/review/phase-6-storage/impl/20260423T163433/0.jsonl:9:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_3","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' spec/v0/08-storage.md && printf '\\\\n---FILEBREAK---\\\\n' && sed -n '1,260p' spec/v0/05-event-protocol.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Storage\n\nThis chapter specifies the three durable stores an EDEN deployment\ndepends on — the **task store**, the **event log**, and the\n**artifact store** — as protocol-level contracts. It pins the\noperations each store MUST expose, the consistency and durability\nproperties those operations MUST honor, and the invariants spanning\nstores that a conforming deployment MUST preserve.\n\nThe stores are introduced in [`01-concepts.md`](01-concepts.md) §10.\nThe entities they hold are defined in\n[`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md); the behavioral contracts\nconsuming them are in [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md),\n[`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md), and\n[`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md). This chapter specifies the\nstorage-side contract those behaviors rely on.\n\nA store is specified by **what observable guarantees it provides**,\nnot by any particular mechanism. A conforming task store MAY be\nbacked by a relational database, an embedded key-value engine, or an\nin-memory structure; the same is true of the event log and artifact\nstore. Conformance is behavioral.\n\n## 1. Task store\n\nThe task store holds the set of tasks and their current state for an\nexperiment. It MUST support the operations below, each with the\nlisted atomicity and failure semantics. Wire-level transport is a\nbinding concern and is not pinned here.\n\n### 1.1 Operations\n\n| Operation | Arguments | Effect |\n|---|---|---|\n| `create_task` | fully-formed `task` object with `state == \"pending\"` | Atomically inserts the task and appends `task.created` to the event log ([§6](#6-transactional-guarantees)). |\n| `claim` | `task_id`, `worker_id`, OPTIONAL `expires_at` | Atomically transitions a `pending` task to `claimed`, issues a fresh claim token, and appends `task.claimed`. |\n| `submit` | `task_id`, `token`, role-specific result | Atomically transitions a `claimed` task to `submitted`, persists the result, and appends `task.submitted`. Idempotent per [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.2. |\n| `accept` | `task_id`, role-specific acceptance payload | Atomically transitions `submitted → completed`, applies the role-specific effect (e.g. proposal completion, trial status write), clears `claim`, and appends `task.completed` plus any composite events. |\n| `reject` | `task_id`, `reason`, role-specific failure payload | Atomically transitions `submitted → failed`, applies the role-specific effect, clears `claim`, and appends `task.failed` plus any composite events. |\n| `reclaim` | `task_id`, OPTIONAL `cause` | Atomically transitions `claimed`/`submitted` back to `pending` (where permitted; [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §5.1), invalidates the token, clears `claim`, performs §2.2 composite effects where applicable, and appends `task.reclaimed`. |\n| `read_task` | `task_id` | Returns the current task object or a well-defined not-found signal. |\n| `list_tasks` | filter (`kind`, `state`, `experiment_id`, …) | Returns matching tasks. Ordering is implementation-defined. |\n\n\"Atomically\" means: the state change, the result-payload write, any\ncross-entity effect listed in [`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md)\n§2.2, and the event appends are all durable together or none of them\nis ([§6](#6-transactional-guarantees)).\n\n### 1.2 Atomic claim (serialization)\n\nFor any given `pending` task, at most one concurrent `claim`\ninvocation MUST succeed. A conforming task store MUST expose this as\na linearizable operation: after a successful claim, every subsequent\nreader of the task observes `state == \"claimed\"` with the new claim\nobject; no reader observes an intermediate state without the claim.\n\nThe serialization mechanism (row lock + update, compare-and-set on\nversion, `SELECT … FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`) is implementation-defined.\nThe observable guarantee is not.\n\n### 1.3 Token-authorization rule\n\n`submit` and any other claim-holder operation MUST reject calls whose\npresented token does not equal the token currently recorded on the\ntask's `claim` object\n([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §3.3). A conforming\ntask store MUST NOT use `worker_id` as a substitute for the token\nwhen authorizing.\n\n### 1.4 Idempotent resubmit\n\nThe idempotency rule for `submit` is specified normatively in\n[`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.2. A conforming task\nstore MUST implement it: a resubmit carrying the current token, with\na content-equivalent payload, MUST succeed without mutating state;\nan inconsistent resubmit MUST be rejected.\n\n### 1.5 Terminal immutability\n\nOnce a task is `completed` or `failed`\n([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.4), no subsequent\noperation MAY change any task field except audit-only metadata the\ntask store documents outside the normative schema. In particular,\n`reclaim` MUST be rejected on terminal tasks.\n\n### 1.6 Per-task serialization\n\nAll mutating operations on a single task MUST be serialized: the\nobservable history of a task is a total order\n([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §6.1). A conforming\ntask store MUST NOT expose a state that has not yet been accompanied\nby its event ([§6](#6-transactional-guarantees)).\n\n### 1.7 Proposal and trial persistence\n\nProposals ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §5) and trials\n(§7) are persisted alongside tasks. A conforming deployment MAY\nback them with the task store itself, with a separate store, or\nwith any combination; the protocol constrains the observable\ncontract, not the physical layout. Every conforming deployment MUST\nexpose, for both proposals and trials, at minimum:\n\n- **Create** — durably insert a new object whose fields validate\n  against the corresponding schema\n  ([`schemas/proposal.schema.json`](schemas/proposal.schema.json),\n  [`schemas/trial.schema.json`](schemas/trial.schema.json)).\n- **Read** — return the current object or a well-defined not-found\n  signal.\n- **List** — return objects matching a filter (by `experiment_id`,\n  `state`/`status`, etc.). Ordering is implementation-defined.\n- **Update** — apply the field writes the role contracts and task-\n  terminal transitions specify ([`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §2.2,\n  §3.2, §4.4; [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.3,\n  §5.4).\n\nAll proposal and trial writes MUST commit atomically with the\naccompanying event(s) per §6. The durability (§3.1), read-after-write\n(§3.2), crash-recovery (§3.3), and no-fabrication (§3.4) rules apply\nuniformly. Terminal immutability applies to proposals in\n`completed` and trials in `success`/`error`/`eval_error` — the\n`trial_commit_sha` field is the **one** post-terminal write\npermitted on a trial, written exclusively by the integrator\n([`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md) §3.4), and it MUST be\nwritten atomically with its event and its `trial/*` ref.\n\n## 2. Event log\n\nThe event log holds the per-experiment event stream. It MUST support\nthe operations below and MUST honor the delivery guarantees specified\nin [`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.\n\n### 2.1 Operations\n\n| Operation | Arguments | Effect |\n|---|---|---|\n| `append` | fully-formed event object | Atomically appends the event, together with the state change it describes ([§6](#6-transactional-guarantees)). |\n| `read_range` | experiment_id, OPTIONAL cursor | Returns a durable, ordered range of events. |\n| `subscribe` | experiment_id, OPTIONAL starting cursor | Returns a stream that delivers events in log order until cancelled. |\n| `replay` | experiment_id | Returns the full stream from the first event ([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.4). |\n\n`append` MUST NOT be exposed as a standalone operation that the task\nstore, proposal store, or trial store can call independently of the\nstate change the event describes. The atomicity contract\n([§6](#6-transactional-guarantees)) requires the append to travel\nwith the state change; the event log operation is a primitive of the\ncomposite transaction, not an independent one.\n\n### 2.2 Ordering and causality\n\nThe log MUST present a single total order per experiment\n([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.1) that respects\ncausality ([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.2). The\nordering mechanism is implementation-defined; the observable\nguarantee is not.\n\nThe log MAY provide a global ordering across experiments as an\noptimization, but MUST NOT require subscribers to depend on it\n([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.6).\n\n### 2.3 Delivery\n\nAt-least-once delivery is the minimum contract\n([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.3).\nImplementations MAY offer exactly-once via durable offset commits.\n\n### 2.4 Retention\n\nFor the lifetime of an experiment — from registration until the\nexperiment reaches its operator-declared terminal state — a\nconforming event log MUST retain **every** appended event for that\nexperiment and MUST serve full replay from the first event\n([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.4). No\ncompaction, archival, or deletion MAY occur inside this window. A\ndeployment MAY compact or archive an experiment's events once it\nhas reached its operator-declared terminal state; after compaction\nthe log MUST either (a) reject replays older than the compaction\nhorizon with a well-defined error, or (b) serve those replays from\nan archive that preserves order and content. It MUST NOT silently\ndrop events from a replay.\n\n## 3. Durability\n\nThe following applies uniformly to the task store and the event log.\n\n### 3.1 Write durability\n\nAn operation that the store acknowledges to its caller MUST survive\na subsequent crash of the store's host. \"Acknowledgement\" is the\nreturn value indicating success; a store MUST NOT return success for\na write that has not yet been persisted to durable media (or the\nequivalent quorum in a replicated implementation).\n\n### 3.2 Read-after-write consistency\n\nAfter a successful write, every subsequent read from any reader MUST\nobserve the effect of the write. A conforming store MUST NOT expose a\nstale view of data after acknowledging the write that would update\nit. (Subscribers receive events via the log's delivery mechanism,\nnot by polling the task store, so the read-after-write rule applies\nto direct reads; event delivery is governed by §4 of the event\nprotocol.)\n\n### 3.3 Crash recovery\n\nA store that crashes mid-operation MUST recover to a state in which\nevery acknowledged write is visible and no partially-applied\noperation is visible. Implementations that use multi-step\ntransactions MUST ensure rollback on failure so that partial state\nnever becomes observable.\n\n### 3.4 No fabrication\n\nA store MUST NOT materialize an event, a task, a proposal, or a trial\nfrom nothing. Every persisted object MUST originate from a caller-\ndriven operation. In particular, a conforming event log MUST NOT\nsynthesize events the caller did not `append`.\n\n## 4. Per-experiment metrics schemas\n\nEvery experiment declares a metrics schema in its `experiment_config`\n([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §1.3,\n[`schemas/metrics-schema.schema.json`](schemas/metrics-schema.schema.json)).\nThe task store (or an equivalently-scoped component in a conforming\ndeployment) MUST enforce the following at every write that touches\ntrial metrics.\n\n### 4.1 Registration\n\nAt experiment registration time, a conforming deployment MUST persist\nthe experiment's metrics schema durably and atomically with the\nexperiment's other configuration. A subsequent write of a\ntrial's `metrics` field MUST be validated against the schema\nregistered for that experiment; no write MAY bypass this validation.\n\n### 4.2 Immutability during an experiment\n\nA metrics schema MUST NOT be mutated for the lifetime of the\nexperiment. An operator who wishes to change the metrics set MUST\nregister a new experiment (Phase 12 defines the control-plane\noperations that govern this, but the invariant applies from v0\nonward). A conforming deployment MUST reject any in-flight mutation\nof an existing experiment's metrics schema.\n\nThe rationale is canonicality: comparing trials across an experiment\nonly has meaning if the metric definitions they are compared on did\nnot move during the experiment.\n\n### 4.3 Per-metric type checks\n\nA successful write of a `trial.metrics` payload MUST satisfy:\n\n- Every key in `metrics` is present in the experiment's metrics\n  schema.\n- Every value either satisfies the declared type of its key — per\n  the type mapping in [`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §1.3\n  (`integer`, `real`, `text`) — or is `null`.\n- No reserved name ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §6.2)\n  appears as a key.\n\nA write that violates any of these MUST be rejected; the store MUST\n\n---FILEBREAK---\n# Event Protocol\n\nThis chapter specifies the event log: the envelope every event MUST\ncarry, the transactional invariant that binds event writes to the\nstate changes they describe, the per-type payload shapes for the v0\nevent registry, and the delivery guarantees a conforming event log\nMUST offer to subscribers.\n\nThe event envelope's JSON Schema is\n[`schemas/event.schema.json`](schemas/event.schema.json). The\nbehavioral contracts that produce events are in\n[`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) (task transitions) and\n[`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md) (trial promotion). The event\nlog's durability and subscription semantics as a *store* are in\n[`08-storage.md`](08-storage.md); this chapter specifies what events\nmean, not how they are persisted.\n\n## 1. Envelope\n\nEvery event appended to a conforming event log MUST be a JSON object\ncarrying:\n\n| Field | Required | Type | Meaning |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `event_id` | yes | string | Unique identifier for this event within the log. Opaque. |\n| `type` | yes | string | Dotted type name (`^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*(\\.[a-z][a-z0-9_]*)+$`). The normative registry is in §3. |\n| `occurred_at` | yes | timestamp | When the state change happened. UTC, trailing `Z`, RFC 3339 profile (`^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}(\\.[0-9]+)?Z$`). |\n| `experiment_id` | yes | string | The experiment the event belongs to. Events are scoped per experiment ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §4.3). |\n| `data` | yes | object | Type-specific payload. Shape is fixed by the registry entry for `type` (§3). |\n\nThe envelope is closed over these fields for conforming events; a\nconforming log MAY persist additional audit metadata alongside each\nevent, but MUST NOT expose it under any of the five envelope keys.\nImplementations MAY include additional top-level keys outside the\nenvelope (e.g. `sequence`, `recorded_at`); subscribers relying only on\nthe envelope MUST remain correct regardless.\n\n### 1.1 `event_id` uniqueness\n\n`event_id` MUST be unique within the log. A subscriber MAY dedupe by\n`event_id` when recovering from a disconnect.\n\n### 1.2 `occurred_at` vs. log order\n\n`occurred_at` records when the state change happened, which MAY\ndiffer from the event's position in the log — clock skew, batched\nwrites, or retried appends can all produce non-monotonic\n`occurred_at` within an experiment's stream. Causal order is\nestablished by the log itself (§4.3), not by timestamps.\n`occurred_at` is advisory for human debugging; subscribers MUST NOT\nrely on it for ordering.\n\n## 2. Transactional invariant\n\nThe central invariant of the event protocol, stated in\n[`01-concepts.md`](01-concepts.md) §6:\n\n> **Every state change that is observable via tasks, proposals, or\n> trials MUST be accompanied by a corresponding event, and the event\n> write MUST be atomic with the state change it describes.**\n\n\"Atomic\" means: either both the state change and the event append are\ndurable, or neither is. A conforming implementation MUST NOT expose a\nstate change to readers (direct store reads, cached projections, push\nnotifications, role dispatch) before its event is durable in the log;\nconversely, it MUST NOT append an event whose state change has not\nbeen durably written. If a transaction covering both is aborted,\nneither is observable.\n\nThe invariant applies to the set of entities enumerated in\n[`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md): tasks, proposals, trials.\nImplementations MAY emit *additional* events outside this set (see\n§3.5) that do not correspond to a protocol-defined state change; those\nadditional events are not subject to the transactional invariant\nbecause there is no state change to bind them to.\n\n### 2.1 Why atomic\n\nThe event log is the normative observability channel\n([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §6.3). Without\natomicity, subscribers reconstructing history can observe either a\nstate without its event or an event whose state the store does not\nyet expose — either is a protocol violation that leaves subscribers\nunable to trust the log.\n\n### 2.2 Composite transitions\n\nSeveral transitions span multiple entities and MUST commit together:\n\n- **Implement dispatch** — creating a `task` with `kind=implement` and\n  transitioning its referenced `proposal` from `ready` to\n  `dispatched` ([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §2).\n  Events: `task.created` + `proposal.dispatched`, in one atomic\n  commit.\n- **Implement terminal** — the `implement` task's terminal transition\n  (`submitted → completed` or `submitted → failed`) and the matching\n  `proposal` transition from `dispatched` to `completed`\n  ([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.3, §7). Events:\n  `task.completed` (or `task.failed`) + `proposal.completed`, in one\n  atomic commit.\n- **Evaluate terminal (`success`/`error`)** — the `evaluate` task's\n  terminal transition plus writes to the trial's `status`, `metrics`,\n  `artifacts_uri`, and `completed_at` ([`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md)\n  §4.4). Events: `task.completed` (or `task.failed`) +\n  `trial.succeeded` / `trial.errored`, in one atomic commit.\n- **Implement reclaim with in-flight trial** — reclamation of an\n  `implement` task whose prior execution left a trial in `starting`\n  requires transitioning that trial to `error` atomically with the\n  reclaim ([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §5.4). Events:\n  `task.reclaimed` + `trial.errored`, in one atomic commit.\n- **Retry-exhausted `eval_error` terminal** — the orchestrator's\n  transition of a trial from `starting` to `eval_error`\n  ([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.3). When the\n  orchestrator persists this transition as a state change on the\n  trial, it MUST emit `trial.eval_errored` atomically.\n- **Trial promotion** — the integrator's write of a `trial/*` commit\n  and the `trial_commit_sha` field on the trial\n  ([`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md)). Event:\n  `trial.integrated`.\n\nA subscriber processing any of these composite events MUST therefore\neither observe the full set or observe none; partial visibility is a\nprotocol violation.\n\n### 2.3 What is *not* a state change\n\nOperations that do not change protocol-owned state do not require\nevents:\n\n- Reading a task, proposal, trial, or event.\n- Uploading artifacts to the artifact store before they are referenced\n  from a protocol-owned object. (Populating `artifacts_uri` on a\n  proposal or trial *is* a state change and is covered by the events\n  on those objects.)\n- Worker-internal progress (scratch files, local logs).\n\nImplementations MAY expose operational telemetry through other\nchannels without running it through the event log.\n\n## 3. Event registry\n\nEvery v0 event `type` defined by the protocol is listed below. For\neach, the `data` object's required fields are pinned; optional fields\nare called out per entry. The JSON Schema in\n[`schemas/event.schema.json`](schemas/event.schema.json) enforces\nthese shapes via `if/then` dispatch on `type`.\n\nImplementations MAY emit additional event types outside this registry\n(§3.5). The `type` pattern in §1 is the only structural constraint on\nthem.\n\n### 3.1 Task events\n\nProduced atomically with the transitions defined in\n[`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md). The `task_id` field on\nevery task-event payload names the task whose transition is being\nrecorded.\n\n| Type | Transition | `data` required fields |\n|---|---|---|\n| `task.created` | — → `pending` | `task_id`, `kind` |\n| `task.claimed` | `pending` → `claimed` | `task_id`, `worker_id` |\n| `task.submitted` | `claimed` → `submitted` | `task_id` |\n| `task.completed` | `submitted` → `completed` | `task_id` |\n| `task.failed` | `submitted` → `failed` | `task_id`, `reason` |\n| `task.reclaimed` | `claimed`/`submitted` → `pending` | `task_id`, `cause` |\n\nPayload field definitions:\n\n- `task_id` — the `task_id` of the transitioning task.\n- `kind` — the task's `kind`\n  ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §3.1); one of `plan`,\n  `implement`, `evaluate`.\n- `worker_id` — the `claim.worker_id` recorded on the successful\n  claim ([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §3.2).\n- `reason` — one of the strings `\"worker_error\"` (the worker's\n  submission declared failure), `\"validation_error\"` (the orchestrator\n  rejected the result as malformed or non-conforming), or\n  `\"policy_limit\"` (a policy such as retry budget caused the failure).\n  The literal set is closed for v0; an implementation that needs\n  finer granularity MAY add a separate operator-level event under its\n  own type (§3.5).\n- `cause` — one of `\"expired\"` (claim `expires_at` passed),\n  `\"operator\"` (explicit operator action), or `\"health_policy\"`\n  (task store health policy declared the worker unreachable). The\n  literal set is closed for v0 on the same terms as `reason`.\n\nThe payload MAY include additional fields beyond those required;\nsubscribers MUST tolerate them. A conforming orchestrator SHOULD\ninclude the submitting worker's `worker_id` on `task.submitted`,\n`task.completed`, and `task.failed` events when known, as an\noperational convenience — but this is not required because the\nworker-task binding is already recoverable from the preceding\n`task.claimed` event.\n\n### 3.2 Proposal events\n\nProduced atomically with the proposal `state` transitions defined in\n[`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §5 and\n[`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §2.2. Composite commits that bind a\nproposal event to a task event are enumerated in §2.2.\n\n| Type | Transition | `data` required fields |\n|---|---|---|\n| `proposal.drafted` | — → `drafting` | `proposal_id` |\n| `proposal.ready` | `drafting` → `ready` | `proposal_id` |\n| `proposal.dispatched` | `ready` → `dispatched` | `proposal_id`, `task_id` |\n| `proposal.completed` | `dispatched` → `completed` | `proposal_id`, `task_id` |\n\nPayload field definitions:\n\n- `proposal_id` — the `proposal_id` of the transitioning proposal.\n- `task_id` — for `proposal.dispatched`, the `task_id` of the\n  `implement` task created by the same commit (§2.2). For\n  `proposal.completed`, the `task_id` of the `implement` task whose\n  terminal transition is being recorded.\n\n### 3.3 Trial events\n\nProduced atomically with trial `status` transitions and with the\nintegrator's promotion write. The trial's lifecycle is defined in\n[`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7 and\n[`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §3–§4; the promotion step is in\n[`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md).\n\n| Type | Transition | `data` required fields |\n|---|---|---|\n| `trial.started` | — → `starting` | `trial_id`, `proposal_id` |\n| `trial.succeeded` | `starting` → `success` | `trial_id`, `commit_sha` |\n| `trial.errored` | `starting` → `error` | `trial_id` |\n| `trial.eval_errored` | `starting` → `eval_error` | `trial_id` |\n| `trial.integrated` | — (integrator writes `trial_commit_sha`) | `trial_id`, `trial_commit_sha` |\n\nPayload field definitions:\n\n- `trial_id` — the `trial_id` of the transitioning trial.\n- `proposal_id` — the trial's `proposal_id`\n  ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1).\n- `commit_sha` — the worker-branch tip recorded on the trial at the\n  moment of success ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1).\n- `trial_commit_sha` — the canonical-lineage SHA the integrator wrote\n  ([`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md)). A 40-hex SHA-1 or a\n  64-hex SHA-256; the same pattern as commits elsewhere in the data\n  model.\n\n`trial.integrated` is not a trial-`status` transition — integration\ndoes not change the trial's `status` field, only its\n`trial_commit_sha`. The event marks promotion so subscribers can\nreconstruct the canonical lineage without reading git directly.\n\n### 3.4 Subscriber observability guarantee\n\nEvery protocol-owned state-machine transition in v0 is covered by\nexactly one event type in §3.1–§3.3. A subscriber that consumes\nevery event with a registered `type`, in log order, MUST be able to\nreconstruct the **lifecycle history** of every task, proposal, and\ntrial in the experiment: which entities exist, in what states, in\nwhat order. A conforming implementation MUST NOT expose a state-\nmachine transition that is not marked by its corresponding\nregistered event.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
docs/plans/review/phase-6-storage/impl/20260423T163433/0.jsonl:18:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_8","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/protocol.py && printf '\\\\n---FILEBREAK---\\\\n' && sed -n '1,320p' reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/_base.py\"","aggregated_output":"\"\"\"The ``Store`` Protocol — structural interface of a conforming EDEN store.\n\nThis module pins the union of the three stores named in chapter 8\n([`spec/v0/08-storage.md`](../../../../spec/v0/08-storage.md)) — task\nstore (§1), event log (§2), proposal/trial persistence (§1.7) — as a\nsingle Python `Protocol`. A deployment MAY split the physical layout\nany way it likes (§7); the Protocol constrains the observable\ncontract.\n\nWhy a single Protocol and not three:\n\n- Chapter 8 §7 explicitly permits a single backend to serve all three\n  stores, and the transactional invariant (§6.1, composite commits)\n  is most naturally expressed when a caller talks to *one* object that\n  atomically applies state changes and their events together. Two\n  separate objects with an external commit coordinator would add\n  complexity with no normative benefit.\n- Every composite commit in\n  [`spec/v0/05-event-protocol.md`](../../../../spec/v0/05-event-protocol.md)\n  §2.2 spans more than one store (task + proposal, task + trial, task\n  + trial, etc.); a Protocol that straddles those stores is the\n  smallest surface that can express \"these writes land together.\"\n\nThe Protocol is structural (`runtime_checkable=False`): a conforming\nbackend does not subclass it; matching the method signatures is\nenough. Two reference backends satisfy it — ``InMemoryStore`` and\n``SqliteStore`` — and conformance tests parametrize across both.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom typing import Protocol\n\nfrom eden_contracts import (\n    EvaluateTask,\n    Event,\n    FailReason,\n    ImplementTask,\n    PlanTask,\n    Proposal,\n    ReclaimCause,\n    Task,\n    TaskClaim,\n    Trial,\n)\n\nfrom .submissions import Submission\n\n__all__ = [\"Store\"]\n\n\nclass Store(Protocol):\n    \"\"\"Union of task store, event log, and proposal/trial persistence.\n\n    Every method is an atomic operation under chapter 8 §6: the\n    state change it describes, any composite effects\n    (``05-event-protocol.md`` §2.2), and the event append(s) all\n    land together or not at all.\n\n    A conforming backend MUST raise the error types from\n    [`errors.py`](errors.py) on rejection:\n\n    - ``NotFound`` — referenced entity does not exist.\n    - ``AlreadyExists`` — insert collided with an existing id.\n    - ``IllegalTransition`` — state transition not in the machine.\n    - ``WrongToken`` — presented token ≠ current claim token.\n    - ``ConflictingResubmission`` — resubmit disagreed with committed\n      result.\n    - ``InvalidPrecondition`` — referenced entity not in required\n      state.\n\n    The Protocol does not cover constructor signatures: backends differ\n    in their constructor (an in-memory store takes no URL; SQLite takes\n    a path). Callers should treat construction as backend-specific.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @property\n    def experiment_id(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"The experiment this store is scoped to.\"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Reads\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def read_task(self, task_id: str) -> Task:\n        \"\"\"Return the current task object, or raise ``NotFound``.\"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    def read_proposal(self, proposal_id: str) -> Proposal:\n        \"\"\"Return the current proposal, or raise ``NotFound``.\"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    def read_trial(self, trial_id: str) -> Trial:\n        \"\"\"Return the current trial, or raise ``NotFound``.\"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    def read_submission(self, task_id: str) -> Submission | None:\n        \"\"\"Return the committed submission for a task, or ``None``.\"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    def list_tasks(\n        self,\n        *,\n        kind: str | None = None,\n        state: str | None = None,\n    ) -> list[Task]:\n        \"\"\"Return tasks matching the optional ``kind`` / ``state`` filter.\n\n        Ordering is implementation-defined per chapter 8 §1.1.\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    def list_proposals(self, *, state: str | None = None) -> list[Proposal]:\n        \"\"\"Return proposals matching the optional ``state`` filter.\"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    def list_trials(self, *, status: str | None = None) -> list[Trial]:\n        \"\"\"Return trials matching the optional ``status`` filter.\"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    def events(self) -> list[Event]:\n        \"\"\"Return an ordered snapshot of the event log.\n\n        Backed by the log's total order per chapter 8 §2.2. For\n        restart-safe backends, replay returns every event since the\n        experiment began (§2.4).\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Task lifecycle — creation\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def create_plan_task(self, task_id: str) -> PlanTask:\n        \"\"\"Atomically insert a ``plan`` task + ``task.created`` event.\"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    def create_implement_task(self, task_id: str, proposal_id: str) -> ImplementTask:\n        \"\"\"Create an ``implement`` task; composite-commits ``proposal.dispatched``.\"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    def create_evaluate_task(self, task_id: str, trial_id: str) -> EvaluateTask:\n        \"\"\"Create an ``evaluate`` task against a starting trial with commit_sha.\"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Task lifecycle — claim, submit, accept, reject, reclaim\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def claim(\n        self,\n        task_id: str,\n        worker_id: str,\n        *,\n        expires_at: datetime | str | None = None,\n    ) -> TaskClaim:\n        \"\"\"Atomically transition ``pending → claimed``; issue a token.\"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    def submit(self, task_id: str, token: str, submission: Submission) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Atomically transition ``claimed → submitted``.\n\n        Idempotent per ``04-task-protocol.md`` §4.2.\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    def accept(self, task_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Atomically transition ``submitted → completed`` with composite effects.\"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    def reject(self, task_id: str, reason: FailReason) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Atomically transition ``submitted → failed`` with composite effects.\"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    def reclaim(self, task_id: str, cause: ReclaimCause) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Move ``claimed`` (or operator-reclaimed ``submitted``) back to ``pending``.\"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    def validate_acceptance(self, task_id: str) -> str | None:\n        \"\"\"Return a validation-error reason for the current submission, or ``None``.\"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    def validate_terminal(self, task_id: str) -> tuple[str, str | None]:\n        \"\"\"Decide how to terminalize: ``accept``, ``reject_worker``, or ``reject_validation``.\"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Proposal store\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def create_proposal(self, proposal: Proposal) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist a new ``drafting`` proposal; emits ``proposal.drafted``.\"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    def mark_proposal_ready(self, proposal_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Atomically transition ``drafting → ready``; emits ``proposal.ready``.\"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Trial store\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def create_trial(self, trial: Trial) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist a new ``starting`` trial; emits ``trial.started``.\"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    def declare_trial_eval_error(self, trial_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Retry-exhausted terminal ``starting → eval_error`` (``05-event-protocol.md`` §2.2).\"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    def integrate_trial(self, trial_id: str, trial_commit_sha: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Integrator promotion: write ``trial_commit_sha``; emits ``trial.integrated``.\"\"\"\n        ...\n\n---FILEBREAK---\n\"\"\"Backend-agnostic transition logic shared by every ``Store`` backend.\n\nEvery EDEN store backend — in-memory, SQLite, a future Postgres or\nremote backend — has to enforce the same state-machine, the same\ncomposite commits (``spec/v0/05-event-protocol.md`` §2.2), the same\ntoken/idempotency/terminal-immutability rules, and the same metrics\nvalidation. Factoring that logic out of any one backend and sharing\nit is the only way to make \"passes the same conformance suite\"\nmean what it says.\n\n``_StoreBase`` owns:\n\n- The public method surface (``claim``, ``submit``, ``accept``,\n  ``reject``, ``reclaim``, ``create_*``, ``read_*``, ``list_*``,\n  ``events``, ``validate_acceptance``, ``validate_terminal``,\n  ``create_proposal``, ``mark_proposal_ready``, ``create_trial``,\n  ``declare_trial_eval_error``, ``integrate_trial``).\n- All validation, composite-commit staging, and event construction.\n\nSubclasses own:\n\n- ``_atomic_operation`` — the transaction scope. In-memory wraps an\n  ``RLock``; SQLite wraps ``BEGIN IMMEDIATE``…``COMMIT``.\n- ``_get_task``/``_get_proposal``/``_get_trial``/``_get_submission``\n  — primitive lookups.\n- ``_iter_tasks``/``_iter_proposals``/``_iter_trials``/``_iter_events``\n  — ordered iteration.\n- ``_apply_commit(tx)`` — apply the staged ``_Tx`` inside the already-\n  open transaction, without committing it. The outer\n  ``_atomic_operation`` context does the actual commit.\n\nEvery public method follows the same pattern: open an atomic\noperation, perform reads + validations (which may raise before any\nwrite), stage all writes into a ``_Tx`` object, and call\n``self._apply_commit(tx)`` exactly once. If validation raises the\natomic operation aborts and no partial state becomes visible\n(chapter 8 §6.1–§6.3).\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport copy\nimport itertools\nimport secrets\nfrom collections.abc import Callable, Iterable, Iterator\nfrom contextlib import AbstractContextManager\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import UTC, datetime\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom eden_contracts import (\n    EvaluatePayload,\n    EvaluateTask,\n    Event,\n    FailReason,\n    ImplementPayload,\n    ImplementTask,\n    MetricsSchema,\n    PlanPayload,\n    PlanTask,\n    Proposal,\n    ReclaimCause,\n    Task,\n    TaskClaim,\n    Trial,\n)\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel, ValidationError\n\nfrom .errors import (\n    AlreadyExists,\n    ConflictingResubmission,\n    IllegalTransition,\n    InvalidPrecondition,\n    NotFound,\n    WrongToken,\n)\nfrom .submissions import (\n    EvaluateSubmission,\n    ImplementSubmission,\n    PlanSubmission,\n    Submission,\n    submissions_equivalent,\n)\n\n_METRIC_PY_TYPES: dict[str, tuple[type, ...]] = {\n    # spec/v0/02-data-model.md §1.3 type mapping: integer / real / text.\n    # bool is excluded from \"integer\" even though it is a Python int subclass.\n    \"integer\": (int,),\n    \"real\": (int, float),\n    \"text\": (str,),\n}\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass _Tx:\n    \"\"\"Staged writes for a single atomic operation.\n\n    A public method stages all mutations here and calls\n    ``_apply_commit`` exactly once at the end. Any precondition\n    failure raises before ``_apply_commit``, so readers never observe\n    a partial state change. Subclasses apply the contents against\n    whatever backing store they use (dicts, SQLite tables, …).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    tasks: dict[str, Task] = field(default_factory=dict)\n    proposals: dict[str, Proposal] = field(default_factory=dict)\n    trials: dict[str, Trial] = field(default_factory=dict)\n    submissions: dict[str, Submission] = field(default_factory=dict)\n    task_deletes_submission: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)\n    events: list[Event] = field(default_factory=list)\n\n\ndef _validated_update[M: BaseModel](model: M, **changes: Any) -> M:\n    \"\"\"Return a copy of ``model`` with ``changes`` applied and re-validated.\n\n    Replaces Pydantic's ``model_copy(update=...)``, which does **not**\n    re-run validators. Without re-validation a caller could stamp an\n    invalid ``commit_sha``, ``artifacts_uri``, or ``metrics`` shape\n    onto a stored trial. Re-validating on every update is how every\n    backend honors ``03-roles.md`` §3.4, §4.4 and ``08-storage.md``\n    §3.\n\n    Passing ``None`` for a field removes it (matches the ``NotNone``\n    rule on optional typed fields in ``eden_contracts._common``:\n    absent is permitted, explicit null is not).\n    \"\"\"\n    data = model.model_dump(mode=\"json\", exclude_none=True)\n    for key, value in changes.items():\n        if value is None:\n            data.pop(key, None)\n        elif isinstance(value, BaseModel):\n            data[key] = value.model_dump(mode=\"json\", exclude_none=True)\n        else:\n            data[key] = value\n    return type(model).model_validate(data)\n\n\ndef _deep[M: BaseModel](model: M) -> M:\n    \"\"\"Return a deep copy of a Pydantic model.\n\n    Used to insulate readers (``read_*``, ``list_*``, ``events``)\n    from in-place mutation of stored values, and to insulate the\n    store from mutation of caller-supplied values at ``create_*``\n    time. Backends whose read path inherently rehydrates a fresh\n    instance (e.g. SQLite's JSON round-trip) still go through\n    ``_deep`` for uniformity.\n    \"\"\"\n    return model.model_copy(deep=True)\n\n\nclass _StoreBase:\n    \"\"\"Shared transaction/validation/event logic for every store backend.\n\n    Subclasses implement the backend primitives listed at module-top.\n    The public surface here is the union of everything ``protocol.Store``\n    declares.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        experiment_id: str,\n        *,\n        metrics_schema: MetricsSchema | None = None,\n        now: Callable[[], datetime] | None = None,\n        event_id_factory: Callable[[], str] | None = None,\n        token_factory: Callable[[], str] | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        self._experiment_id = experiment_id\n        self._metrics_schema = metrics_schema\n        self._now = now or (lambda: datetime.now(UTC))\n        self._event_ids = itertools.count(1)\n        self._event_id_factory = event_id_factory or self._default_event_id\n        self._token_factory = token_factory or (lambda: secrets.token_hex(16))\n\n    def _default_event_id(self) -> str:\n        return f\"evt-{next(self._event_ids):06d}\"\n\n    @property\n    def experiment_id(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"The experiment this store is scoped to.\"\"\"\n        return self._experiment_id\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Backend primitives (subclasses MUST override)\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _atomic_operation(self) -> AbstractContextManager[None]:\n        \"\"\"Return a context manager providing atomic-operation semantics.\n\n        Either every write staged inside the context lands, or none of\n        them does. The outer context manager's exit is responsible for\n        committing (normal exit) or rolling back (exception); the inner\n        ``_apply_commit`` call stages the writes without committing.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def _get_task(self, task_id: str) -> Task | None:\n        \"\"\"Return the stored task, or ``None`` if absent.\"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def _get_proposal(self, proposal_id: str) -> Proposal | None:\n        \"\"\"Return the stored proposal, or ``None`` if absent.\"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def _get_trial(self, trial_id: str) -> Trial | None:\n        \"\"\"Return the stored trial, or ``None`` if absent.\"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def _get_submission(self, task_id: str) -> Submission | None:\n        \"\"\"Return the committed submission, or ``None`` if absent.\"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def _iter_tasks(\n        self, *, kind: str | None = None, state: str | None = None\n    ) -> Iterable[Task]:\n        \"\"\"Iterate tasks matching the optional filters.\"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def _iter_proposals(self, *, state: str | None = None) -> Iterable[Proposal]:\n        \"\"\"Iterate proposals matching the optional filter.\"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def _iter_trials(self, *, status: str | None = None) -> Iterable[Trial]:\n        \"\"\"Iterate trials matching the optional filter.\"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def _iter_events(self) -> Iterable[Event]:\n        \"\"\"Iterate events in log order.\"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def _apply_commit(self, tx: _Tx) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Stage the contents of ``tx`` for the current atomic operation.\n\n        For in-memory backends this applies directly to dicts. For\n        SQLite it issues INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE statements against the\n        already-open transaction; COMMIT fires when\n        ``_atomic_operation`` exits without an exception.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Public read API\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def read_task(self, task_id: str) -> Task:\n        \"\"\"Return a snapshot of the current task, or raise ``NotFound``.\"\"\"\n        with self._atomic_operation():\n            task = self._get_task(task_id)\n            if task is None:\n                raise NotFound(f\"task {task_id!r}\")\n            return _deep(task)\n\n    def read_proposal(self, proposal_id: str) -> Proposal:\n        \"\"\"Return a snapshot of the current proposal, or raise ``NotFound``.\"\"\"\n        with self._atomic_operation():\n            proposal = self._get_proposal(proposal_id)\n            if proposal is None:\n                raise NotFound(f\"proposal {proposal_id!r}\")\n            return _deep(proposal)\n\n    def read_trial(self, trial_id: str) -> Trial:\n        \"\"\"Return a snapshot of the current trial, or raise ``NotFound``.\"\"\"\n        with self._atomic_operation():\n            trial = self._get_trial(trial_id)\n            if trial is None:\n                raise NotFound(f\"trial {trial_id!r}\")\n            return _deep(trial)\n\n    def read_submission(self, task_id: str) -> Submission | None:\n        \"\"\"Return the committed submission for a task, or ``None`` if not submitted.\n\n        Returns a deep copy. Submission dataclasses are ``frozen``,\n        but their nested ``metrics`` dict is not; without deep copy a\n        caller could mutate the committed metrics in place and\n        corrupt future idempotency decisions.\n        \"\"\"\n        with self._atomic_operation():\n            submission = self._get_submission(task_id)\n            if submission is None:\n                return None\n            return copy.deepcopy(submission)\n\n    def list_tasks(\n        self,\n        *,\n        kind: str | None = None,\n        state: str | None = None,\n    ) -> list[Task]:\n        \"\"\"Return snapshots of tasks matching an optional ``kind`` and ``state``.\"\"\"\n        with self._atomic_operation():\n            return [_deep(task) for task in self._iter_tasks(kind=kind, state=state)]\n\n    def list_proposals(self, *, state: str | None = None) -> list[Proposal]:\n        \"\"\"Return snapshots of proposals matching an optional ``state`` filter.\"\"\"\n        with self._atomic_operation():\n            return [_deep(p) for p in self._iter_proposals(state=state)]\n\n    def list_trials(self, *, status: str | None = None) -> list[Trial]:\n        \"\"\"Return snapshots of trials matching an optional ``status`` filter.\"\"\"\n        with self._atomic_operation():\n            return [_deep(t) for t in self._iter_trials(status=status)]\n\n    def events(self) -> list[Event]:\n        \"\"\"Return an ordered snapshot of the full event log.\n\n        Every returned event is a deep copy; mutation of the return\n        value cannot rewrite log entries.\n        \"\"\"\n        with self._atomic_operation():\n            return [_deep(e) for e in self._iter_events()]\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Task lifecycle — creation\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def create_plan_task(self, task_id: str) -> PlanTask:\n        \"\"\"Create a ``plan`` task. Emits ``task.created`` atomically.\"\"\"\n        with self._atomic_operation():\n            self._require_no_task(task_id)\n            now = self._ts()\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

Idempotent per [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.2. |\n    38\t| `accept` | `task_id`, role-specific acceptance payload | Atomically transitions `submitted → completed`, applies the role-specific effect (e.g. proposal completion, trial status write), clears `claim`, and appends `task.completed` plus any composite events. |\n    39\t| `reject` | `task_id`, `reason`, role-specific failure payload | Atomically transitions `submitted → failed`, applies the role-specific effect, clears `claim`, and appends `task.failed` plus any composite events. |\n    40\t| `reclaim` | `task_id`, OPTIONAL `cause` | Atomically transitions `claimed`/`submitted` back to `pending` (where permitted; [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §5.1), invalidates the token, clears `claim`, performs §2.2 composite effects where applicable, and appends `task.reclaimed`. |\n    41\t| `read_task` | `task_id` | Returns the current task object or a well-defined not-found signal. |\n    42\t| `list_tasks` | filter (`kind`, `state`, `experiment_id`, …) | Returns matching tasks. Ordering is implementation-defined. |\n    43\t\n    44\t\"Atomically\" means: the state change, the result-payload write, any\n    45\tcross-entity effect listed in [`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md)\n    46\t§2.2, and the event appends are all durable together or none of them\n    47\tis ([§6](#6-transactional-guarantees)).\n    48\t\n    49\t### 1.2 Atomic claim (serialization)\n    50\t\n    51\tFor any given `pending` task, at most one concurrent `claim`\n    52\tinvocation MUST succeed. A conforming task store MUST expose this as\n    53\ta linearizable operation: after a successful claim, every subsequent\n    54\treader of the task observes `state == \"claimed\"` with the new claim\n    55\tobject; no reader observes an intermediate state without the claim.\n    56\t\n    57\tThe serialization mechanism (row lock + update, compare-and-set on\n    58\tversion, `SELECT … FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`) is implementation-defined.\n    59\tThe observable guarantee is not.\n    60\t\n    61\t### 1.3 Token-authorization rule\n    62\t\n    63\t`submit` and any other claim-holder operation MUST reject calls whose\n    64\tpresented token does not equal the token currently recorded on the\n    65\ttask's `claim` object\n    66\t([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §3.3). A conforming\n    67\ttask store MUST NOT use `worker_id` as a substitute for the token\n    68\twhen authorizing.\n    69\t\n    70\t### 1.4 Idempotent resubmit\n    71\t\n    72\tThe idempotency rule for `submit` is specified normatively in\n    73\t[`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.2. A conforming task\n    74\tstore MUST implement it: a resubmit carrying the current token, with\n    75\ta content-equivalent payload, MUST succeed without mutating state;\n    76\tan inconsistent resubmit MUST be rejected.\n    77\t\n    78\t### 1.5 Terminal immutability\n    79\t\n    80\tOnce a task is `completed` or `failed`\n    81\t([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.4), no subsequent\n    82\toperation MAY change any task field except audit-only metadata the\n    83\ttask store documents outside the normative schema. In particular,\n    84\t`reclaim` MUST be rejected on terminal tasks.\n    85\t\n    86\t### 1.6 Per-task serialization\n    87\t\n    88\tAll mutating operations on a single task MUST be serialized: the\n    89\tobservable history of a task is a total order\n    90\t([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §6.1). A conforming\n    91\ttask store MUST NOT expose a state that has not yet been accompanied\n    92\tby its event ([§6](#6-transactional-guarantees)).\n    93\t\n    94\t### 1.7 Proposal and trial persistence\n    95\t\n    96\tProposals ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §5) and trials\n    97\t(§7) are persisted alongside tasks. A conforming deployment MAY\n    98\tback them with the task store itself, with a separate store, or\n    99\twith any combination; the protocol constrains the observable\n   100\tcontract, not the physical layout. Every conforming deployment MUST\n   101\texpose, for both proposals and trials, at minimum:\n   102\t\n   103\t- **Create** — durably insert a new object whose fields validate\n   104\t  against the corresponding schema\n   105\t  ([`schemas/proposal.schema.json`](schemas/proposal.schema.json),\n   106\t  [`schemas/trial.schema.json`](schemas/trial.schema.json)).\n   107\t- **Read** — return the current object or a well-defined not-found\n   108\t  signal.\n   109\t- **List** — return objects matching a filter (by `experiment_id`,\n   110\t  `state`/`status`, etc.). Ordering is implementation-defined.\n   111\t- **Update** — apply the field writes the role contracts and task-\n   112\t  terminal transitions specify ([`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §2.2,\n   113\t  §3.2, §4.4; [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.3,\n   114\t  §5.4).\n   115\t\n   116\tAll proposal and trial writes MUST commit atomically with the\n   117\taccompanying event(s) per §6. The durability (§3.1), read-after-write\n   118\t(§3.2), crash-recovery (§3.3), and no-fabrication (§3.4) rules apply\n   119\tuniformly. Terminal immutability applies to proposals in\n   120\t`completed` and trials in `success`/`error`/`eval_error` — the\n   121\t`trial_commit_sha` field is the **one** post-terminal write\n   122\tpermitted on a trial, written exclusively by the integrator\n   123\t([`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md) §3.4), and it MUST be\n   124\twritten atomically with its event and its `trial/*` ref.\n   125\t\n   126\t## 2. Event log\n   127\t\n   128\tThe event log holds the per-experiment event stream. It MUST support\n   129\tthe operations below and MUST honor the delivery guarantees specified\n   130\tin [`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.\n   131\t\n   132\t### 2.1 Operations\n   133\t\n   134\t| Operation | Arguments | Effect |\n   135\t|---|---|---|\n   136\t| `append` | fully-formed event object | Atomically appends the event, together with the state change it describes ([§6](#6-transactional-guarantees)). |\n   137\t| `read_range` | experiment_id, OPTIONAL cursor | Returns a durable, ordered range of events. |\n   138\t| `subscribe` | experiment_id, OPTIONAL starting cursor | Returns a stream that delivers events in log order until cancelled. |\n   139\t| `replay` | experiment_id | Returns the full stream from the first event ([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.4). |\n   140\t\n   141\t`append` MUST NOT be exposed as a standalone operation that the task\n   142\tstore, proposal store, or trial store can call independently of the\n   143\tstate change the event describes. The atomicity contract\n   144\t([§6](#6-transactional-guarantees)) requires the append to travel\n   145\twith the state change; the event log operation is a primitive of the\n   146\tcomposite transaction, not an independent one.\n   147\t\n   148\t### 2.2 Ordering and causality\n   149\t\n   150\tThe log MUST present a single total order per experiment\n   151\t([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.1) that respects\n   152\tcausality ([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.2). The\n   153\tordering mechanism is implementation-defined; the observable\n   154\tguarantee is not.\n   155\t\n   156\tThe log MAY provide a global ordering across experiments as an\n   157\toptimization, but MUST NOT require subscribers to depend on it\n   158\t([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.6).\n   159\t\n   160\t### 2.3 Delivery\n   161\t\n   162\tAt-least-once delivery is the minimum contract\n   163\t([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.3).\n   164\tImplementations MAY offer exactly-once via durable offset commits.\n   165\t\n   166\t### 2.4 Retention\n   167\t\n   168\tFor the lifetime of an experiment — from registration until the\n   169\texperiment reaches its operator-declared terminal state — a\n   170\tconforming event log MUST retain **every** appended event for that\n   171\texperiment and MUST serve full replay from the first event\n   172\t([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.4). No\n   173\tcompaction, archival, or deletion MAY occur inside this window. A\n   174\tdeployment MAY compact or archive an experiment's events once it\n   175\thas reached its operator-declared terminal state; after compaction\n   176\tthe log MUST either (a) reject replays older than the compaction\n   177\thorizon with a well-defined error, or (b) serve those replays from\n   178\tan archive that preserves order and content. It MUST NOT silently\n   179\tdrop events from a replay.\n   180\t\n   181\t## 3.
