docs/plans/review/eden-phase-8a-wire-protocol/20260424T115133-impl/0.jsonl:55:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_28","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"nl -ba reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/protocol.py | sed -n '1,320p'\"","aggregated_output":"     1\t\"\"\"The ``Store`` Protocol — structural interface of a conforming EDEN store.\n     2\t\n     3\tThis module pins the task-side stores named in chapter 8\n     4\t([`spec/v0/08-storage.md`](../../../../spec/v0/08-storage.md)) — task\n     5\tstore (§1), event log reads (§2.1 ``replay`` + ``read_range``), and\n     6\tproposal/trial persistence (§1.7) — as a single Python `Protocol`.\n     7\tA deployment MAY split the physical layout any way it likes (§7);\n     8\tthe Protocol constrains the observable contract.\n     9\t\n    10\t**Out of scope at Phase 6:**\n    11\t\n    12\t- The artifact store (§5) is deferred to Phase 10 when blob storage\n    13\t  lands alongside the Compose stack.\n    14\t- The streaming ``subscribe`` operation (§2.1) is deferred to Phase 8\n    15\t  when the cross-process wire protocol exposes it over a transport\n    16\t  that can actually stream; Phase 6's in-process / SQLite backends\n    17\t  implement the pull-based ``replay`` + ``read_range`` siblings\n    18\t  instead.\n    19\t\n    20\tWhy a single Protocol and not three:\n    21\t\n    22\t- Chapter 8 §7 explicitly permits a single backend to serve all three\n    23\t  stores, and the transactional invariant (§6.1, composite commits)\n    24\t  is most naturally expressed when a caller talks to *one* object that\n    25\t  atomically applies state changes and their events together. Two\n    26\t  separate objects with an external commit coordinator would add\n    27\t  complexity with no normative benefit.\n    28\t- Every composite commit in\n    29\t  [`spec/v0/05-event-protocol.md`](../../../../spec/v0/05-event-protocol.md)\n    30\t  §2.2 spans more than one store (task + proposal, task + trial, task\n    31\t  + trial, etc.); a Protocol that straddles those stores is the\n    32\t  smallest surface that can express \"these writes land together.\"\n    33\t\n    34\tThe Protocol is structural (`runtime_checkable=False`): a conforming\n    35\tbackend does not subclass it; matching the method signatures is\n    36\tenough. Two reference backends satisfy it — ``InMemoryStore`` and\n    37\t``SqliteStore`` — and conformance tests parametrize across both.\n    38\t\"\"\"\n    39\t\n    40\tfrom __future__ import annotations\n    41\t\n    42\tfrom datetime import datetime\n    43\tfrom typing import Any, Protocol\n    44\t\n    45\tfrom eden_contracts import (\n    46\t    EvaluateTask,\n    47\t    Event,\n    48\t    FailReason,\n    49\t    ImplementTask,\n    50\t    PlanTask,\n    51\t    Proposal,\n    52\t    ReclaimCause,\n    53\t    Task,\n    54\t    TaskClaim,\n    55\t    Trial,\n    56\t)\n    57\t\n    58\tfrom .submissions import Submission\n    59\t\n    60\t__all__ = [\"Store\"]\n    61\t\n    62\t\n    63\tclass Store(Protocol):\n    64\t    \"\"\"Union of task store, event log, and proposal/trial persistence.\n    65\t\n    66\t    Every method is an atomic operation under chapter 8 §6: the\n    67\t    state change it describes, any composite effects\n    68\t    (``05-event-protocol.md`` §2.2), and the event append(s) all\n    69\t    land together or not at all.\n    70\t\n    71\t    A conforming backend MUST raise the error types from\n    72\t    [`errors.py`](errors.py) on rejection:\n    73\t\n    74\t    - ``NotFound`` — referenced entity does not exist.\n    75\t    - ``AlreadyExists`` — insert collided with an existing id.\n    76\t    - ``IllegalTransition`` — state transition not in the machine.\n    77\t    - ``WrongToken`` — presented token ≠ current claim token.\n    78\t    - ``ConflictingResubmission`` — resubmit disagreed with committed\n    79\t      result.\n    80\t    - ``InvalidPrecondition`` — referenced entity not in required\n    81\t      state.\n    82\t\n    83\t    The Protocol does not cover constructor signatures: backends differ\n    84\t    in their constructor (an in-memory store takes no URL; SQLite takes\n    85\t    a path). Callers should treat construction as backend-specific.\n    86\t    \"\"\"\n    87\t\n    88\t    @property\n    89\t    def experiment_id(self) -> str:\n    90\t        \"\"\"The experiment this store is scoped to.\"\"\"\n    91\t        ...\n    92\t\n    93\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    94\t    # Reads\n    95\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    96\t\n    97\t    def read_task(self, task_id: str) -> Task:\n    98\t        \"\"\"Return the current task object, or raise ``NotFound``.\"\"\"\n    99\t        ...\n   100\t\n   101\t    def read_proposal(self, proposal_id: str) -> Proposal:\n   102\t        \"\"\"Return the current proposal, or raise ``NotFound``.\"\"\"\n   103\t        ...\n   104\t\n   105\t    def read_trial(self, trial_id: str) -> Trial:\n   106\t        \"\"\"Return the current trial, or raise ``NotFound``.\"\"\"\n   107\t        ...\n   108\t\n   109\t    def read_submission(self, task_id: str) -> Submission | None:\n   110\t        \"\"\"Return the committed submission for a task, or ``None``.\"\"\"\n   111\t        ...\n   112\t\n   113\t    def list_tasks(\n   114\t        self,\n   115\t        *,\n   116\t        kind: str | None = None,\n   117\t        state: str | None = None,\n   118\t    ) -> list[Task]:\n   119\t        \"\"\"Return tasks matching the optional ``kind`` / ``state`` filter.\n   120\t\n   121\t        Ordering is implementation-defined per chapter 8 §1.1.\n   122\t        \"\"\"\n   123\t        ...\n   124\t\n   125\t    def list_proposals(self, *, state: str | None = None) -> list[Proposal]:\n   126\t        \"\"\"Return proposals matching the optional ``state`` filter.\"\"\"\n   127\t        ...\n   128\t\n   129\t    def list_trials(self, *, status: str | None = None) -> list[Trial]:\n   130\t        \"\"\"Return trials matching the optional ``status`` filter.\"\"\"\n   131\t        ...\n   132\t\n   133\t    def events(self) -> list[Event]:\n   134\t        \"\"\"Return an ordered snapshot of the event log.\n   135\t\n   136\t        Backed by the log's total order per chapter 8 §2.2. Equivalent\n   137\t        to ``replay()``; retained as the pre-Phase-6 convenience name.\n   138\t        \"\"\"\n   139\t        ...\n   140\t\n   141\t    def replay(self) -> list[Event]:\n   142\t        \"\"\"Return every event for this experiment in log order.\n   143\t\n   144\t        Chapter 8 §2.1 / §4.4: the event log MUST serve full replay\n   145\t        from the first event for the experiment's lifetime.\n   146\t        \"\"\"\n   147\t        ...\n   148\t\n   149\t    def read_range(self, cursor: int | None = None) -> list[Event]:\n   150\t        \"\"\"Return events after ``cursor`` in log order (chapter 8 §2.1).\n   151\t\n   152\t        ``cursor`` is the **cumulative** count of events the caller\n   153\t        has already observed — not the size of the last chunk. A\n   154\t        caller polling in a loop advances ``cursor`` by the length\n   155\t        of each returned chunk; passing the last chunk's size alone\n   156\t        would skip past events already read. The reference backends'\n   157\t        log order is total (chapter 8 §2.2), so indexing by\n   158\t        cumulative count is stable. A ``None`` (or ``0``) cursor is\n   159\t        equivalent to ``replay()``.\n   160\t        \"\"\"\n   161\t        ...\n   162\t\n   163\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n   164\t    # Task lifecycle — creation\n   165\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n   166\t\n   167\t    def create_task(self, task: Task) -> Task:\n   168\t        \"\"\"Atomically insert a fully-formed task + ``task.created`` event.\n   169\t\n   170\t        Chapter 8 §1.1: accepts a task object with ``state == \"pending\"``\n   171\t        and an empty ``claim``. For ``implement`` tasks this is a\n   172\t        composite commit that also transitions the referenced proposal\n   173\t        ``ready → dispatched`` (``05-event-protocol.md`` §2.2).\n   174\t\n   175\t        The typed helpers below (``create_plan_task``,\n   176\t        ``create_implement_task``, ``create_evaluate_task``) are\n   177\t        convenience constructors that build the task payload for the\n   178\t        caller; they both route through the same commit path as\n   179\t        ``create_task``.\n   180\t        \"\"\"\n   181\t        ...\n   182\t\n   183\t    def create_plan_task(self, task_id: str) -> PlanTask:\n   184\t        \"\"\"Atomically insert a ``plan`` task + ``task.created`` event.\"\"\"\n   185\t        ...\n   186\t\n   187\t    def create_implement_task(self, task_id: str, proposal_id: str) -> ImplementTask:\n   188\t        \"\"\"Create an ``implement`` task; composite-commits ``proposal.dispatched``.\"\"\"\n   189\t        ...\n   190\t\n   191\t    def create_evaluate_task(self, task_id: str, trial_id: str) -> EvaluateTask:\n   192\t        \"\"\"Create an ``evaluate`` task against a starting trial with commit_sha.\"\"\"\n   193\t        ...\n   194\t\n   195\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n   196\t    # Task lifecycle — claim, submit, accept, reject, reclaim\n   197\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n   198\t\n   199\t    def claim(\n   200\t        self,\n   201\t        task_id: str,\n   202\t        worker_id: str,\n   203\t        *,\n   204\t        expires_at: datetime | str | None = None,\n   205\t    ) -> TaskClaim:\n   206\t        \"\"\"Atomically transition ``pending → claimed``; issue a token.\"\"\"\n   207\t        ...\n   208\t\n   209\t    def submit(self, task_id: str, token: str, submission: Submission) -> None:\n   210\t        \"\"\"Atomically transition ``claimed → submitted``.\n   211\t\n   212\t        Idempotent per ``04-task-protocol.md`` §4.2.\n   213\t        \"\"\"\n   214\t        ...\n   215\t\n   216\t    def accept(self, task_id: str) -> None:\n   217\t        \"\"\"Atomically transition ``submitted → completed`` with composite effects.\"\"\"\n   218\t        ...\n   219\t\n   220\t    def reject(self, task_id: str, reason: FailReason) -> None:\n   221\t        \"\"\"Atomically transition ``submitted → failed`` with composite effects.\"\"\"\n   222\t        ...\n   223\t\n   224\t    def reclaim(self, task_id: str, cause: ReclaimCause) -> None:\n   225\t        \"\"\"Move ``claimed`` (or operator-reclaimed ``submitted``) back to ``pending``.\"\"\"\n   226\t        ...\n   227\t\n   228\t    def validate_acceptance(self, task_id: str) -> str | None:\n   229\t        \"\"\"Return a validation-error reason for the current submission, or ``None``.\"\"\"\n   230\t        ...\n   231\t\n   232\t    def validate_terminal(self, task_id: str) -> tuple[str, str | None]:\n   233\t        \"\"\"Decide how to terminalize: ``accept``, ``reject_worker``, or ``reject_validation``.\"\"\"\n   234\t        ...\n   235\t\n   236\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n   237\t    # Proposal store\n   238\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n   239\t\n   240\t    def create_proposal(self, proposal: Proposal) -> None:\n   241\t        \"\"\"Persist a new ``drafting`` proposal; emits ``proposal.drafted``.\"\"\"\n   242\t        ...\n   243\t\n   244\t    def mark_proposal_ready(self, proposal_id: str) -> None:\n   245\t        \"\"\"Atomically transition ``drafting → ready``; emits ``proposal.ready``.\"\"\"\n   246\t        ...\n   247\t\n   248\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n   249\t    # Trial store\n   250\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n   251\t\n   252\t    def create_trial(self, trial: Trial) -> None:\n   253\t        \"\"\"Persist a new ``starting`` trial; emits ``trial.started``.\"\"\"\n   254\t        ...\n   255\t\n   256\t    def declare_trial_eval_error(self, trial_id: str) -> None:\n   257\t        \"\"\"Retry-exhausted terminal ``starting → eval_error`` (``05-event-protocol.md`` §2.2).\"\"\"\n   258\t        ...\n   259\t\n   260\t    def integrate_trial(self, trial_id: str, trial_commit_sha: str) -> None:\n   261\t        \"\"\"Integrator promotion: write ``trial_commit_sha``; emits ``trial.integrated``.\"\"\"\n   262\t        ...\n   263\t\n   264\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n   265\t    # Shared validators\n   266\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n   267\t\n   268\t    def validate_metrics(self, metrics: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n   269\t        \"\"\"Validate metrics against the experiment's ``metrics_schema``.\n   270\t\n   271\t        Chapter 8 §4 (submit-time) and chapter 6 §2 (integration-time)\n   272\t        both depend on this guard. Raises ``InvalidPrecondition`` on\n   273\t        violation; no-op when the store has no registered schema.\n   274\t        \"\"\"\n   275\t        ...\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

docs/plans/review/eden-phase-8a-wire-protocol/20260424T115133-impl/0.jsonl:89:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_46","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"nl -ba docs/plans/eden-phase-8a-wire-protocol.md | sed -n '260,520p'\"","aggregated_output":"   260\t  A third-party caller can validate locally against the\n   261\t  experiment's metrics schema, or skip the pre-check and let the\n   262\t  server reject malformed payloads at submit time.\n   263\t\n   264\tThe reference Python `StoreClient` calls these `/_reference/`\n   265\tendpoints through `validate_terminal` / `validate_metrics` methods\n   266\tthat match the `Store` Protocol; this keeps the existing dispatch\n   267\tdriver working verbatim. The `Store` Protocol stays what it is; the\n   268\twire chapter stays narrower than the Protocol.\n   269\t\n   270\tIf a future phase decides one of these helpers deserves normative\n   271\tstatus (e.g. a conformance test needs it), it gets promoted into the\n   272\tchapter — and into `/v0/` — at that time, with a spec version bump\n   273\tif it's a breaking change. At 8a, neither is normative.\n   274\t\n   275\t### Schemas: `spec/v0/schemas/wire/`\n   276\t\n   277\tRequest/response JSON Schemas listed in §4 above. Each gets a\n   278\tpassing test under `reference/packages/eden-contracts/tests/`\n   279\t(extending the existing `schema-validity` + `schema-parity` jobs).\n   280\t\n   281\tThe error envelope schema is shared across all endpoints and is\n   282\tcited from each endpoint's prose in the chapter.\n   283\t\n   284\t### Package: `reference/packages/eden-wire/`\n   285\t\n   286\tA new workspace member depending on `eden-contracts` and\n   287\t`eden-storage`. Provides three cooperating pieces:\n   288\t\n   289\t1. **`eden_wire.models`** — Pydantic request/response types for\n   290\t   every endpoint. Each body type has a schema-parity test matching\n   291\t   `spec/v0/schemas/wire/*.schema.json`. Follows the same\n   292\t   `strict=True`, `NotNone`, `UriStr`, `DateTimeStr` discipline as\n   293\t   `eden-contracts`.\n   294\t2. **`eden_wire.server`** — A FastAPI application factory:\n   295\t   `make_app(store: Store) -> ASGIApp`. Each endpoint is a thin\n   296\t   adapter that:\n   297\t   - Validates the request body against its Pydantic model.\n   298\t   - Validates the experiment-ID header against the URL segment.\n   299\t   - Invokes the matching `Store` method.\n   300\t   - Catches each `StoreError` subclass and maps it to the RFC 7807\n   301\t     envelope.\n   302\t   - Returns a Pydantic-serialized response body.\n   303\t\n   304\t   The server does not embed business logic — every operation\n   305\t   delegates to `Store`. This keeps the server's conformance\n   306\t   story trivial: if the underlying `Store` is conforming, the\n   307\t   HTTP facade is conforming.\n   308\t3. **`eden_wire.client`** — A `StoreClient` class whose public API\n   309\t   satisfies the `Store` Protocol. Every method makes the\n   310\t   corresponding HTTP call via `httpx.Client` and either:\n   311\t   - Returns the decoded Pydantic entity, or\n   312\t   - Raises the matching `StoreError` subclass reconstructed from\n   313\t     the problem+json body.\n   314\t\n   315\t   `StoreClient` is used wherever a `Store` is used today: dispatch\n   316\t   driver, integrator, test fixtures. Structural Protocol conformance\n   317\t   means callers don't need to know whether they hold an in-process\n   318\t   `Store` or a wire proxy.\n   319\t\n   320\t4. **`eden_wire.errors`** — Serde for the RFC 7807 envelope,\n   321\t   including the `eden://error/...` type → exception class mapping.\n   322\t   Used by both server (raise → serialize) and client (deserialize\n   323\t   → raise).\n   324\t\n   325\t### Services\n   326\t\n   327\t- **`reference/services/task-store-server/`** — Entry point\n   328\t  `eden_task_store_server.__main__` that:\n   329\t  1. Parses CLI args (`--db-path`, `--experiment-id`,\n   330\t     `--experiment-config`, `--host`, `--port`).\n   331\t  2. Constructs either `SqliteStore.open(...)` or\n   332\t     `InMemoryStore(...)` based on `--db-path`.\n   333\t  3. Calls `eden_wire.server.make_app(store)`.\n   334\t  4. Runs it via `uvicorn`.\n   335\t\n   336\t  Logs structured events (stdout) for observability.\n   337\t\n   338\t- **`reference/services/orchestrator-service/`** — Entry point\n   339\t  `eden_orchestrator.__main__` that:\n   340\t  1. Parses CLI args (`--task-store-url`, `--experiment-id`,\n   341\t     `--repo-path`, `--plan-tasks`, `--implement-task-prefix`,\n   342\t     `--evaluate-task-prefix`).\n   343\t  2. Constructs `StoreClient(base_url, experiment_id)`.\n   344\t  3. Constructs scripted workers (Phase 5 / 8a flavor) against the\n   345\t     `StoreClient`.\n   346\t  4. Constructs an `Integrator(store=client, repo=..., author=...)`;\n   347\t     passes `integrator.integrate` as `integrate_trial`.\n   348\t  5. Calls `run_experiment(...)` to completion.\n   349\t  6. Exits zero on clean termination.\n   350\t\n   351\t- Existing directories `reference/services/orchestrator/`,\n   352\t  `planner/`, `implementer/`, `evaluator/`, `web-ui/`,\n   353\t  `control-plane/` remain stubbed with `.gitkeep`.\n   354\t  `orchestrator-service/` is a new directory; the bare\n   355\t  `orchestrator/` stays reserved as an alias in case a later\n   356\t  phase prefers that shorter name.\n   357\t\n   358\t### Tests\n   359\t\n   360\t1. **Parametrized conformance** — `eden-wire/tests/` adds a new\n   361\t   backend to the `make_store` parametrization used across\n   362\t   `eden-storage` and `eden-dispatch`: a `ClientStore` that wraps a\n   363\t   `StoreClient` pointed at an in-process FastAPI `TestClient` (no\n   364\t   network, but the full serialization + dispatch round-trip\n   365\t   happens). Every existing conformance scenario runs against this\n   366\t   backend unchanged. Drift between the wire binding and the\n   367\t   direct-store semantics surfaces as a test failure.\n   368\t2. **End-to-end two-process test** — `eden-wire/tests/test_e2e.py`\n   369\t   actually boots the task-store server as a subprocess on an\n   370\t   ephemeral port, runs the orchestrator service against it, and\n   371\t   asserts the post-run state matches the Phase 5 / 7b end-to-end\n   372\t   fixtures (3 trials, 3 evaluations, 3 promotions visible in\n   373\t   `list_trials(status=\"success\")` with `trial_commit_sha` set).\n   374\t3. **Error-envelope round-trip** — every `StoreError` subclass gets\n   375\t   a test that triggers it server-side and verifies the client\n   376\t   reconstructs the same exception class.\n   377\t4. **Experiment-id header check** — a request whose header\n   378\t   disagrees with the URL segment is rejected with\n   379\t   `experiment-id-mismatch`.\n   380\t5. **Schema parity** — each new wire schema has a\n   381\t   `tests/test_wire_schema_parity.py` that round-trips Pydantic\n   382\t   dumps against the JSON Schema.\n   383\t6. **Integrate-over-HTTP reconciliation — three-outcome coverage.**\n   384\t   One test per branch of the three-outcome rule:\n   385\t   - *Confirmed success.* Server's\n   386\t     `POST /trials/{id}/integrate` commits durably; the response\n   387\t     is then lost (injected transport disconnect after the\n   388\t     transaction but before serialization). The\n   389\t     `StoreClient.integrate_trial` read-back observes\n   390\t     `trial_commit_sha` equal to the expected value; the\n   391\t     Integrator returns success and MUST NOT compensate-delete\n   392\t     the ref.\n   393\t   - *Confirmed divergence.* Server has already committed a\n   394\t     *different* `trial_commit_sha` (simulated by seeding the\n   395\t     store with one SHA, then calling integrate with another).\n   396\t     The request is rejected inline with\n   397\t     `invalid-precondition` (same-value idempotency fails);\n   398\t     alternatively, the transport fails and the read-back\n   399\t     observes the different SHA. Both paths raise\n   400\t     `AtomicityViolation`; neither compensates.\n   401\t   - *Indeterminate.* Server has no `trial_commit_sha` at\n   402\t     read-back time (request never arrived, or transaction\n   403\t     rolled back, or is still in flight) or the read-back\n   404\t     itself fails. The client MUST raise\n   405\t     `IndeterminateIntegration` and MUST NOT compensate; the\n   406\t     `null`-SHA reading is not treated as positive proof of\n   407\t     non-commit.\n   408\t\n   409\t   A separate test verifies the compensation path is still\n   410\t   exercised by a **non-transport, non-divergence** rejection.\n   411\t   The test injects a mock `Store` (or uses a direct-`Store`\n   412\t   subclass) whose `integrate_trial` raises a synthetic\n   413\t   `InvalidPrecondition` without the different-SHA branch (for\n   414\t   example, a simulated \"trial status flipped out of success\n   415\t   concurrently\" condition). The Integrator MUST compensate-\n   416\t   delete the ref it wrote and re-raise, matching the Phase 7b\n   417\t   rule. This preserves the one branch where compensating\n   418\t   deletion is correct.\n   419\t7. **Same-value idempotency on `integrate_trial`.** Calling the\n   420\t   endpoint twice with the same `trial_commit_sha` emits exactly\n   421\t   one `trial.integrated` event; calling it with a different SHA\n   422\t   returns `invalid-precondition`. Verified by counting events and\n   423\t   by asserting the error type.\n   424\t8. **Long-poll `subscribe` semantics.** A subscriber connected at\n   425\t   `GET /events/subscribe?cursor=N` receives events appended after\n   426\t   cursor `N` within the poll window, receives an empty batch on\n   427\t   timeout, and resumes from the returned cursor on the next call.\n   428\t   Replay from cursor `0` returns the full experiment stream.\n   429\t\n   430\t### Build & CI\n   431\t\n   432\t- Add `reference/packages/eden-wire` to the uv workspace members in\n   433\t  root `pyproject.toml`; add it to `dev` dependencies; add its\n   434\t  tests to `testpaths`; add it to `pyright.include`.\n   435\t- Dependencies added to the workspace: `fastapi`, `uvicorn`,\n   436\t  `httpx`. (`jsonschema` and `pyyaml` already present; no new\n   437\t  schema-validator deps.)\n   438\t- No new CI job category. Existing `python-lint`,\n   439\t  `python-typecheck`, `python-test`, and `schema-validity` cover\n   440\t  the new code and new schemas; `schema-parity` extends\n   441\t  automatically to the wire Pydantic types.\n   442\t- `spec-xref-check.py` will automatically validate the new `07`\n   443\t  chapter's cross-references to chapters 4, 5, 8.\n   444\t\n   445\t## Algorithm — what the orchestrator's turn looks like over HTTP\n   446\t\n   447\tFor reference, a single iteration of `run_experiment` — unchanged\n   448\tfrom Phase 7b structurally — now performs these HTTP calls via\n   449\t`StoreClient`:\n   450\t\n   451\t1. `planner.run_pending(client)`:\n   452\t   - `GET /tasks?kind=plan&state=pending`\n   453\t   - For each: `POST /tasks/{id}/claim`, run scripted planner, per\n   454\t     drafted proposal `POST /proposals`, per ready proposal\n   455\t     `POST /proposals/{id}/mark-ready`,\n   456\t     `POST /tasks/{id}/submit`.\n   457\t2. `_finalize_submitted(client, kind=\"plan\")`:\n   458\t   - `GET /tasks?kind=plan&state=submitted`\n   459\t   - For each: `GET /_reference/tasks/{id}/validate-terminal`\n   460\t     (reference-only helper; not in `07-wire-protocol.md`) →\n   461\t     `POST /tasks/{id}/accept` or `POST /tasks/{id}/reject`.\n   462\t3. `_dispatch_implement_tasks`:\n   463\t   - `GET /proposals?state=ready`\n   464\t   - For each: `POST /tasks` (kind=implement).\n   465\t4. Implementer and evaluator loops follow the same pattern.\n   466\t5. `_promote_successful_trials`:\n   467\t   - `GET /trials?status=success`\n   468\t   - For each without a `trial_commit_sha`: `integrator.integrate(id)`.\n   469\t     Integrator calls into `client.read_trial`,\n   470\t     `client.validate_metrics`, and — on success —\n   471\t     `POST /trials/{id}/integrate` which atomically writes the\n   472\t     field and the event per chapter 6 §3.4 (server-side).\n   473\t\n   474\tPer-iteration HTTP count is proportional to the number of tasks in\n   475\tflight; the polling loop quiesces in O(1) round-trips after the\n   476\tqueue drains.\n   477\t\n   478\t## Atomicity and composite-commit handling\n   479\t\n   480\t### In-process composite commits stay atomic\n   481\t\n   482\tChapter 5 §2.2 composite commits (task-completed + proposal-completed,\n   483\tevaluate-terminal + trial.succeeded, reclaim + trial.errored,\n   484\ttrial-promotion ref + field + event) still commit atomically on the\n   485\tserver side: each endpoint runs under the existing `Store._commit`\n   486\ttransaction. A client invoking `POST /tasks/{id}/accept` on an\n   487\t`implement` task receives one response after the task-completed\n   488\tevent and the proposal-completed event have both been durably\n   489\tappended.\n   490\t\n   491\tThe binding does not distribute transactions across HTTP calls. No\n   492\tendpoint splits a composite commit into two HTTP requests; each\n   493\tcomposite commit maps to exactly one server transaction.\n   494\t\n   495\t### Chapter 6 §3.4 across the process boundary\n   496\t\n   497\tThe trial-promotion invariant (ref + trial_commit_sha + event all\n   498\tconsistent post-promotion) is the one composite commit that spans\n   499\tthe HTTP boundary: the ref write happens client-side in the\n   500\tIntegrator, and the store-side half (`trial_commit_sha` + event)\n   501\thappens server-side through `POST /trials/{id}/integrate`. In-\n   502\tprocess at Phase 7b, a `Store` exception from `integrate_trial`\n   503\tcaused the Integrator to compensate-delete the ref — the\n   504\t\"post-promotion reading\" from\n   505\t[`spec/v0/design-notes/integrator-atomicity.md`](../../spec/v0/design-notes/integrator-atomicity.md).\n   506\t\n   507\tOver HTTP, a transport failure — connection reset, timeout, proxy\n   508\tdisconnect — between the request being sent and the response being\n   509\treceived is **indeterminate**: the server may have durably committed\n   510\tthe transition before the response was lost. A blind\n   511\tcompensating-delete in that case would violate §3.4: the ref\n   512\tdisappears while the `trial_commit_sha` and `trial.integrated`\n   513\tevent stay, producing exactly the split-brain state §3.4 forbids\n   514\tafter a promotion returns.\n   515\t\n   516\tTwo mechanisms together close this gap:\n   517\t\n   518\t1. **Server-side same-value idempotency** on the endpoint (see\n   519\t   \"Request and response bodies\" §6 above): a repeated\n   520\t   `POST /trials/{id}/integrate` with the **same** `trial_commit_sha`\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

Each endpoint inherits the\n    61\tnormative semantics of the underlying operation; this section lists\n    62\tonly the wire shape.\n    63\t\n    64\t| Operation | HTTP | Path |\n    65\t|---|---|---|\n    66\t| `create_task` | `POST` | `/v0/experiments/{E}/tasks` |\n    67\t| `list_tasks` | `GET`  | `/v0/experiments/{E}/tasks` |\n    68\t| `read_task` | `GET`  | `/v0/experiments/{E}/tasks/{T}` |\n    69\t| `read_submission` | `GET`  | `/v0/experiments/{E}/tasks/{T}/submission` |\n    70\t| `claim` | `POST` | `/v0/experiments/{E}/tasks/{T}/claim` |\n    71\t| `submit` | `POST` | `/v0/experiments/{E}/tasks/{T}/submit` |\n    72\t| `accept` | `POST` | `/v0/experiments/{E}/tasks/{T}/accept` |\n    73\t| `reject` | `POST` | `/v0/experiments/{E}/tasks/{T}/reject` |\n    74\t| `reclaim` | `POST` | `/v0/experiments/{E}/tasks/{T}/reclaim` |\n    75\t\n    76\t### 2.1 Create\n    77\t\n    78\t`POST /v0/experiments/{E}/tasks` accepts a JSON request body whose\n    79\tshape matches\n    80\t[`schemas/task.schema.json`](schemas/task.schema.json) with\n    81\t`state == \"pending\"` and no `claim`. On success the server returns\n    82\t200 with the created task as the response body (same schema). The\n    83\tcomposite-commit rules in [`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md)\n    84\t§2.2 apply: creating an `implement` task transitions the referenced\n    85\tproposal atomically with the task insert; the server MUST perform\n    86\tboth effects in a single transaction.\n    87\t\n    88\t### 2.2 List and read\n    89\t\n    90\t- `GET /v0/experiments/{E}/tasks` returns an array of tasks\n    91\t  matching optional query parameters `kind` (one of `plan`,\n    92\t  `implement`, `evaluate`) and `state` (one of the five task\n    93\t  states). Ordering is implementation-defined per\n    94\t  [`08-storage.md`](08-storage.md) §1.1.\n    95\t- `GET /v0/experiments/{E}/tasks/{T}` returns the task object with\n    96\t  id `T`, or 404 `eden://error/not-found`.\n    97\t\n    98\t### 2.3 Claim\n    99\t\n   100\t`POST /v0/experiments/{E}/tasks/{T}/claim` accepts a JSON body with\n   101\tat minimum the field `worker_id` and an OPTIONAL `expires_at`\n   102\ttimestamp. On success the server returns 200 with a `claim` object\n   103\tmatching the shape from [`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §3.4\n   104\t(see also [`schemas/task.schema.json`](schemas/task.schema.json)) —\n   105\tnotably including the fresh `token`. On a non-`pending` task the\n   106\tserver returns 409 `eden://error/illegal-transition`. The\n   107\tclaim-token secrecy rule ([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md)\n   108\t§3.2) implies that intermediary proxies and logs MUST NOT persist\n   109\tclaim tokens in plaintext; that is a deployment concern and the\n   110\tbinding does not enforce it.\n   111\t\n   112\t### 2.4 Submit\n   113\t\n   114\t`POST /v0/experiments/{E}/tasks/{T}/submit` accepts a JSON body\n   115\tcontaining the current `token` and a role-specific `payload`\n   116\t(matching the claim's task `kind`). Atomicity, idempotency, and\n   117\tcontent-equivalence rules from [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md)\n   118\t§4 apply unchanged. A resubmit with a content-equivalent payload\n   119\tMUST return 200; a resubmit with a divergent payload MUST return\n   120\t409 `eden://error/conflicting-resubmission`. A wrong token MUST\n   121\treturn 403 `eden://error/wrong-token`.\n   122\t\n   123\t### 2.5 Accept and reject\n   124\t\n   125\t- `POST /v0/experiments/{E}/tasks/{T}/accept` transitions\n   126\t  `submitted → completed` per\n   127\t  [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.3. The request\n   128\t  body is empty (the orchestrator has already persisted the\n   129\t  submission; the server re-reads it to apply the composite-commit\n   130\t  effects).\n   131\t- `POST /v0/experiments/{E}/tasks/{T}/reject` transitions\n   132\t  `submitted → failed` per\n   133\t  [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.3. The request body carries a single\n   134\t  field `reason` drawn from the closed v0 vocabulary\n   135\t  ([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §3.1):\n   136\t  `\"worker_error\"`, `\"validation_error\"`, or `\"policy_limit\"`.\n   137\t\n   138\t### 2.6 Reclaim\n   139\t\n   140\t`POST /v0/experiments/{E}/tasks/{T}/reclaim` transitions a\n   141\t`claimed` or (operator-invoked) `submitted` task back to `pending`\n   142\tper [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §5. The request\n   143\tbody carries a single field `cause` drawn from the closed v0\n   144\tvocabulary: `\"expired\"`, `\"operator\"`, or `\"health_policy\"`.\n   145\t\n   146\t## 3. Proposal operations\n   147\t\n   148\t| Operation | HTTP | Path |\n   149\t|---|---|---|\n   150\t| `create_proposal` | `POST` | `/v0/experiments/{E}/proposals` |\n   151\t| `list_proposals` | `GET` | `/v0/experiments/{E}/proposals` |\n   152\t| `read_proposal` | `GET` | `/v0/experiments/{E}/proposals/{P}` |\n   153\t| `mark_proposal_ready` | `POST` | `/v0/experiments/{E}/proposals/{P}/mark-ready` |\n   154\t\n   155\tRequest and response bodies match\n   156\t[`schemas/proposal.schema.json`](schemas/proposal.schema.json).\n   157\t`list_proposals` accepts an optional `state` query parameter.\n   158\t`mark-ready` has an empty request body.\n   159\t\n   160\t## 4. Trial operations\n   161\t\n   162\t| Operation | HTTP | Path |\n   163\t|---|---|---|\n   164\t| `create_trial` | `POST` | `/v0/experiments/{E}/trials` |\n   165\t| `list_trials` | `GET` | `/v0/experiments/{E}/trials` |\n   166\t| `read_trial` | `GET` | `/v0/experiments/{E}/trials/{T}` |\n   167\t| `declare_trial_eval_error` | `POST` | `/v0/experiments/{E}/trials/{T}/declare-eval-error` |\n   168\t| `integrate_trial` | `POST` | `/v0/experiments/{E}/trials/{T}/integrate` |\n   169\t\n   170\t`integrate_trial` binds [`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md) §3.4\n   171\tand carries additional idempotency rules (§5 below); the other\n   172\tendpoints are transport-only bindings of their\n   173\t[`08-storage.md`](08-storage.md) §1.7 operations.\n   174\t\n   175\t## 5. `integrate_trial` — same-value idempotency\n   176\t\n   177\tThe `POST /v0/experiments/{E}/trials/{T}/integrate` endpoint has\n   178\tadditional semantics beyond transport-binding because\n   179\t[`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md) §3.4's atomicity invariant\n   180\tspans the process boundary. The request body carries the single\n   181\tfield `trial_commit_sha`.\n   182\t\n   183\t- **Success (first call).** The server atomically writes\n   184\t  `trial_commit_sha` on the trial and appends\n   185\t  `trial.integrated` per [`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md)\n   186\t  §3.4. Response: 200, empty body.\n   187\t- **Same-value idempotency.** A repeated call whose\n   188\t  `trial_commit_sha` equals the value already stored on the trial\n   189\t  MUST return 200 and MUST NOT append a second\n   190\t  `trial.integrated` event. This is what lets a client safely\n   191\t  retry an `integrate_trial` request after a transport-\n   192\t  indeterminate failure without double-commit.\n   193\t- **Different-value rejection.** A call whose `trial_commit_sha`\n   194\t  differs from the value already stored MUST return 409\n   195\t  `eden://error/invalid-precondition`. A conforming client MUST\n   196\t  surface this as an atomicity violation (the chapter 6 §1.2\n   197\t  sole-writer rule has been violated somewhere upstream);\n   198\t  operator intervention is required.\n   199\t- **Other preconditions.** Standard\n   200\t  [`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md) §3.4 preconditions\n   201\t  continue to apply: the trial MUST be in `status == \"success\"`,\n   202\t  and the `trial_commit_sha` MUST be a well-formed commit SHA;\n   203\t  violations return `invalid-precondition`.\n   204\t\n   205\tA client that issues `integrate_trial` and does not receive a\n   206\t2xx response (connection reset, read timeout, proxy disconnect)\n   207\tMUST treat the outcome as indeterminate and MUST NOT assume the\n   208\tserver has not committed. The reference reconciliation procedure\n   209\tis a read-back (`GET /v0/experiments/{E}/trials/{T}`) that resolves\n   210\tto one of three outcomes:\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

<<'PY'\nfrom fastapi.testclient import TestClient\nfrom eden_contracts import MetricsSchema, Proposal, Trial\nfrom eden_storage import InMemoryStore\nfrom eden_storage.submissions import The repo contains three distinct kinds of artifact, each\nwith different change discipline:\n\n| Layer | Path | Authoritative? | Change discipline |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| Protocol specification | [`spec/`](spec/) | Yes — the source of truth | RFC-style: versioned, normative (MUST/SHOULD/MAY), carefully reviewed |\n| Reference implementation | [`reference/`](reference/) | No — *one* valid impl | Normal code-review discipline |\n| Conformance suite | [`conformance/`](conformance/) | Normative for tests | Black-box; must be implementation-agnostic |\n\nNon-normative human docs live in [`docs/`](docs/).\n\n## Current phase\n\n**Phase 0 (bootstrap) complete.** No code yet. See\n[`docs/roadmap.md`](docs/roadmap.md) for the full 13-phase plan. The spec\nitself is not yet written — [`spec/v0/README.md`](spec/v0/README.md) lists\nthe planned chapters with their target phases.\n\n## Commands\n\nAt Phase 0 only markdown linting is in play.\n\n| Command | Purpose |\n|---|---|\n| `markdownlint-cli2 \"**/*.md\" \"#node_modules\" \"#.venv\" \"#docs/archive/**\"` | Lint all tracked markdown (matches CI exactly) |\n\n### Commands that will exist in later phases\n\nThese are listed for orientation; none of the tooling is wired up yet.\n\n| Command | Lands in |\n|---|---|\n| `uv sync` | Phase 3 (first `pyproject.toml`) |\n| `uv run ruff check .` | Phase 3 |\n| `uv run pyright` | Phase 3 |\n| `uv run pytest -q` | Phase 3 |\n| `check-jsonschema --schemafile ...` | Phase 1 (first JSON Schema) |\n| Schema ↔ Pydantic parity check | Phase 3 |\n| `docker compose up` end-to-end | Phase 10 |\n\n## Contribution conventions\n\n### Spec edits\n\n- Every cross-component contract (wire format, state machine, invariant)\n  is defined in `spec/` first, then implemented in `reference/`. When\n  they disagree, the spec wins and the impl gets a bug.\n- Spec chapters use RFC 2119 language: MUST, SHOULD, MAY.\n- Spec versions are frozen once stable (`spec/v0/` is a single lineage;\n  breaking changes go to `spec/v1/`).\n- Schema changes must update the spec prose, the JSON Schema file, and\n  (from Phase 3 onward) the Pydantic bindings in lockstep. CI will\n  enforce schema ↔ model parity once both exist.\n\n### Reference implementation\n\n- Normal code-review discipline.\n- Normative behavior is tested against the conformance suite (once it\n  exists in Phase 11).\n- The reference impl is explicitly not a monopoly on correctness. A\n  third-party implementation that passes conformance is equally valid.\n\n## Commit guidelines\n\n- Short imperative subjects (e.g., \"Add event protocol chapter\",\n  \"Pin task state machine\").\n- For pull requests, include: what the change is, which phase it\n  advances, and any spec ↔ impl implications.\n\n## Related docs\n\n- [`docs/naming.md`](docs/naming.md) — what EDEN is, at the concept level.\n- [`docs/roadmap.md`](docs/roadmap.md) — the 13-phase build-up plan with\n  unit-level decomposition.\n- [`docs/plans/`](docs/plans/) — active implementation plans.\n- [`docs/archive/`](docs/archive/) — historical reference docs.\n- [`STYLE_GUIDE.md`](STYLE_GUIDE.md) — formatting and naming conventions.\n- [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](CONTRIBUTING.md) — setup and PR workflow.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

Root docs\n\n- **`README.md`** — three-sentence elevator pitch + \"EDEN is a protocol\n  for directed-code-evolution orchestration; this repo ships the spec,\n  a reference implementation, and a conformance suite. Anyone can\n  build a conforming component in any language.\" Links to `spec/`,\n  `reference/`, `conformance/`, `docs/roadmap.md`. Current status:\n  \"Phase 0 (bootstrap); nothing runnable yet.\"\n- **`AGENTS.md`** — agent-facing. Layout tour with the spec/reference/\n  conformance split made loud. Commands section lists only what\n  currently works (markdownlint) and calls out what's planned\n  (`uv sync`, `ruff`, `pyright`, `pytest`, schema validation — to\n  be added in Phase 3+). Contribution conventions: **spec edits\n  require extra care** (versioned, normative, reviewed);\n  **reference edits are normal code changes**; **schema changes\n  must update the spec and the Pydantic bindings in lockstep** (CI\n  enforces this starting in Phase 3).\n- **`CONTRIBUTING.md`** — two-path structure mirroring the repo\n  structure: \"Contributing to the spec\" (RFC discipline, change\n  proposals, versioning) and \"Contributing to the reference\n  implementation\" (standard code-review workflow).\n- **`STYLE_GUIDE.md`** — ported from direvo.\n- **`CLAUDE.md`** — symlink to `AGENTS.md`.\n\n### G. Build & CI config\n\n**Bootstrap is docs-only**, so CI runs exactly one check.\n\n- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — single workflow named `ci`, single job\n  named **`docs-lint`**. That job name is also the required status\n  check in branch protection (pinned explicitly so the `gh api` call\n  is deterministic).\n- The `docs-lint` job:\n  1. Checks out.\n  2. Runs exactly this command (same in CI and local verification):\n\n     ```bash\n     markdownlint-cli2 \"**/*.md\" \"#node_modules\" \"#.venv\" \"#docs/archive/**\" \"#docs/plans/review/**\"\n     ```\n\n     Configuration comes from the repo's `.markdownlint.json`. Two\n     deliberate exclusions:\n     - `docs/archive/**` — archived historical documents (moved, not\n       rewritten) should not be held to the new lint rules; that\n       would be busywork against the archive's purpose.\n     - `docs/plans/review/**` — `/codex-review` transcripts are\n       tool-generated faithful records of review sessions; reformatting\n       them to satisfy markdownlint would damage their value as\n       records, and the rules they commonly violate (MD036\n       emphasis-as-heading, MD041 no-h1, MD040 fenced-code-language)\n       reflect Codex's output style, not a repo quality concern.\n\n     The `#node_modules` and `#.venv` exclusions guard against noise\n     from future dependency installs.\n  3. Exits 0 if all markdown passes.\n- **No Python job** at bootstrap (no Python files exist).\n- **No JSON Schema job** at bootstrap (no schemas exist, and an\n  empty-glob job is brittle — either a find returning nothing exits 0\n  quietly and hides a real misconfiguration, or the tool errors on\n  no-input. Easier to add this job in Phase 1 when the first schema\n  lands.)\n- **Later phases add jobs**, each as a distinct job name (so branch\n  protection can require each one as it stabilizes):\n  - Phase 1: `schema-validity` — validates `spec/v0/schemas/*.schema.json`.\n  - Phase 3: `python-lint` (ruff), `python-typecheck` (pyright),\n    `python-test` (pytest) — once the first package exists and has a\n    real test.\n  - Phase 3+: `schema-parity` — Pydantic models match their JSON\n    Schemas.\n  - Phase 11: `conformance` — runs the conformance suite against\n    the reference impl.\n\n### H. Git + GitHub execution\n\nSame as before:\n\n1. `git init -b main`\n2. Initial commit containing all of the above.\n3. `gh auth status`.\n4. `gh repo create eden --private --source . --remote origin --push \\\n   --description \"A protocol for directed code evolution orchestration\"`.\n5. `gh repo edit --add-topic protocol --add-topic specification \\\n   --add-topic directed-evolution --add-topic orchestration \\\n   --add-topic ai --add-topic research-automation`.\n6. Wait for the first `docs-lint` run on `main` to complete (so the\n   check name is registered in GitHub's status-check cache), **then**\n   enable branch protection.\n7. Branch protection uses **classic branch protection** (not the\n   newer repository rulesets — classic is better-documented for\n   scripted setup and the two tools can coexist). Call:\n\n   ```bash\n   gh api -X PUT \"repos/<user>/eden/branches/main/protection\" \\\n     -H \"Accept: application/vnd.github+json\" \\\n     --input - <<'JSON'\n   {\n     \"required_status_checks\": {\n       \"strict\": true,\n       \"contexts\": [\"docs-lint\"]\n     },\n     \"enforce_admins\": false,\n     \"required_pull_request_reviews\": {\n       \"required_approving_review_count\": 0,\n       \"dismiss_stale_reviews\": false,\n       \"require_code_owner_reviews\": false\n     },\n     \"restrictions\": null,\n     \"allow_force_pushes\": false,\n     \"allow_deletions\": false\n   }\n   JSON\n   ```\n\n   Rationale for each field:\n   - `contexts: [\"docs-lint\"]` — exact CI job name pinned in section G.\n     Added to as Phase 3 / Phase 11 introduce more jobs.\n   - `enforce_admins: false` — solo-repo hotfix path stays open.\n   - `required_approving_review_count: 0` — solo contributor;\n     bumping this to 1+ would block every merge. Increase when\n     collaborators arrive.\n   - `strict: true` — branches must be up-to-date with `main` before\n     merging; cheap safety on a low-traffic repo.\n   - `allow_force_pushes: false`, `allow_deletions: false` — keep\n     `main` durable.\n\n## Files to Reference\n\n- `direvo/pyproject.toml` — ruff/pyright config template.\n- `direvo/.gitignore` — baseline.\n- `direvo/STYLE_GUIDE.md` — copy verbatim.\n- `direvo/AGENTS.md` — structural model for the new one (content\n  differs because the project framing differs).\n- `eden/docs/naming.md` — source for README's elevator pitch.\n- `eden/docs/plans/eden-microservices-refactor.md` — source of\n  functional/non-functional requirements for the spec. Read before\n  writing spec; then move to `docs/archive/`.\n- RFC 2119 — the source for MUST/SHOULD/MAY language conventions used\n  in spec prose.\n\n## Verification\n\nBefore declaring done:\n\n1. `tree -L 3` shows: `spec/`, `reference/`, `conformance/`, `docs/`\n   at top level; `spec/v0/` present; `docs/archive/microservices-refactor-plan.md`\n   present; old `docs/plans/eden-microservices-refactor.md` gone.\n2. `readlink CLAUDE.md` prints `AGENTS.md`.\n3. The exact CI command passes locally:\n   `markdownlint-cli2 \"**/*.md\" \"#node_modules\" \"#.venv\" \"#docs/archive/**\" \"#docs/plans/review/**\"`.\n   (Running the identical command locally avoids works-locally /\n   fails-in-CI drift.)\n4. Spec prose (`spec/`) has its own\n     7\tconventions noted at the end.\n     8\t\n     9\t## General\n    10\t\n    11\t- Indentation: 4 spaces (Python), 2 spaces (JSON, YAML).\n    12\t- Maximum line length: 120 characters.\n    13\t- Use trailing commas in multiline structures.\n    14\t- End files with a single newline.\n    15\t\n    16\t## Python\n    17\t\n    18\t- Target version: Python 3.12+.\n    19\t- Formatter/linter: [Ruff](https://docs.astral.sh/ruff/).\n    20\t- Type checker: [Pyright](https://github.com/microsoft/pyright), standard\n    21\t  mode.\n    22\t\n    23\t### Naming\n    24\t\n    25\t| Kind | Convention | Example |\n    26\t|---|---|---|\n    27\t| Modules | `snake_case.py` | `task_queue.py` |\n    28\t| Test files | `test_<area>.py` | `test_task_queue.py` |\n    29\t| Functions, variables | `snake_case` | `claim_task()` |\n    30\t| Classes | `PascalCase` | `TaskClaim` |\n    31\t| Constants | `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` | `MAX_CLAIM_LEASE_S` |\n    32\t| Booleans | prefix with `is`, `has`, `should`, `can` | `is_idempotent` |\n    33\t\n    34\tRelated config fields that serve parallel roles use consistent\n    35\tgrammatical form — e.g., all imperative verbs: `plan_command`,\n    36\t`implement_command`, `evaluate_command` — not a mix of verb/noun.\n    37\t\n    38\t### Type annotations\n    39\t\n    40\t- Explicit types on all public API signatures and dataclass fields.\n    41\t- Use `from __future__ import annotations` when needed for forward refs.\n    42\t- Prefer built-in generics (`list[str]`, `dict[str, int]`) over `typing`\n    43\t  imports.\n    44\t\n    45\t### Docstrings\n    46\t\n    47\tGoogle style:\n    48\t\n    49\t```python\n    50\tdef claim_task(store: TaskStore, worker_id: str) -> Task | None:\n    51\t    \"\"\"Atomically claim the next ready task for a worker.\n    52\t\n    53\t    Args:\n    54\t        store: The task store.\n    55\t        worker_id: Identity of the claiming worker.\n    56\t\n    57\t    Returns:\n    58\t        The claimed task, or None if no tasks are ready.\n    59\t    \"\"\"\n    60\t```\n    61\t\n    62\tModule-, magic-method-, and `__init__` docstrings are not required.\n    63\t\n    64\t### Patterns\n    65\t\n    66\t**Frozen dataclasses for value types**:\n    67\t\n    68\t```python\n    69\tfrom dataclasses import dataclass\n    70\t\n    71\t@dataclass(frozen=True)\n    72\tclass TaskClaim:\n    73\t    task_id: str\n    74\t    worker_id: str\n    75\t    claim_token: str\n    76\t```\n    77\t\n    78\t**Explicit error handling at boundaries** (subprocess, network, file\n    79\tI/O):\n    80\t\n    81\t```python\n    82\ttry:\n    83\t    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, check=True)\n    84\texcept subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:\n    85\t    raise ExecutionError(f\"Command failed: {e.stderr}\") from e\n    86\t```\n    87\t\n    88\t**Avoid mutable default arguments**:\n    89\t\n    90\t```python\n    91\t# Avoid\n    92\tdef process(items: list[str] = []) -> None: ...\n    93\t\n    94\t# Prefer\n    95\tdef process(items: list[str] | None = None) -> None:\n    96\t    items = items or []\n    97\t```\n    98\t\n    99\t## Ruff configuration\n   100\t\n   101\tLives in `pyproject.toml` once the first Python package lands in Phase 3.\n   102\tBaseline rules to carry over:\n   103\t\n   104\t- line-length 120, target Python 3.12\n   105\t- enabled rule sets: `A`, `B`, `D`, `E`, `F`, `I`, `PT`, `SIM`, `UP`\n   106\t- ignored: `D100`, `D105`, `D107`, `SIM108`\n   107\t- docstring rules disabled for test files\n   108\t\n   109\t## Pyright configuration\n   110\t\n   111\t- `typeCheckingMode = \"standard\"`\n   112\t- `reportUnnecessaryTypeIgnoreComment = \"warning\"`\n   113\t- `reportMissingTypeStubs = \"none\"`\n   114\t\n   115\t## Spec prose (`spec/`)\n   116\t\n   117\t- Use RFC 2119 normative keywords (MUST, SHOULD, MAY) where behavior is\n   118\t  prescriptive. Informative prose uses ordinary English and says so.\n   119\t- Numbered sections per chapter.\n   120\t- Every wire format has a corresponding JSON Schema under\n   121\t  `spec/v*/schemas/`. The chapter cites the schema file by path.\n   122\t- No references to specific technologies (Postgres, Redis, FastAPI).\n   123\t  The spec describes *semantics*; mechanisms are reference-impl detail.\n   124\t\n   125\t## Markdown (all `.md` in the repo)\n   126\t\n   127\t- Atx-style headings (`#`, `##`, `###`).\n   128\t- Reference-style links are fine when a URL repeats; inline otherwise.\n   129\t- Code fences specify a language tag when applicable.\n   130\t- `markdownlint-cli2` is the authoritative linter;\n   131\t  [`.markdownlint.json`](.markdownlint.json) holds the exception list.\n   132\t\n   133\tFor commands to run linters, see [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#commands).\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

describe\n    68\t- Update comments when code changes\n    69\t\n    70\t## Patterns\n    71\t\n    72\t### Preferred: Frozen Dataclasses\n    73\t\n    74\t```python\n    75\tfrom dataclasses import dataclass\n    76\t\n    77\t@dataclass(frozen=True)\n    78\tclass TrialResult:\n    79\t    trial_id: int\n    80\t    branch: str\n    81\t    metrics: dict[str, float]\n    82\t```\n    83\t\n    84\t### Preferred: Explicit Error Handling at Boundaries\n    85\t\n    86\t```python\n    87\t# At system boundaries (subprocess calls, file I/O), handle errors explicitly\n    88\ttry:\n    89\t    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, check=True)\n    90\texcept subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:\n    91\t    raise ExecutionError(f\"Command failed: {e.stderr}\") from e\n    92\t```\n    93\t\n    94\t### Avoid: Mutable Default Arguments\n    95\t\n    96\t```python\n    97\t# Avoid this:\n    98\tdef process(items: list[str] = []) -> None: ...\n    99\t\n   100\t# Prefer this:\n   101\tdef process(items: list[str] | None = None) -> None:\n   102\t    items = items or []\n   103\t```\n   104\t\n   105\t## Linting Configuration\n   106\t\n   107\tConfiguration is in unnecessary type-ignore comments\n   114\t\n   115\t### Key Rules\n   116\t\n   117\t- **D (pydocstyle)**: Google-style docstrings required on public functions (disabled in tests)\n   118\t- **I A **planner** proposes experiments via a shared SQLite database, and the **orchestrator** dispatches them as parallel git worktrees, each executed by an isolated Linux user. A **Web UI** provides browser-based observability into experiments, both during live runs and for post-hoc exploration of completed experiments.\n    29\t\n    30\t### Experiment Root, Planner Root, And Workspace Root\n    31\t\n    32\tThe **experiment root** is the top-level directory (inferred from the config path\n    33\tas the parent of `.eden/`). It contains shared session state and acts as the\n    34\touter trust boundary.\n    35\t\n    36\tThe **planner root** is configured by `planner_root`, must live under the\n    37\texperiment root, and contains planner-owned state such as `proposals.db`,\n    38\tproposal docs, and the planner subprocess working directory.\n    39\t\n    40\tThe **workspace root** is a git repo configured by `workspace`, resolved\n    41\trelative to `planner_root`, and must live under the planner root. Trials\n    42\toperate on worktrees of the workspace repo.\n    43\t\n    44\tThis separation prevents reward hacking: the implementer can only modify files\n    45\tin its worktree, planner assets live under `planner_root`, and evaluation runs\n    46\tafter the trial commit so eval-side writes can be reset without contaminating\n    47\tthe committed result.\n    48\t\n    49\tPaths resolve by scope:\n    50\t- experiment-scoped paths resolve from `experiment_root`\n    51\t- planner-scoped paths resolve from `planner_root`\n    52\t- command file tokens are resolved against the command's scope root at config load time\n    53\t\n    54\t### Data Flow\n    55\t\n    56\t1. Planner process writes proposals to `proposals.db` (status: drafting → ready)\n    57\t2. Orchestrator's async dispatch loop atomically claims a ready proposal (`BEGIN IMMEDIATE`)\n    58\t3. For each claimed proposal, the orchestrator: reserves a trial ID in `results.db` → creates a git worktree → copies proposal docs → creates transient implementer grants → runs implement command → removes grants → commits results → runs evaluate command as root → resets eval-side writes → records to `results.db`\n    59\t4. Planner is notified of completed trials via stdin of its long-running subprocess\n    60\t\n    61\t### Two-Database Design\n    62\t\n    63\t- **results.db**: Orchestrator writes, planner reads. Uses SQLite `DELETE` journal mode and stores trial outcomes with user-defined metric columns from `metrics_schema`.\n    64\t- **proposals.db**: Planner writes, orchestrator reads/updates. Uses SQLite `WAL` journal mode and acts as the proposal queue. Proposals carry parent commits and a slug used for branch naming.\n    65\t\n    66\t### Web UI\n    67\t\n    68\tThe Web UI (`eden ui`) provides browser-based experiment observability. It is a thin Python file server (Starlette) that serves experiment data files over HTTP, paired with a React SPA that queries SQLite databases directly in the browser using sql.js (SQLite compiled to WebAssembly).\n    69\t\n    70\t**Architecture**: The backend serves raw files — `results.db` is served as-is (DELETE journal mode), `proposals.db` is served as a WAL-checkpointed snapshot via `sqlite3.backup()`, and artifacts/logs are served via standard static file serving. The frontend downloads the database files and runs SQL queries in-browser, enabling a SQL Console for arbitrary exploration. Live updates use HEAD-based polling (every 3 seconds) to detect file changes.\n    71\t\n    72\t**Two modes**:\n    73\t- **Live** (`eden ui --config .eden/config.yaml`): reads from the experiment root alongside a running orchestrator\n    74\t- **Post-run** (`eden ui --experiment-dir /path/to/exported/`): reads from an exported experiment directory\n    75\t\n    76\t**Key files**: `src/eden/web/server.py` (Starlette app), `packages/web-ui/` (React frontend)\n    77\t\n    78\t### Subprocess Isolation Model\n    79\t\n    80\tThe system runs inside Docker with multiple Linux users created at container startup by `runtime.py`:\n    81\t- `planner` user: runs the planner subprocess, has read access to results.db and read/write to proposals.db\n    82\t- `trial-{slot}` users (one per `parallel_trials`): each owns their worktree, isolated from other slots\n    83\t- Planner subprocesses run as `planner` with `planner_root` as the working directory\n    84\t- Implement commands run as `trial-{slot}` from the slot worktree\n    85\t- Evaluation runs from the committed worktree as root, then the worktree is reset to `HEAD`\n    86\t\n    87\t### Directory Structure\n    88\t\n    89\t```\n    90\teden/\n    91\t├── src/eden/   # Core package\n    92\t│   ├── sql/            # SQL schema templates\n    93\t│   ├── docker/         # Shipped Docker scripts (entrypoint, auth, export)\n    94\t│   ├── web/            # Web UI server (Starlette file server)\n    95\t│   ├── cli.py          # CLI entry point (run, doctor, docker build/run, ui)\n    96\t│   ├── bootstrap.py    # Session bootstrap: config load, DB init, runtime setup\n    97\t│   ├── orchestrator.py # Async dispatch loop\n    98\t│   ├── docker_runner.py # Dockerfile generation, image build, container run\n    99\t│   ├── config.py       # YAML loading, scoped path resolution, validation\n   100\t│   ├── db.py           # SQLite manager for both databases\n   101\t│   ├── git_manager.py  # Worktree lifecycle, branch ops\n   102\t│   ├── execution.py    # Implement/evaluate subprocess execution with user switching\n   103\t│   ├── grants.py       # Transient cross-scope grant symlink helpers\n   104\t│   ├── planner.py      # Persistent planner subprocess (CWD=planner_root)\n   105\t│   ├── termination.py  # Stop condition evaluation\n   106\t│   ├── runtime.py      # Container user/permission bootstrap\n   107\t│   ├── worktree.py     # Trial directory setup\n   108\t│   ├── models.py       # Frozen dataclasses for config/results\n   109\t│   └── logging.py      # Logging configuration\n   110\t├── tests/              # pytest suite, mirrors src modules\n   111\t│   └── fixtures/experiment/  # E2E test: seed-sum experiment\n   112\t├── packages/web-ui/    # React/Vite/TypeScript frontend SPA\n   113\t├── docker/             # Backward-compat entrypoint wrapper\n   114\t├── scripts/            # Docker integration/validation scripts\n   115\t├── docs/plans/         # Implementation plans\n   116\t└── docs/prds/          # Product requirement documents\n   117\t```\n   118\t\n   119\tAn experiment directory (e.g., `tests/fixtures/experiment/`) has this layout:\n   120\t\n   121\t```\n   122\texperiment/              # experiment_root\n   123\t├── .eden/\n   124\t│   └── config.yaml\n   125\t├── eval.py              # evaluation script (experiment-scoped)\n   126\t├── implement.py         # implementer entry script (experiment-scoped)\n   127\t├── plan.py              # persistent planner subprocess (experiment-scoped)\n   128\t└── planner/             # planner_root\n   129\t    └── workspace/       # workspace_root (git repo)\n   130\t        └── seeds.md     # trial data\n   131\t```\n   132\t\n   133\t## Key Patterns\n   134\t\n   135\t- **Frozen dataclasses** (`models.py`): All config and result types are immutable.\n   136\t- **Atomic claiming**: Proposals are claimed via `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` transactions to prevent double-dispatch.\n   137\t- **Deterministic recovery**: Failed worktrees are cleaned with hard reset + clean untracked + porcelain status verification.\n   138\t- **Entrypoint sequence** (`docker/entrypoint.sh`): Runs `runtime.py` for user/permission setup, then `cli.py run`.\n   139\t\n   140\t## Gotchas\n   141\t\n   142\t- Proposals use `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` for atomic claiming — regular `BEGIN` would allow double-dispatch under concurrent access.\n   143\t- Git worktree cleanup requires both hard reset and clean; either alone leaves stale state.\n   144\t- The planner subprocess is long-lived and receives trial notifications via stdin, not polling.\n   145\t- Docker tests require privileged mode for user creation; use the scripts in `scripts/` rather than running Docker commands by hand.\n   146\t- The planner subprocess introduces timing non-determinism: a fast implementer can exhaust the proposal queue before the planner reacts to completion notifications. The orchestrator handles this via idle-polling, but tests must not assume deterministic proposal ordering when a subprocess planner is involved.\n   147\t- The Web UI cannot serve `proposals.db` as a raw file because it uses WAL journal mode — recent writes may live in the `-wal` sidecar, and sql.js (browser SQLite) has no WAL support. The backend serves a checkpointed snapshot via `sqlite3.backup()` instead. `results.db` uses DELETE mode and is served as-is.\n   148\t- The Web UI frontend requires building before `eden ui` can serve it: run `cd packages/web-ui && npm run build` first. Use `--dev` to proxy to the Vite dev server during frontend development.\n   149\t\n   150\t## Coding Style\n   151\t\n   152\t- **Python 3.12+**, compatible with the `pyproject.toml` toolchain\n   153\t- 4-space indentation, 120-character line limit\n   154\t- Google-style docstrings where docstrings are needed\n   155\t- `snake_case` for functions/modules, `PascalCase` for classes, `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` for constants\n   156\t- Explicit types on public APIs and dataclass fields\n   157\t- Keep modules small and responsibility-focused\n   158\t\n   159\tFor detailed formatting rules, see [STYLE_GUIDE.md](STYLE_GUIDE.md).\n   160\t\n   161\t## Testing Guidelines\n   162\t\n   163\t- Name files `tests/test_<area>.py` and functions `test_<behavior>()`\n   164\t- Add or update tests for any behavioral change, especially around git operations, SQLite coordination, orchestration flows, and Docker/runtime permissions\n   165\t- Run the repo-local suite first, then Docker scripts in `scripts/` for environment-specific behavior\n   166\t- Demos and examples must be exercised by the test suite — standalone scripts that aren't tested rot silently\n   167\t- When test inputs produce deterministic outputs, assert exact values rather than loose properties (e.g., verify scores against actual committed data, not just \"scores increase\")\n   168\t- When testing systems with async subprocesses, don't assume deterministic ordering — design assertions around structural invariants that hold regardless of timing\n   169\t\n   170\t## Commit Guidelines\n   171\t\n   172\t- Short imperative commit subjects (e.g., \"Add proposal claiming\", \"Fix worktree cleanup\")\n   173\t- For pull requests, include: problem/solution summary, config/Docker/permission implications, commands run and test results, linked issue or plan doc when relevant\n   174\t\n   175\t## Configuration\n   176\t\n   177\tWorkspace config lives at `.eden/config.yaml`. docs/plans/review/eden-protocol-bootstrap/impl/phase-4/20260423T123425/0.jsonl:5:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_1","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' AGENTS.md\"","aggregated_output":"# AGENTS.md\n\nThis file provides guidance to AI agents working with this repository.\n\n## What this repo is\n\nEDEN is a **protocol** for directed-code-evolution orchestration, not a\nsingle product. The repo contains three distinct kinds of artifact, each\nwith different change discipline:\n\n| Layer | Path | Authoritative? | Change discipline |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| Protocol specification | [`spec/`](spec/) | Yes — the source of truth | RFC-style: versioned, normative (MUST/SHOULD/MAY), carefully reviewed |\n| Reference implementation | [`reference/`](reference/) | No — *one* valid impl | Normal code-review discipline |\n| Conformance suite | [`conformance/`](conformance/) | Normative for tests | Black-box; must be implementation-agnostic |\n\nNon-normative human docs live in [`docs/`](docs/).\n\n## Current phase\n\n**Phase 4 complete.** The v0 spec now ships the event protocol\n(`05-event-protocol.md`), integrator contract (`06-integrator.md`),\nand storage contract (`08-storage.md`). `event.schema.json` now\npins per-type payload shapes for the 15 registered event types via\n`if/then` dispatch on `type`, and the `eden-contracts` package has a\nmatching discriminated-union `RegisteredEvent` model plus corpus\ncoverage. All six CI checks on `main` remain green. Phase 5 is next:\nthe in-memory reference dispatch loop. See\n[`docs/roadmap.md`](docs/roadmap.md) for the full 13-phase plan.\n\n## Commands\n\nAt Phase 4, markdown linting, JSON Schema validation, and the Python\ntoolchain for the `eden-contracts` reference package are wired up.\n\n| Command | Purpose |\n|---|---|\n| `npx --yes markdownlint-cli2@0.14.0 \"**/*.md\" \"#node_modules\" \"#.venv\" \"#docs/archive/**\" \"#docs/plans/review/**\"` | Lint all tracked markdown (pinned to CI's version; matches CI exactly) |\n| `pipx run 'check-jsonschema==0.29.4' --check-metaschema spec/v0/schemas/*.schema.json` | Validate each schema file against the Draft 2020-12 meta-schema (version pinned to CI) |\n| `pipx run 'check-jsonschema==0.29.4' --schemafile spec/v0/schemas/experiment-config.schema.json tests/fixtures/experiment/.eden/config.yaml` | Validate the fixture experiment config against its schema |\n| `uv sync` | Install/refresh the workspace virtualenv (root + `reference/packages/eden-contracts`) |\n| `uv run ruff check .` | Lint Python (config in root `pyproject.toml`) |\n| `uv run pyright` | Type-check the reference Python packages |\n| `uv run pytest -q` | Run the reference-package test suite (includes schema ↔ model parity) |\n| `uv run pytest reference/packages/eden-contracts/tests/test_schema_parity.py` | Run only the schema ↔ Pydantic model parity check |\n\n### Commands that will exist in later phases\n\nThese are listed for orientation; the tooling is not wired up yet.\n\n| Command | Lands in |\n|---|---|\n| `docker compose up` end-to-end | Phase 10 |\n\n## Contribution conventions\n\n### Spec edits\n\n+ Every cross-component contract (wire format, state machine, invariant)\n  is defined in `spec/` first, then implemented in `reference/`. When\n  they disagree, the spec wins and the impl gets a bug.\n+ Spec chapters use RFC 2119 language: MUST, SHOULD, MAY.\n+ Spec versions are frozen once stable (`spec/v0/` is a single lineage;\n  breaking changes go to `spec/v1/`).\n+ Schema changes must update the spec prose, the JSON Schema file, and\n  (from Phase 3 onward) the Pydantic bindings in lockstep. CI will\n  enforce schema ↔ model parity once both exist.\n\n### Reference implementation\n\n+ Normal code-review discipline.\n+ Normative behavior is tested against the conformance suite (once it\n  exists in Phase 11).\n+ The reference impl is explicitly not a monopoly on correctness. A\n  third-party implementation that passes conformance is equally valid.\n\n### Adding or extending a JSON Schema + Pydantic binding\n\nThe `schema-parity` CI job is only as strong as what both sides of the\ntest actually enforce. Several Pydantic and `jsonschema` defaults let\ndrift through silently. When adding a new schema — or a new field type\nto an existing one — evaluate each of the following; reusable\nimplementations live in\n[`reference/packages/eden-contracts/src/eden_contracts/_common.py`](reference/packages/eden-contracts/src/eden_contracts/_common.py).\n\n+ **Strict numeric parsing.** Top-level models set\n  `ConfigDict(strict=True, extra=\"allow\")`. Non-strict mode coerces\n  `True`/`\"2\"` into int, but the schemas treat `type: integer` /\n  `type: number` as strict JSON types. New models must keep\n  `strict=True`.\n+ **Format assertions on both sides.** `format` keywords (`uri`,\n  `date-time`, …) are advisory by default in both the `jsonschema`\n  library and Pydantic. The schema-side validator wires a custom\n  `FormatChecker` in\n  [`tests/conftest.py`](reference/packages/eden-contracts/tests/conftest.py),\n  and the model side uses the reusable types in `_common.py`. A new\n  `format` keyword in any schema requires handlers on *both* sides;\n  the `test_format_coverage` test fails loudly if the schema-side\n  handler is missing.\n+ **Real date-time / URI validation, not just regex or `urlparse`.**\n  The regex on `DateTimeStr` accepts impossible values like\n  `2026-99-99T…Z`; an `AfterValidator` runs `datetime.fromisoformat`\n  to reject those. `UriStr` uses `rfc3986-validator`, not\n  `urllib.parse.urlparse`, which accepts malformed schemeful URIs\n  (e.g., spaces in the host).\n+ **Null vs absent.** JSON Schema's `type: X` rejects explicit `null`,\n  but Pydantic's `X | None = None` accepts it. Wrap every optional\n  typed field with `NotNone` from `_common.py` so absent is accepted\n  and explicit null is rejected.\n+ **Round-trip emission.** `model.model_dump(mode=\"json\",\n  exclude_none=True)` must re-validate against the schema. Add any\n  new model to `tests/test_roundtrip.py` so this is checked.\n+ **Corpus coverage.** Parity is asserted over the fixture corpus in\n  `tests/cases.py`. A new field type deserves at least one accept\n  fixture and one reject fixture per constraint the schema imposes\n  (required, pattern, enum, min/max, format, cross-field).\n\n## Commit guidelines\n\n+ Short imperative subjects (e.g., \"Add event protocol chapter\",\n  \"Pin task state machine\").\n+ For pull requests, include: what the change is, which phase it\n  advances, and any spec ↔ impl implications.\n\n## Related docs\n\n+ [`docs/naming.md`](docs/naming.md) — what EDEN is, at the concept level.\n+ [`docs/roadmap.md`](docs/roadmap.md) — the 13-phase build-up plan with\n  unit-level decomposition.\n+ [`docs/plans/`](docs/plans/) — active implementation plans.\n+ [`docs/archive/`](docs/archive/) — historical reference docs.\n+ [`STYLE_GUIDE.md`](STYLE_GUIDE.md) — formatting and naming conventions.\n+ [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](CONTRIBUTING.md) — setup and PR workflow.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

Heavy cross-referencing: the integrator emits\nevents; storage persists them.\n\n**Units:**\n\n- **4a** — `05-event-protocol.md` + `event.schema.json` refinement.\n  Transactional invariant (state change + event insert must commit\n  atomically); delivery guarantees subscribers can rely on.\n- **4b** — `06-integrator.md`. Git topology invariants: `work/*`,\n  `trial/*`, `main` namespaces; sole-integrator rule; squash rule;\n  eval-manifest shape (metrics + blob URIs + hashes).\n- **4c** — `08-storage.md`. Repository interface as protocol-level\n  contract; durability requirements; per-experiment metrics schemas.\n\n**Chunk:** 4a + 4b + 4c — one chunk. Cross-references are dense and\nsplitting risks inconsistency.\n\n**Non-goals:** control-plane (Phase 12); conformance chapter (Phase 11);\nany code.\n\n**Exit:** three chapters land with consistent cross-references; schema\nupdates (if any) pass parity.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 5 — In-memory reference dispatch loop\n\nFirst executable implementation. Proves the spec's state machines are\nimplementable. Single process; no git yet; no persistence.\n\n**Units:**\n\n- **5a** — In-memory task queue + event log with the transactional\n  invariant enforced.\n- **5b** — Scripted planner/implementer/evaluator workers (fake\n  outputs, real state transitions) driving the queue through a full\n  trial lifecycle.\n- **5c** — First conformance scenarios (state-machine coverage)\n  implemented as pytest-based black-box tests against 5a+5b.\n\n**Chunks:** 5a one chunk; 5b + 5c one chunk (scenarios need a worker\nharness to drive).\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
docs/plans/review/eden-protocol-bootstrap/impl/phase-4/20260423T123425/0.jsonl:18:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_8","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"nl -ba spec/v0/05-event-protocol.md | sed -n '1,260p'\"","aggregated_output":"     1\t# Event Protocol\n     2\t\n     3\tThis chapter specifies the event log: the envelope every event MUST\n     4\tcarry, the transactional invariant that binds event writes to the\n     5\tstate changes they describe, the per-type payload shapes for the v0\n     6\tevent registry, and the delivery guarantees a conforming event log\n     7\tMUST offer to subscribers.\n     8\t\n     9\tThe event envelope's JSON Schema is\n    10\t[`schemas/event.schema.json`](schemas/event.schema.json). The\n    11\tbehavioral contracts that produce events are in\n    12\t[`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) (task transitions) and\n    13\t[`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md) (trial promotion). The event\n    14\tlog's durability and subscription semantics as a *store* are in\n    15\t[`08-storage.md`](08-storage.md); this chapter specifies what events\n    16\tmean, not how they are persisted.\n    17\t\n    18\t## 1. Envelope\n    19\t\n    20\tEvery event appended to a conforming event log MUST be a JSON object\n    21\tcarrying:\n    22\t\n    23\t| Field | Required | Type | Meaning |\n    24\t|---|---|---|---|\n    25\t| `event_id` | yes | string | Unique identifier for this event within the log. Opaque. |\n    26\t| `type` | yes | string | Dotted type name (`^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*(\\.[a-z][a-z0-9_]*)+$`). The normative registry is in §3. |\n    27\t| `occurred_at` | yes | timestamp | When the state change happened. UTC, trailing `Z`, RFC 3339 profile (`^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}(\\.[0-9]+)?Z$`). |\n    28\t| `experiment_id` | yes | string | The experiment the event belongs to. Events are scoped per experiment ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §4.3). |\n    29\t| `data` | yes | object | Type-specific payload. Shape is fixed by the registry entry for `type` (§3). |\n    30\t\n    31\tThe envelope is closed over these fields for conforming events; a\n    32\tconforming log MAY persist additional audit metadata alongside each\n    33\tevent, but MUST NOT expose it under any of the five envelope keys.\n    34\tImplementations MAY include additional top-level keys outside the\n    35\tenvelope (e.g. `sequence`, `recorded_at`); subscribers relying only on\n    36\tthe envelope MUST remain correct regardless.\n    37\t\n    38\t### 1.1 `event_id` uniqueness\n    39\t\n    40\t`event_id` MUST be unique within the log. A subscriber MAY dedupe by\n    41\t`event_id` when recovering from a disconnect.\n    42\t\n    43\t### 1.2 `occurred_at` vs. log order\n    44\t\n    45\t`occurred_at` records when the state change happened, which MAY\n    46\tdiffer from the event's position in the log — clock skew, batched\n    47\twrites, or retried appends can all produce non-monotonic\n    48\t`occurred_at` within an experiment's stream. Causal order is\n    49\testablished by the log itself (§4.3), not by timestamps.\n    50\t`occurred_at` is advisory for human debugging; subscribers MUST NOT\n    51\trely on it for ordering.\n    52\t\n    53\t## 2. Transactional invariant\n    54\t\n    55\tThe central invariant of the event protocol, stated in\n    56\t[`01-concepts.md`](01-concepts.md) §6:\n    57\t\n    58\t> **Every state change that is observable via tasks, proposals, or\n    59\t> trials MUST be accompanied by a corresponding event, and the event\n    60\t> write MUST be atomic with the state change it describes.**\n    61\t\n    62\t\"Atomic\" means: either both the state change and the event append are\n    63\tdurable, or neither is. A conforming implementation MUST NOT expose a\n    64\tstate change to readers (direct store reads, cached projections, push\n    65\tnotifications, role dispatch) before its event is durable in the log;\n    66\tconversely, it MUST NOT append an event whose state change has not\n    67\tbeen durably written. If a transaction covering both is aborted,\n    68\tneither is observable.\n    69\t\n    70\tThe invariant applies to the set of entities enumerated in\n    71\t[`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md): tasks, proposals, trials.\n    72\tImplementations MAY emit *additional* events outside this set (see\n    73\t§3.5) that do not correspond to a protocol-defined state change; those\n    74\tadditional events are not subject to the transactional invariant\n    75\tbecause there is no state change to bind them to.\n    76\t\n    77\t### 2.1 Why atomic\n    78\t\n    79\tThe event log is the normative observability channel\n    80\t([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §6.3). Without\n    81\tatomicity, subscribers reconstructing history can observe either a\n    82\tstate without its event or an event whose state the store does not\n    83\tyet expose — either is a protocol violation that leaves subscribers\n    84\tunable to trust the log.\n    85\t\n    86\t### 2.2 Composite transitions\n    87\t\n    88\tSeveral transitions span multiple entities and MUST commit together:\n    89\t\n    90\t- **Implement dispatch** — creating a `task` with `kind=implement` and\n    91\t  transitioning its referenced `proposal` from `ready` to\n    92\t  `dispatched` ([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §2).\n    93\t  Events: `task.created` + `proposal.dispatched`, in one atomic\n    94\t  commit.\n    95\t- **Implement terminal** — the `implement` task's terminal transition\n    96\t  (`submitted → completed` or `submitted → failed`) and the matching\n    97\t  `proposal` transition from `dispatched` to `completed`\n    98\t  ([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.3, §7). Events:\n    99\t  `task.completed` (or `task.failed`) + `proposal.completed`, in one\n   100\t  atomic commit.\n   101\t- **Evaluate terminal (`success`/`error`)** — the `evaluate` task's\n   102\t  terminal transition plus writes to the trial's `status`, `metrics`,\n   103\t  `artifacts_uri`, and `completed_at` ([`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md)\n   104\t  §4.4). Events: `task.completed` (or `task.failed`) +\n   105\t  `trial.succeeded` / `trial.errored`, in one atomic commit.\n   106\t- **Implement reclaim with in-flight trial** — reclamation of an\n   107\t  `implement` task whose prior execution left a trial in `starting`\n   108\t  requires transitioning that trial to `error` atomically with the\n   109\t  reclaim ([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §5.4). Events:\n   110\t  `task.reclaimed` + `trial.errored`, in one atomic commit.\n   111\t- **Retry-exhausted `eval_error` terminal** — the orchestrator's\n   112\t  transition of a trial from `starting` to `eval_error`\n   113\t  ([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.3).
